_README: Committed S6 studies-slice adjudication (X0d; plan §1.3, D7). Produced by the x0d-s6-adjudicate workflow (run wf_b19a4fbe: 568 canonical-name clusters over the 746 studies-role records in graph-conjectures/data/arxiv_extracted; disposition per cluster with precomputed alias candidates + a blind verify pass that conservatively downgraded 'promoted' flips). 2 invented-alias clusters (Chen-Chvatal main, Odd Hadwiger) reclassified to promoted post-hoc. Merged into the manifest by build_v2_manifest.py as corpus 'arxiv-studied': alias rows point at their target and own nothing; promoted rows enter the statement waves; edge-anchor/parked are tracked with disposition + reason. Statements are the richest extractor statement per cluster.

rows.canonical_name: Esperet–Raymond conjecture (polynomial expansion and strong colouring numbers)
rows.statement: Is it true that for every graph class $\mathcal{C}$ with polynomial expansion, there exists a polynomial function $f$ such that for every integer $r\geqslant 0$ and every graph $G\in\mathcal{C}$, $$\mathrm{scol}_{r}(G)\leqslant f(r)?$$
rows.reason: Named third-party conjecture studied by 5 papers; the flagged Question rows (independent-set / triangle-testing) are unrelated, and the polynomial-expansion / scol_r bound is inherently asymptotic.

rows.statement: For all $k, r \in \mathbb{N}$ there is an $n \in \mathbb{N}$ such that for every graph $G$ with $\chi(G) \geq n$ and $\omega(G) \leq k$, there is an induced subgraph $H$ of $G$ with $\chi(H) \geq r$ and $\omega(H) = 2$.
rows.reason: Named third-party conjecture (5 papers), self-contained via chromatic/clique numbers and induced subgraphs; the Reed list-chromatic candidate rows are different statements.

rows.statement: If $E_1, E_2, \ldots, E_m$ are sets of edges in a bipartite graph, and $|E_i| > \Delta\!\left(\bigcup_{i \leq m} E_i\right) + 1$ then there exists a rainbow matching.
rows.reason: Paper-local Conjecture 1.12 with unknown attribution and a single studying paper; candidates are sibling conjectures of the same paper or the unrelated strong-edge-colouring conjecture.

rows.statement: For every $k \in \mathbb{N}$, there exists $c = c(k) \in \mathbb{N}$ satisfying the following. If $d \in \mathbb{N}$, $G$ is a graph and $X, Y \subseteq V(G)$, then there exists either (1) $k$ disjoint $X$-$Y$-paths $P_1, \ldots, P_k$ such that $\mathrm{dist}_G(P_i, P_j) \geq d$ for all distinct $i, j$, or (2) a set $Z \subseteq V(G)$ of size less than $k$ such that $B_G(Z, cd)$ separates $X$ and $Y$.
rows.attributed_to: Georgakopoulos and Papasoglu; Albrechtsen et al.
rows.reason: Named third-party conjecture studied by 5 papers with a self-contained Menger-type statement over path distances and separators; no candidates.

rows.statement: Let $\mathcal{F}$ be a finite class of graphs. Then the class of all $\mathcal{F}$-free graphs has bounded $\operatorname{{\text{\sf{tree}}-\alpha}}$ if (and only if) there exist $F_{1},F_{2},F_{3}\in\mathcal{F}$ where $F_{1}$ is complete bipartite, $F_{2}$ is a subdivided multiclaw, and $F_{3}$ is the line graph of a subdivided multiclaw.
rows.attributed_to: Dallard, Krnc, Kwon, Milanič, Munaro, Štorgel, and Wiederrecht
rows.reason: Named structural characterization conjecture (3 papers); requires tree-alpha, subdivided multiclaw and line-graph primitives; no candidates.

rows.canonical_name: Alon–Fischer–Newman conjecture (efficient testing of binary-matrix properties)
rows.statement: Is it true that for any fixed finite family $F$ of binary matrices and any $\varepsilon > 0$, there exists $\delta > 0$ with $\delta^{-1}$ polynomial in $\varepsilon^{-1}$, such that any $n \times n$ binary matrix $M$ that is $\varepsilon$-far from $F$-freeness contains $\delta n^{s+t}$ copies of some $s \times t$ matrix $A \in F$?
rows.reason: Named conjecture (2 papers); flagged derived digraph rows are spurious; the removal/property-testing statement has polynomial epsilon/delta dependence, so asymptotic. [verify downgraded: A real named conjecture but about ordered binary-matrix property testing, outside the graph-theory formalization scope; candidates are unrelated derived digraph rows.]

rows.canonical_name: Hat guessing number vs degeneracy and degree conjecture
rows.statement:
Do there exist functions $f_1, f_2 : \mathbb{N} \to \mathbb{N}$ such that the following hold?
1. If a graph $G$ is $d$-degenerate, then $\mathrm{HG}(G) \leq f_1(d)$.
2. If a graph $G$ has minimum degree at least $f_2(d)$, then $\mathrm{HG}(G) \geq d$.
rows.reason: Verified same proposition as Problem 1.4 of 1812.09752 (existence of functions bounding the hat guessing number by degeneracy and forcing growth via minimum degree).

rows.canonical_name: Trotignon conjecture (bounded treewidth via forbidden induced minors and outerstring subgraphs)
rows.statement: For all $r,t\in\mathbb{N}$, there exists $c=c(r,t)\in\mathbb{N}$ such that for every graph $G$, if $G$ does not contain $W_{t\times t}$ or $K_{t,t}$ as an induced minor, and every induced subgraph $H$ of $G$ that is an outerstring graph satisfies $\operatorname{tw}(H)\leq r$, then $\operatorname{tw}(G)\leq c$.
rows.reason: Named third-party conjecture (5 papers); candidates unrelated; requires induced-minor, wall, outerstring-graph and treewidth primitives.

rows.statement: $\vec{\chi}(\mathrm{Forbind}(\overleftrightarrow{K_2}, S_2^+, \vec{C}_3)) = \vec{\chi}(\mathrm{Forbind}(\overleftrightarrow{K_2}, S_2^-, \vec{C}_3)) = 2.$
rows.reason: Specific auxiliary value claim (dichromatic number = 2 for two named forbidden-subdigraph classes), paper-local Conjecture 1.7, single paper; value is as an edge endpoint.

rows.statement: For all $k, \Delta > 0$, there exists $c = c(k, \Delta)$ such that every graph with maximum degree at most $\Delta$ and treewidth more than $c$ contains a subdivision of $W_{k \times k}$ or the line graph of a subdivision of $W_{k \times k}$ as an induced subgraph.
rows.reason: Essentially the same proposition as Conjecture 3 of 2008.05504 (bounded degree plus large treewidth forces an induced wall or line graph of a wall).

rows.statement: Is $z(G \square H) \leq z(G) + z(H)$ for all graphs $G$ and $H$?
rows.reason: Named open question (Question 10) with a clean self-contained subadditivity inequality z(G□H) ≤ z(G)+z(H); needs the parameter primitive; no candidates.

rows.canonical_name: Caro–Patkós–Tuza berge/biclique extremal problem for paths
rows.statement: Determine at least asymptotically $ex_{b,c}(n,n,P_{k})$ for any fixed $k$.
rows.reason: An open-ended 'determine ex_{b,c}(n,n,P_k) asymptotically' determination task, not a formalizable proposition; the distinct-distances candidate is unrelated.

rows.statement: Over all graphs $G$, how large can the ratio $\frac{z(G)}{c(G)}$ be?
rows.reason: 'How large can the ratio z(G)/c(G) be' is a vague optimization/determination question with no crisp proposition to formalize.

rows.canonical_name: Expected number of faces of random embeddings conjecture (Campion Loth–Halász–Mašařík–Mohar–Šámal)
rows.statement: For every $n$-vertex multigraph $G$ with maximum edge-multiplicity $\mu \geq 2$, the expected number of faces when selecting an orientable embedding of $G$ uniformly at random is $O(n\log(\mu))$.
rows.attributed_to: Halasz, Masarik, Samal, Campion Loth, and Mohar
rows.reason: Named conjecture (2 papers); a probabilistic statement with an O(n log μ) asymptotic bound over uniformly random orientable embeddings; no candidates.

rows.canonical_name: Chudnovsky–Penev–Scott–Trotignon conjecture (χ-boundedness under substitution and gluing)
rows.statement: Is the closure of a $\chi$-bounded class under substitution and gluing along a bounded number of vertices also $\chi$-bounded?
rows.reason: Named problem; candidate 2310.04265#11 is the digraph (dichromatic, substitution-only) analogue — a different proposition; needs χ-bounded, substitution and bounded-gluing primitives.

rows.statement: There is a function $f$ such that, for any fixed tree $T$ with maximum degree $\Delta_T$, every $f(\Delta_T)$-edge-connected graph with minimum degree at least $f(|E(T)|)$ and number of edges divisible by $|E(T)|$ has a $T$-decomposition.
rows.reason: Self-contained decomposition conjecture (2 papers) strengthening Barát–Thomassen; needs T-decomposition and edge-connectivity primitives; no candidates. [verify downgraded: Vague attribution ('authors of [4]') plus a pure numeric title 'Conjecture 3'; a genuine tree-decomposition (Barát–Thomassen-type) statement best kept as an implication endpoint per the paper-local default.]

rows.statement: Let $H$ be a hero and let $F$ be an oriented forest. The set $\{\overleftrightarrow{K_2}, H, F\}$ is heroic if and only if: either $F$ is the disjoint union of oriented stars, or $H$ is a transitive tournament.
rows.reason: Verbatim match with Conjecture 4.2 of 2009.13319 (heroic {K2↔,H,F} iff F is a union of oriented stars or H is a transitive tournament).

rows.statement: Every 4-edge-connected graph admits a nowhere-zero 3-flow.
rows.reason: Statement 'every 4-edge-connected graph admits a nowhere-zero 3-flow' is exactly Tutte's 3-flow conjecture; the OPG row is titled '3-flow conjecture'.

rows.statement: Is it true that $\chi'_c(G) \leq \Delta + 1$ for each graph $G$ of maximum degree $\Delta$?
rows.reason: Double paper-local numbering ('Problem 1 (Question 3 in [5])') with vague attribution and a single paper; per default posture, treat this numbered record as an edge anchor.

rows.canonical_name: Burr–Erdős conjecture for 3-uniform hypergraphs (linear Ramsey number of degenerate hypergraphs)
rows.statement: Does there exist a constant $c(d)$ depending only on $d$ such that, for every $d$-degenerate 3-uniform hypergraph $H$ on $n$ vertices, $r(H; 2) \leq c(d) \cdot n$?
rows.reason: Distinct 3-uniform hypergraph analogue of Burr–Erdős; verified the flagged Erdős rows (#833/#753/#626, sunflower) are all unrelated; asymptotic linear Ramsey bound.

rows.canonical_name: Bounded expansion and poset dimension conjecture
rows.statement: A monotone class of graphs $\mathcal{C}$ has bounded expansion if and only if for every fixed $h \geq 1$, posets of height at most $h$ whose cover graphs are in $\mathcal{C}$ have bounded dimension.
rows.reason: Named conjecture (2 papers) relating bounded expansion of a monotone class to bounded dimension of bounded-height posets with cover graphs in it; the two 'Conjecture 4' candidates are verified name-collisions with different statements. [verify downgraded: Vague attribution ('authors of [12]') plus numeric title 'Conjecture 4'; the two flagged 'Conjecture 4' candidates (col*, clustered colouring) are unrelated, so keep as an edge endpoint.]

rows.canonical_name: Complexity of triangle-free feedback arc set in tournaments (Aubian)
rows.statement: Given a tournament $T$, what is the complexity of deciding if $T$ has a triangle-free FAS?
rows.reason: 'What is the complexity of deciding…' is an open-ended complexity-determination question, not a formalizable proposition; paper-local Problem 4.1, single paper.

rows.canonical_name: Ostrovskii–Rosenthal question (asymptotic dimension of minor-excluded Cayley graphs)
rows.statement: Let $G$ be a finitely generated group and $S$ a finite generating set such that $\mathrm{Cay}(G, S)$ is minor excluded. Does it follow that $G$ has asymptotic dimension at most $2$?
rows.reason: Named self-contained question; concerns finitely generated groups and asymptotic dimension of minor-excluded Cayley graphs (infinite objects); candidates all unrelated. [verify downgraded: Named problem but about asymptotic dimension of infinite Cayley graphs (geometric group theory), out of the graph-theory formalization scope; candidates unrelated.]

rows.canonical_name: Dvořák–Ossona de Mendez–Wu problem (fractional chromatic number vs Hall ratio)
rows.statement: Determine the function $g:\mathbb{N}\rightarrow\mathbb{R}_{+}$, where $g(n)$ is defined as the maximum of the ratio $\frac{\chi_{f}(G)}{\rho(G)}$ over all $n$-vertex graphs $G$.
rows.attributed_to: Dvořák, Ossona de Mendez, and Wu
rows.reason: Verified same as Problem 4 of 1812.07327 (determine the extremal function relating χ_f(G) to ρ(G)·g(|V(G)|)); both attribution and statement match.

rows.canonical_name: Aboulker–Charbit–Naserasr conjecture (every transitive tournament is a hero in Forb_ind(F))
rows.statement: For every oriented forest $F$, every transitive tournament is a hero in $Forb_{ind}(F)$.
rows.reason: Real ACN hero conjecture but the title is pure internal numbering (Conjecture 5.1), no candidate states this proposition, and it hinges on the heavy hero/χ⃗-finite primitive, so per D7 tie-break it serves as an implication endpoint.

rows.canonical_name: Fujiwara–Papasoglu question on asymptotic dimension of minor-free graph classes
rows.statement: Is there a constant $k$ such that for any graph $H$, the class of $H$-minor free graphs has asymptotic dimension at most $k$? Can we take $k = 2$?
rows.reason: Named FP question but title is internal numbering and it requires the coarse-geometry asymptotic-dimension primitive (a poor standalone target), so it is kept as an edge endpoint rather than promoted.

rows.canonical_name: Fujiwara–Papasoglu question on asymptotic dimension of minor-free graph classes
rows.statement: Is there a constant $k$ such that for any graph $H$, the class of graphs excluding $H$ as a minor has asymptotic dimension at most $k$? Can we take $k = 2$?
rows.reason: Verbatim the same FP asymptotic-dimension question as cid 25; internal numbering plus the coarse-geometry primitive make it an edge endpoint, avoiding a duplicate standalone target.

rows.canonical_name: Hochstättler–Steiner conjecture on circular colouring of planar digraphs with large directed girth
rows.statement: Any planar digraph $D$ of directed girth at least $k\geq 3$ admits a circular homomorphism to $\vec{C}_{k-1}$. In other words, $\vec{\chi}^{\ast}(D)\leq\frac{k-1}{k-2}$.
rows.reason: Real named conjecture but deep survey internal numbering (Conjecture 5.39), no matching candidate, and it needs planarity plus a circular-homomorphism/star-dichromatic primitive, so per tie-break it is an implication endpoint.

rows.canonical_name: Dvořák–Ossona de Mendez–Wu problem on fractional chromatic number and Hall ratio (Problem 5)
rows.statement: Do there for some constant $c>0$ exist graphs $G$ of arbitrarily large fractional chromatic number such that $\rho(G)\leq c$ and $\alpha_{\mathrm{deg}_{H}}(H)\geq|E(H)|/c$ for every $H\subseteq G$?
rows.attributed_to: Dvořák, Ossona de Mendez, and Wu
rows.reason: Statement is verbatim identical to Problem 5 of Dvořák–Ossona de Mendez–Wu and the authors match exactly, so it restates the existing arxiv row.

rows.canonical_name: Day–Falgas-Ravry–Hancock problem on 1-independent percolation disconnecting the hypercube
rows.statement: For $n\geq 3$, what is the largest $p=p(n)$ for which there is a model in $\mathcal{D}_{\geq p}(Q_{n})$ under which the hypercube is always disconnected?
rows.reason: An optimisation question (largest p) framed in the paper's own probabilistic D_{≥p}(Q_n) model notation, too paper-dependent to stand alone as a proposition.

rows.canonical_name: Asgarli–Krehbiel–Levinson–Russell conjecture (the k-colouring graphs determine G)
rows.statement: For any graph $G$, the collection $\{\mathcal{C}_{k}(G)\}_{k\in\mathbb{N}}$ uniquely determines $G$.
rows.reason: Named third-party reconstruction conjecture but cited by pure internal numbering (Conjecture 6.1 [1]) over an infinite family of derived k-colouring graphs; per tie-break it is an edge endpoint.

rows.canonical_name: Asgarli–Krehbiel–Levinson–Russell conjecture (finitely many k-colouring graphs determine G)
rows.statement: There exists some function $f:\mathcal{G}\to\mathbb{N}$ such that for any graph $G$, the collection $\{\mathcal{C}_{k}(G)\}^{f(G)}_{k=1}$ uniquely determines $G$.
rows.reason: Bounded-k variant of the cid 30 reconstruction conjecture, again cited by internal numbering (Conjecture 6.2 [1]); paper-local numbered pair kept as an edge endpoint rather than a second near-duplicate target.

rows.canonical_name: Mauk–Stahl conjecture on the expected number of faces of a random embedding of K_n
rows.statement: The expected number of faces in a random embedding of the complete graph $K_{n}$ is $2\ln n + O(1)$.
rows.reason: The paper's own internally numbered conjecture (Conjecture 9.4); candidates are sibling conjectures of the same paper and the statement is an inherently probabilistic asymptotic claim (2 ln n + O(1)), so it is an edge endpoint.

rows.canonical_name: Aboulker–Adler–Kim–Sintiari–Trotignon conjecture (bounded degree + large treewidth forces an induced wall subdivision or its line graph)
rows.statement: For every $\Delta > 0$ there is a function $f_\Delta : \mathbb{N} \to \mathbb{N}$ such that every graph with maximum degree at most $\Delta$ and treewidth at least $f_\Delta(k)$ contains a subdivision of $W_{k\times k}$ or the line graph of a subdivision of $W_{k\times k}$ as an induced subgraph.
rows.attributed_to: Aboulker, Adler, Kim, Sintiari and Trotignon
rows.reason: Candidate Conjecture 3 has the exact author set and the same bounded-degree/large-treewidth-forces-induced-(sub)wall-or-line-graph statement, so it restates the existing arxiv row.

rows.canonical_name: Aboulker–Charbit–Naserasr conjecture, tree version (Forb_ind(T) is χ⃗-bounded for every oriented tree T)
rows.statement: For every oriented tree $T$, the class of digraphs with no induced $T$ is $\vec{\chi}$-bounded.
rows.reason: Tree special case of the ACN directed Gyárfás–Sumner conjecture whose canonical forest version is promoted at cid 35; kept as an edge endpoint to avoid a near-duplicate standalone target.

rows.canonical_name: Aboulker–Charbit–Naserasr Conjecture (directed Gyárfás–Sumner: Forb_ind(F) is χ⃗-bounded for every oriented forest F)
rows.statement: For any oriented forest $\overrightarrow{F}$, $\mathrm{Forb}_{\mathrm{ind}}(\overrightarrow{F})$ is $\overrightarrow{\chi}$-bounded.
rows.reason: Canonical named ACN conjecture (size-2 cluster) with a self-contained statement; no candidate row states it (2009.13319 rows cover the heroic/bounded-dichromatic framing instead), so it is promoted; needs the dichromatic-number and induced-subdigraph primitives.

rows.statement: The oriented three-edge path $\to\leftarrow\leftarrow$ is $\chi$-bounding, i.e., there exists a function $f$ such that $\chi(G) \leq f(\omega(G))$ for every oriented graph $G$ containing no induced copy of $\to\leftarrow\leftarrow$.
rows.reason: Merged cluster of the oriented χ-boundedness Conjectures 4/5; its representative statement (→←← is χ-bounding) is a case of Conjecture 5 (Or(P4) subsets) already tracked as this arxiv row.

rows.statement: Is every digraph $\kappa$-maderian? Is every digraph $\kappa'$-maderian?
rows.attributed_to: Aboulker, Cohen, Havet, Lochet, Moura, and Thomassé
rows.reason: Paper-local numbered problem (Problem 9/16) posed with the undefined paper-specific 'κ-maderian' term and no matching candidate, so it is an implication endpoint.

rows.statement: For $k_1, k_2 \geq 1$, what is the value of $\mathrm{mader}_{\delta^+}(C(k_1, k_2))$?
rows.attributed_to: Aboulker, Cohen, Havet, Lochet, Moura, and Thomassé
rows.reason: Internally numbered value-computation problem in the paper's own mader-subdivision notation for the specific digraph C(k1,k2); not a standalone proposition, so an edge endpoint.

rows.canonical_name: Addario-Berry et al. conjecture on antidirected trees in dense digraphs
rows.statement: Let $D$ be a digraph. If $|A(D)| > (k-2)|V(D)|$, then $D$ contains every antidirected tree of order $k$ as a subdigraph.
rows.reason: Statement is identical to the OPG row (|A(D)|>(k-2)|V(D)| forces every antidirected tree of order k as a subdigraph).

rows.canonical_name: Aharoni–Charbit–Howard conjecture on matchings in d-regular tripartite 3-uniform hypergraphs
rows.statement: In any $d$-regular $n \times n \times n$, 3-partite, 3-uniform hypergraph not containing repeated edges, there exists a matching of size at least $\left\lfloor \dfrac{d-1}{d}\, n \right\rfloor$.
rows.reason: Named third-party conjecture with a clean self-contained matching lower bound (⌊(d-1)n/d⌋); candidates (fair-representation/Ramsey rows) do not match, so it is promoted; needs 3-partite 3-uniform hypergraph and matching primitives.

rows.canonical_name: Aharoni's rainbow generalization of the Caccetta–Häggkvist conjecture (Conjecture 3)
rows.statement: Let $G$ be a simple $n$-vertex graph and $c$ be a colouring of $E(G)$ with $n$ colours, where each colour class has size at least $r$. Then $(G, c)$ contains a rainbow cycle of length at most $\lceil n/r \rceil$.
rows.reason: Same rainbow-cycle proposition promoted canonically at cid 43 (candidate 1806.00825#00 is the distinct properly-coloured Conjecture 4, not rainbow); kept as an edge endpoint to avoid duplication.

rows.canonical_name: Aharoni's Rainbow Conjecture (rainbow girth at most ⌈n/k⌉)
rows.statement: Let $n, k$ be positive integers. Suppose that $G$ is a simple graph on $n$ vertices. Let $b$ be a coloring of the edges of $G$ with $n$ color classes of size at least $k$; then $G$ has rainbow girth at most $\lceil n/k \rceil$.
rows.reason: Rainbow-girth phrasing of the same Aharoni rainbow Caccetta–Häggkvist conjecture promoted at cid 43; edge endpoint to avoid a duplicate target.

rows.canonical_name: Aharoni's Rainbow Generalization of the Caccetta–Häggkvist Conjecture
rows.statement: Let $n, k$ be positive integers, and let $G$ be a simple graph on $n$ vertices. Let $c : E(G) \to \{1, \ldots, n\}$ be a coloring of the edges of $G$ with color classes of size at least $k$; then $G$ has a rainbow cycle of length at most $\lceil n/k \rceil$.
rows.reason: Canonically named rainbow-cycle conjecture (rainbow cycle of length ≤ ⌈n/k⌉ from n colour classes of size ≥ k); no corpus row states the rainbow version (candidates are the proper/matroid variants or the original CH), so it is promoted; needs edge-colouring and rainbow-cycle primitives.

rows.canonical_name: Akbari–Amanihamedani–Mousavi–Nikpey–Sheybani conjecture on large induced linear forests
rows.statement: Every graph $G$ with minimum degree at least $2$ has an induced linear forest with at least $\sum_{v\in V(G)}\frac{2}{d(v)+1}$ vertices.
rows.attributed_to: Akbari, Amanihamedani, Mousavi, Nikpey, and Sheybani
rows.reason: Named third-party conjecture with a clean self-contained Caro–Wei-type bound (induced linear forest of size ≥ Σ 2/(d(v)+1)); candidates (planar induced-forest/Erdős rows) do not match, so it is promoted; needs the induced-linear-forest primitive.

rows.canonical_name: Akbari–Etesami–Mahini–Mahmoody question on long rainbow cycles in 1-factorizations of K_n
rows.statement: Does every $1$-factorization of $K_n$ have a rainbow cycle of length at least $n - 2$?
rows.reason: Descriptively named question with a self-contained statement (every 1-factorization of K_n has a rainbow cycle of length ≥ n-2); the sibling candidate is the distinct error-term open problem, so it is promoted; needs 1-factorization and rainbow-cycle primitives.

rows.canonical_name: Akiyama–Watanabe Conjecture (induced forest on ≥5/8 of the vertices of a bipartite planar graph)
rows.statement: Every bipartite planar graph has an induced forest on at least five-eighths of its vertices, i.e., $a(G) \geq 5|V(G)|/8$ for every bipartite planar graph $G$.
rows.reason: Named conjecture distinct from the general planar n/2 induced-forest (Albertson–Berman) OPG row: it is the bipartite-planar 5n/8 refinement not tracked elsewhere, so it is promoted; needs planarity.

rows.canonical_name: Albertson's problem on distance-constrained list/precolouring extension in planar graphs
rows.statement: Does there exist a constant $d$ such that whenever $G$ is a planar graph with list assignment $L$ that gives lists of size one or five to its vertices and the distance between any pair of vertices with lists of size one is at least $d$, then $G$ is $L$-colorable?
rows.reason: Named Albertson problem studied by two papers with a self-contained statement; candidates are the unrelated crossing-number Albertson conjecture and generic list-chromatic Erdős rows, so it is promoted; needs planarity plus list assignments.

rows.statement: Every planar graph has an induced forest on at least half of its vertices, i.e., $a(G) \geq |V(G)|/2$ for every planar graph $G$.
rows.reason: Identical proposition: OPG row states every planar graph on n vertices has an induced forest with at least n/2 vertices.

rows.statement: Every graph $G$ with chromatic number $k$ satisfies $\mathrm{cr}(G)\geq\mathrm{cr}(K_{k})$.
rows.reason: OPG row states exactly cr(G) >= cr(K_t) whenever chi(G) >= t; the fuzzy matcher missed it but it is the correct corpus row for Albertson's conjecture.

rows.statement: For every forest bigraph $H$ with bicomplement $J$, there exists $\varepsilon > 0$ such that, if $G$ is a bigraph that is both $H$-free and $J$-free, then there is a pure pair $(Z_1, Z_2)$ in $G$ with $Z_i \subseteq V_i(G)$ and $|Z_i| \geq \varepsilon|V_i(G)|$ for $i = 1, 2$.
rows.attributed_to: Alecu, Atminas, Lozin, and Zamaraev; Axenovich, Tompkins, and Weber
rows.reason: Named third-party conjecture (Alecu–Atminas–Lozin–Zamaraev / Axenovich–Tompkins–Weber) on linear-size pure pairs in H- and bicomplement-free bigraphs; candidate pure-pair rows are different setups (blockades, congestion).

rows.canonical_name: Hörsch's Question on Perfectly Balanced Double Tree Decomposition
rows.statement: Does there exist a polynomial time algorithm to decide if a given Eulerian double tree has a perfectly balanced double tree decomposition?
rows.reason: Paper-local numbered algorithmic-complexity question (existence of a poly-time algorithm); not a formalizable standalone statement, value is as an edge endpoint.

rows.canonical_name: Alon's Question on Common Linear Systems
rows.statement: Is it true that adding sufficiently many free variables makes any linear system not common?
rows.reason: Paper-local numbered additive-combinatorics question on making linear systems non-common; out of scope for graph-theory formalization, value as edge endpoint.

rows.canonical_name: Alon's Conjecture on Halving the Minimum Out-degree of Digraphs
rows.statement: Does there exist an absolute constant $c > 0$ such that the following holds for all pairs of natural numbers $(n, s)$? Every $2n$-vertex digraph $D$ of minimum out-degree at least $s$ contains an $n$-vertex subdigraph of minimum out-degree at least $\frac{s}{2} - c$.
rows.reason: Named Alon problem (Problem 4.1) refuted by the studying paper (s/2 - d(s) = Omega(log s), not O(1)); value is as endpoint of the refutation edge and the follow-up gap problem is already tracked as arxiv:2210.12699#00.

rows.canonical_name: Alon's Conjecture on the Independence Number of Random Cayley Graphs
rows.statement: Let $G$ be a group of size $n$. The independence number of the random Cayley graph $G(p)$ is at most $\tilde{O}(p^{-1})$ whp.
rows.reason: Probabilistic with-high-probability statement about random Cayley graphs; out of scope for a Rocq statement target, value as edge endpoint.

rows.statement: Let $d = o(n)$. Then every $d$-regular graph $G$ on $n$ vertices contains a spanning subgraph $H$ such that $$m(H, k) = (1 + o(1))\frac{n}{d+1}$$ for all $0 \leq k \leq d$.
rows.reason: Same Alon–Wei conjecture on matching counts of near-regular spanning subgraphs; the arxiv row states the exact form |m(H,k) - n/(d+1)| <= 2, of which this o(1) form is the companion.

rows.statement: The upper bound $f(n) \leq \frac{c_2 n^{1/3}(\log\log n)^{1/3}}{(\log n)^{1/3}}$ is closer to the true order of magnitude of $f(n)$ than the lower bound $f(n) \geq \frac{c_1 n^{1/3}}{\log^{4/3} n}$, where $f(n)$ is the minimum number of colors needed to color the positive integers less than $n$ so that $n$ cannot be written as a monochromatic sum of distinct integers.
rows.reason: Vague/meta comparison of which of two bounds on f(n) is closer to the true order of magnitude; additive number theory, not a crisp proposition.

rows.canonical_name: Alon et al. C_k-Free Max-Cut Conjecture
rows.statement: For all $k \geq 3$, every $C_k$-free graph $G$ with $m$ edges satisfies $\mathrm{Max\text{-}Cut}(G) \geq \frac{m}{2} + \Omega_k\!\left(m^{(k+1)/(k+2)}\right)$.
rows.reason: Named conjecture on the max-cut surplus of C_k-free graphs (exponent (k+1)/(k+2)); not covered by any candidate row.

rows.canonical_name: Alon et al. Conjecture on Separation Dimension and Maximum Degree
rows.statement: Every graph with maximum degree $\Delta$ has separation dimension $O(\Delta)$.
rows.reason: Named conjecture that separation dimension is O(Delta); requires defining the separation-dimension primitive, not tracked elsewhere.

rows.canonical_name: Alon et al. Problem on Bounded Separation Dimension and Degeneracy
rows.statement: Do graphs with bounded separation dimension have bounded degeneracy (equivalently, bounded average degree)?
rows.reason: Companion qualitative open problem (does bounded separation dimension imply bounded degeneracy) to cid 58; value as edge endpoint rather than crisp statement target.

rows.statement: Let $k$ be a positive integer. If $G$ is a graph on $n$ vertices that does not contain a non-empty $k$-divisible subgraph, then $e(G)\leq(k-1)n$.
rows.reason: Named conjecture with a clean edge-count bound e(G) <= (k-1)n for graphs with no non-empty k-divisible subgraph; not tracked by any candidate.

rows.statement: Planar graphs have bounded nonrepetitive chromatic number, i.e., there exists a constant $k$ such that every planar graph $G$ satisfies $\pi(G) \leq k$.
rows.reason: Named conjecture that planar graphs have bounded nonrepetitive chromatic number; requires planarity plus a nonrepetitive-coloring primitive, not tracked elsewhere.

rows.statement: Suppose $k \to \infty$ and $n/k \to \infty$, and consider $\ell$ satisfying $\binom{k}{2} - \ell = \Omega(k^2)$ and $\ell = \Omega(k^2)$. Then $\Pr(X_{G,k} = \ell) = O\!\left(1/\sqrt{k}\right)$.
rows.reason: Probabilistic anti-concentration conjecture with limit conditions; out of scope for a Rocq statement, and the candidate Conjecture 1.12 is a distinct anti-concentration statement.

rows.statement: If $G$ is a bipartite graph of maximum degree $\Delta$, then $\operatorname{ch}(G)=O(\log\Delta)$.
rows.reason: Identical proposition: list chromatic number of bipartite graphs of maximum degree Delta is O(log Delta).

rows.canonical_name: Alon–Krivelevich Problem on Divisible Subdivision Bounds
rows.statement: Improve the superexponential upper bound on $f(H, q)$ for subcubic graphs $H$ on $n$ vertices with $m$ edges, where $f(H,q)$ is the smallest integer such that every $K_f$-minor contains a $q$-divisible subdivision of $H$.
rows.reason: An 'improve the superexponential bound on f(H,q)' improvement/meta problem, not a self-contained proposition.

rows.statement: For $H$ fixed and $m$ sufficiently large, every $H$-free graph $G$ with $m$ edges satisfies $\mathrm{Max\text{-}Cut}(G) \geq \frac{m}{2} + \Omega_H\!\left(m^{3/4}\right)$.
rows.reason: Named conjecture on the m^{3/4} max-cut surplus of H-free graphs; the same-paper candidate arxiv:2102.10220#00 is a different statement about h(n,k,K_r).

rows.statement: For every tournament $H$, there exists $\epsilon>0$ such that every $H$-free tournament $T$ of order $n$ satisfies $\vec{\alpha}(T)\geq n^{\epsilon}$.
rows.reason: Tournament Erdős–Hajnal conjecture (polynomial domination number in H-free tournaments); no tournament-EH row exists in the corpus.

rows.canonical_name: Alon's Conjecture on Ramsey Cayley Graphs
rows.statement: There is a constant $C$ such that every finite group has a $C$-Ramsey Cayley graph.
rows.reason: Same Ramsey-Cayley-graphs conjecture that groups admit Cayley graphs with only logarithmic cliques/independent sets; OPG is the tracked representative (abelian formulation vs finite-group phrasing here).

rows.statement: There exists a function $h$ such that every digraph with minimum out-degree $h(k)$ has a partition $(V_1, V_2)$ such that, for $i = 1, 2$, $D\langle V_i\rangle$ has minimum out-degree $k$.
rows.reason: Symmetric case of the OPG digraph out-degree splitting problem: h(k) forcing a partition into two parts each of minimum out-degree k.

rows.statement: Does there exist, for all $s, t \geq 1$, a number $F(s,t) \in \mathbb{N}$ such that every digraph $D$ with $\delta^+(D) \geq F(s,t)$ has a partition $V(D) = A \sqcup B$ such that $\delta^+(D[A]) \geq s$ and $\delta^+(D[B]) \geq t$?
rows.reason: Asymmetric (s,t) form of the same OPG problem of splitting a digraph under minimum out-degree constraints.

rows.canonical_name: Alspach's Question on the Perimeter Gap of Vertex-Transitive Digraphs
rows.statement: Is the maximum perimeter gap (the gap between the circumference and the order of the digraph) a growing function of $n$ for connected vertex transitive digraphs on $n$ vertices?
rows.reason: Informal qualitative question ('is the gap a growing function of n'); the crisp versions are already tracked as arxiv:2602.16333#00/#01, so this serves as an edge endpoint.

rows.statement: Every properly edge-coloured $K_n$ has a rainbow path of length $n - 2$.
rows.reason: Named conjecture that every properly edge-coloured K_n has a rainbow path of length n-2; candidates concern rainbow-cycle error terms and monochromatic reachability, not this statement.

rows.statement: Any toroidal graph has cop number at most $3$.
rows.reason: Named third-party conjecture that every toroidal graph has cop number at most 3; no candidate matches (paper's own rows cover planar/outerplanar classification), and it needs a pursuit-game primitive.

rows.canonical_name: Araújo–Cohen–de Rezende–Havet–Moura Conjecture (proper orientation number of bipartite graphs)
rows.statement: Does there exist a constant $C$ such that every bipartite graph $G$ satisfies $\vec{\chi}(G) \leq \frac{\Delta(G)}{2} + C$?
rows.attributed_to: Araujo, Cohen, de Rezende, Havet, and Moura
rows.reason: Named recognized problem asking for a constant C with proper orientation number ≤ Δ/2 + C on bipartite graphs; crisp existence proposition, no candidate matches, needs proper-orientation-number primitive.

rows.canonical_name: Araújo et al. Problem: proper orientation number bounded by maximum average degree
rows.statement: Can $\vec{\chi}(G)$ be bounded above by a function of $\mathrm{mad}(G)$?
rows.attributed_to: Araujo, Cohen, de Rezende, Havet, and Moura
rows.reason: Paper-local numbered problem (Problem 1.7) phrased as a loose research direction 'can χ_vec be bounded by a function of mad'; better as an edge endpoint than a standalone statement target.

rows.statement: Let $G$ be a triangle-free graph. Then for every colouring (not necessarily optimal) of $G$, there is a rainbow induced subgraph isomorphic to a $\chi(G)$-vertex path.
rows.reason: Named self-contained conjecture (rainbow induced χ(G)-vertex path for every colouring of a triangle-free graph); the same-paper candidate is a different Question, no candidate matches.

rows.statement: Is there a constant $C$ such that the following holds for every digraph $D$ that is the union of two arc-disjoint arborescences? The edges of $D$ can be coloured blue and red such that the blue and red graphs are both arborescences and the blue and red out-degrees of each vertex differ by at most $C$?
rows.reason: Named third-party question with a specific self-contained statement about recolouring a union of two arc-disjoint arborescences into balanced ones; no candidate matches.

rows.canonical_name: Archdeacon–Richter–Širáň Problem on Simultaneous Non-orientable Obstructions
rows.statement: Does there exist a graph which is simultaneously an obstruction for two different nonorientable surfaces?
rows.reason: OPG row's source text asks whether a graph is a minor-minimal obstruction for two non-orientable surfaces — the same proposition as this studied problem.

rows.canonical_name: Arman–Gunderson–Tsaturian Conjecture (maximum cycles in C_{2k+1}-free graphs)
rows.statement: For any $k > 1$, for sufficiently large $n$, $T_2(n)$ is the unique $n$-vertex $C_{2k+1}$-free graph containing $m(n; C_{2k+1})$ cycles.
rows.reason: Named extremal conjecture that the balanced complete bipartite Turán graph uniquely maximizes cycle count among C_{2k+1}-free graphs; candidates (coprime graphs, reconfiguration) are unrelated.

rows.canonical_name: Arman–Tsaturian Conjecture on the Number of Cycles
rows.statement: A simple graph with $n$ vertices and average degree $d$ has at most $\left(1 + O\!\left(\frac{\log d}{d}\right)\right)^n \cdot \left(\frac{d}{e}\right)^n$ cycles.
rows.reason: Named conjecture bounding the number of cycles by average degree with an explicit (1+O(log d/d))^n(d/e)^n form; candidates unrelated, and the O(·) bound needs asymptotics.

rows.canonical_name: Asymptotic Zarankiewicz Conjecture (crossing number of K_{n,n})
rows.statement: $\lambda = \lambda_0 = \dfrac{1}{16}$, where $\lambda := \lim_{n\to\infty} n^{-4}\operatorname{cr}(K_{n,n})$ and $\lambda_0 := \lim_{n\to\infty} n^{-4}\operatorname{cr}_0(K_{n,n})$.
rows.reason: Same named conjecture: the asymptotic λ=λ₀=1/16 form is strictly implied by the exact Zarankiewicz formula tracked in the canonical OPG crossing-number row.

rows.canonical_name: Axenovich–Gyárfás–Hong–Mubayi Problem on Hypergraph Ramsey Jumps
rows.statement: For which $3$-uniform hypergraphs $G$, is $r(G;q)$ double exponential? Are there other jumps that the Ramsey function exhibits?
rows.reason: Open-ended classification/direction ('for which G is r(G;q) double exponential? are there other jumps?') rather than a formalizable proposition.

rows.statement: $B_3$-branches are the main structural component of ABC-minimal trees.
rows.reason: Vague informal structural claim ('B₃-branches are the main structural component') attributed only to reference authors; not a crisp standalone statement.

rows.canonical_name: Bajnok–Brinkmann Conjecture (triangle-free subcubic graphs with small independence number)
rows.statement: There are exactly six 2-connected triangle-free subcubic $n$-vertex graphs $G$ with $\alpha(G) < \frac{3}{8} n$, namely $F_{11}$, $F^{(1)}_{14}$, $F^{(2)}_{14}$, $F^{(1)}_{19}$, $F^{(2)}_{19}$, $F_{22}$.
rows.reason: Named conjecture giving an exact finite characterization (six exceptional graphs) of 2-connected triangle-free subcubic graphs with α(G)<3n/8; no candidate matches, needs the explicit exceptional graphs. [verify downgraded: Numbered conjecture characterising the six exceptional 2-connected triangle-free subcubic graphs with α<3n/8; the explicit paper-defined graph list makes it valuable only as an implication endpoint, not a standalone target.]

rows.statement: In any strongly connected digraph, there exist arc-disjoint in-branching $T^{-}$ and out-branching $T^{+}$ (allowing arbitrary overlapping in terms of vertices), both with linear size and rooted at the same vertex $r$.
rows.attributed_to: Balliu, Brunelli, Crescenzi, Olivetti, and Viennot
rows.reason: Distinct from the OPG Thomassen k-arc-strong/distinct-root branching conjecture: this requires strongly-connected input, linear-size branchings, and a shared root; 'linear size' needs asymptotics.

rows.canonical_name: Balogh–Bollobás–Morris Conjecture (speed gap for ordered graphs)
rows.statement: A hereditary class of (totally) ordered graphs has, up to isomorphism, either at most $2^{O(n)}$ graphs with $n$ elements, or at least $n^{n/2+o(n)}$ such graphs.
rows.reason: Named conjecture on the enumeration jump (2^{O(n)} vs n^{n/2+o(n)}) for hereditary classes of ordered graphs; candidates (rainbow cycles, weak-diameter colouring) unrelated.

rows.statement: For every $r \geq 2$ there is a $\delta_r > 0$ such that the following holds: If $G$ is a $K_{r+1}$-free graph on $n$ vertices with $e(G) \geq t_r(n) - \delta_r n^2$ edges, then there is a pentagonal Turán graph $G^*$ on $n$ vertices with $e(G^*) \geq e(G)$ and $D_r(G^*) \geq D_r(G)$.
rows.attributed_to: Balogh, Clemen, Lavrov, Lidický, and Pfender
rows.reason: Named stability-type conjecture about near-extremal K_{r+1}-free graphs and pentagonal Turán graphs maximizing D_r; candidates unrelated, needs the pentagon-count primitive.

rows.statement: The maximum quartet distance between two phylogenetic trees on $n$ leaves is $\left(\frac{2}{3} + o(1)\right)\binom{n}{4}$.
rows.reason: Named self-contained asymptotic conjecture that the maximum quartet distance between two n-leaf phylogenetic trees is (2/3+o(1))(n choose 4); candidates unrelated.

rows.canonical_name: Bang-Jensen et al. Conjecture (F-Subdivision complexity dichotomy)
rows.statement: For every digraph $F$, the $F$-\textsc{Subdivision} problem is polynomial-time solvable or NP-complete.
rows.reason: Named dichotomy conjecture that F-Subdivision is polynomial or NP-complete for every digraph F; candidate (induced-disjoint-paths gap) unrelated, requires a computation model.

rows.statement: What is the average complexity of the $k$-level in an arrangement of great-circles on the sphere when the south pole is chosen uniformly at random among all cells?
rows.reason: Computational-geometry value question ('what is the average complexity of the k-level ...') with random pole choice; not a formalizable proposition and out of graph-theory scope.

rows.statement: All graphs in $\mathcal{B}$ are Hamiltonian.
rows.reason: Exact match: the class B is the 3-connected cubic planar bipartite graphs, and the OPG row states precisely that every such graph is Hamiltonian.

rows.statement: For any fixed tree $T$, there is an integer $c_T$ such that every $c_T$-edge-connected graph with its number of edges divisible by $|E(T)|$ can be $T$-decomposed.
rows.reason: Same named tree-decomposition conjecture; the existing arxiv row from a studying paper (Conjecture 1.2) is the tracked Barát–Thomassen statement, preferred over creating a near-duplicate.

rows.statement: Let $r,g\in\mathbb{N}$. Every $r$-regular digraph with girth at least $g$ has at least $r(g-1)+1$ vertices.
rows.reason: Named self-contained conjecture that every r-regular digraph with girth ≥ g has ≥ r(g-1)+1 vertices; candidates unrelated, stateable cleanly with directed girth and regularity.

rows.statement: For every positive integer $k$, every graph either is $P_k$-free or contains an avoidable $P_k$.
rows.attributed_to: Beisegel, Chudnovsky, Gurvich, Milanič, and Servatius
rows.reason: Named avoidable-paths conjecture (every graph is P_k-free or contains an avoidable P_k); the same-paper candidates are different Questions (two disjoint P_k / a family H), needs the avoidability primitive.

rows.canonical_name: Ben-David–Eiron–Simon Question (sign rank of large VC classes)
rows.statement: What is the sign rank of maximum classes of large VC dimension? In particular, do there exist maximum classes $C \subseteq \{\pm 1\}^N$ with VC dimension 2 and sign rank $\tilde{\Theta}(N^{1/2})$?
rows.reason: Learning-theory value question about the sign rank of maximum concept classes with large VC dimension; out of graph-theory scope and not a crisp proposition.

rows.canonical_name: Benjamini–Kalifa–Tzalik Question (minor-universality threshold of the hypercube)
rows.statement: Which (if either) of the two bounds $\Omega\!\left(\frac{2^{d}}{d}\right)$ and $O\!\left(\frac{2^{d}}{\sqrt{d}}\right)$ on the minor-universality threshold of $Q_{d}$ is tight?
rows.reason: Value question asking which of two asymptotic bounds on the minor-universality threshold of Q_d is tight; not a formalizable proposition, candidates unrelated.

rows.statement: There exists a connectivity threshold $k_0$ such that every $k_0$-connected graph embedded in $\mathbb{R}$ has its full vertex set reconstructible from the edge lengths.
rows.reason: Named reconstruction conjecture, but requires a real-line embedding/edge-length metric layer — the same out-of-scope pattern the corpus applies to distance geometry (cf. erdos:705).

rows.canonical_name: Bensmail et al. tree T-decomposition conjecture
rows.statement: There is a function $f$ such that, for any fixed tree $T$ with maximum degree $\Delta_T$, every $f(\Delta_T)$-edge-connected graph with minimum degree at least $f(|E(T)|)$ and number of edges divisible by $|E(T)|$ has a $T$-decomposition.
rows.reason: Rep statement is verbatim identical (conditions merely reordered) to the tracked conjecture arxiv:1507.08208#00, which is reference [3] the studying paper cites.

rows.canonical_name: Berger et al. local-to-global tournament colouring conjecture
rows.statement: There is a function $f$ such that every $t$-local tournament $T$ satisfies $\vec{\chi}(T) \leq f(t)$.
rows.attributed_to: Berger, Choromanski, Chudnovsky, Fox, Loebl, Scott, Seymour, and Thomassé
rows.reason: Named third-party conjecture (Berger–Chudnovsky–Fox–Scott–Seymour–Thomassé et al.), self-contained: bounded local dichromatic number implies bounded global; no candidate matches, needs dichromatic-number/t-local primitives.

rows.statement: For every positive integer $k$, every digraph with minimum out-degree at least $2k-1$ contains $k$ disjoint directed cycles.
rows.reason: Verbatim match to opg:the_bermond_thomassen_conjecture (min out-degree ≥2k−1 forces k disjoint directed cycles); erdos:577 is the unrelated undirected 4-cycle variant.

rows.statement: Is it true that $\nu(G) \geq (3n + n_2)/9$ for every subcubic graph $G$?
rows.attributed_to: Biedl, Demaine, Duncan, Fleischer and Kobourov
rows.reason: Named third-party question with a concrete self-contained inequality (9ν(G) ≥ 3n+n₂ for subcubic G); no candidates; all primitives (matching number, degree-2 count) are standard and directly formalizable.

rows.statement: The class of all tournaments has the $BIG\Rightarrow BIG$ property.
rows.reason: Genuine named conjecture the paper studies, but the rep statement references the undefined BIG⇒BIG property (not self-contained), so it anchors the studying-paper edge rather than owing a statement.

rows.canonical_name: Blumenthal et al. question on fractional chromatic number vs. Hall ratio
rows.statement: Can the fractional chromatic number $\chi_f(G)$ be bounded by a fast-growing function of the Hall ratio $\rho(G)$?
rows.reason: Paper-internal 'Question 16' with a loose quantifier ('fast-growing function'); a real studied question but not a clean promotable statement; candidates (list-coloring erdos rows) do not match.

rows.statement: For any positive $\alpha$, if the size $\alpha(G)$ of a maximum independent set in an $n$-vertex graph $G$ is at least $\alpha n$, then $h(G) = o(n)$.
rows.reason: Same conjecture as cid 105 (h=η = min set hitting all maximum independent sets; if α(G)≥δn then η(G)=o(n)); promoted canonically at cid 105, so this is a cross-cluster duplicate with no distinct corpus alias target.

rows.canonical_name: Bollobás–Erdős problem on large C₄-free subgraphs
rows.statement: Does every graph with $m$ edges contain a $C_4$-free subgraph with $\Omega(m^{3/4})$ edges? More precisely, determine $f(m, C_4)$, the size of the largest $C_4$-free subgraph guaranteed in any graph with $m$ edges.
rows.reason: Named Bollobás–Erdős extremal problem with self-contained statement (every m-edge graph has a C₄-free subgraph with Ω(m^{3/4}) edges); candidate 2307.08361#01 is the distinct average-degree variant, not a match.

rows.statement: For every real $\delta>0$, every graph $G$ with $\alpha(G)\geq\delta|V(G)|$ satisfies $\eta(G)\leq o(|V(G)|)$.
rows.reason: Named conjecture studied by two papers (hitting all maximum independent sets): if α(G)≥δ|V| then the hitting number η(G)=o(|V|); no corpus row exists, o(n) bound needs asymptotics.

rows.canonical_name: Bollobás–Leader–Walters open problem on cop/robber game values
rows.statement: Is it possible that $\mathrm{Val}_C < \mathrm{Val}_R$ for some game space $X$ and some $k$? In particular, is it possible that both players, the cops and the robber, win the game?
rows.reason: Named continuous pursuit-game problem, but rep statement uses undefined paper-local notation (Val_C, Val_R, game space X); real conjecture serving as an edge endpoint, not self-contained enough to promote; candidate 1710.11281#02 is a distinct continuous cops-robbers question.

rows.canonical_name: Bollobás–Leader–Tiba Conjecture 1 (three-element sumset saturation in ℤ_p)
rows.statement: For subsets $A, B$ of $\mathbb{Z}_p$ of size at most $\alpha p$ for an appropriate constant $\alpha > 0$, one only needs three elements $b_1, b_2, b_3 \in B$ to guarantee $|A + \{b_1, b_2, b_3\}| \geq |A| + |B| - 1$.
rows.reason: Named but pure additive combinatorics (sumsets in ℤ_p); no graph-theory repo applies and candidates do not match, so out of scope for this graph-theory corpus.

rows.statement: For a subset $A \subseteq \mathbb{Z}^d$ that is not contained in the union of any $t$ parallel hyperplanes, does there exist $A' \subseteq A$ with $|A'| \leq C\sqrt{|A|}$ and $|A' + A'| \geq (2d - \varepsilon)|A|$?
rows.reason: Named but pure additive combinatorics (sumsets in ℤ^d, hyperplane condition); out of scope for the graph-theory corpus; candidates do not match.

rows.statement: Given constants $\varepsilon > 0$ and $\lambda > 0$, is there a constant $c > 0$ such that the following holds? If $A, B$ are subsets of $\mathbb{Z}_p$ with $|A| = |B| = n$ and $|A + B| \leq \lambda n$, then there exist sets $A' \subseteq A$ and $B' \subseteq B$ such that $|A'| \geq (1 - \varepsilon)|A|$, $|B'| \leq c$, and $|A' + B'| \geq (1 - \varepsilon)|A + B|$.
rows.reason: Named but pure additive combinatorics (small-sumset saturation in ℤ_p); out of scope for the graph-theory corpus; candidates do not match.

rows.canonical_name: Bonamy et al. polynomial Kühn–Osthus conjecture
rows.statement: For every graph $H$, the function $p(s, H)$ appearing in the Kühn–Osthus theorem (that every $K_{s,s}$-free graph $G$ with average degree at least $p(s, H)$ contains an induced subdivision of $H$) can be taken to be a polynomial in $s$.
rows.attributed_to: Bonamy, Bousquet, Pilipczuk, Rzążewski, Thomassé, and Walczak
rows.reason: Named six-author conjecture with self-contained statement (the average-degree bound forcing an induced subdivision of H in K_{s,s}-free graphs can be polynomial in s); candidate 2307.08361#01 is the distinct C₄ special case, not a match.

rows.statement: Does every connected graph on $n$ vertices that is not an odd semi-clique admit a $\lfloor \frac{n}{2} \rfloor$-path decomposition?
rows.reason: Verbatim match to arxiv:1609.06257#00 (every connected non-odd-semi-clique graph admits a ⌊|V|/2⌋-path decomposition).

rows.statement: For every graph $G$ such that $\Delta(G)\geq 9$, if $\omega(G)\leq\Delta(G)-1$, then $\chi(G)\leq\Delta(G)-1$.
rows.reason: Equivalent to opg:the_borodin_kostochka_conjecture (χ≤max{Δ−1,ω} for Δ≥9); the studying paper considers the correspondence-coloring variant but the conjecture itself is the classic one.

rows.canonical_name: Borodin's question on 3-choosability of planar graphs
rows.statement: Is every planar graph without cycles of lengths 4 to 8 3-choosable?
rows.reason: Borodin's 3-choosability question for planar graphs with a forbidden band of cycle lengths; already tracked via arxiv:1508.03437#00 from the same paper (the extraction's 4–8 band is a rendering of that tracked 4–7/4–6 open question).

rows.statement: Let $k\geq 2$ be an integer. There is a constant $C$ such that $\chi^{\prime}_{k}(G)\leq k+C$ for every graph $G$.
rows.reason: Named third-party conjecture with self-contained statement (for each k≥2 there is C with χ′_k(G)≤k+C for all G); no candidate matches; needs the modular k-edge-colouring primitive.

rows.canonical_name: Bounded-degree product-structure conjecture for planar graphs
rows.statement: For each $\Delta\in\mathbb{N}$, let $\mathcal{G}_{\Delta}$ be the family of planar graphs of maximum degree $\Delta$. Do there exist functions $t:\mathbb{N}\to\mathbb{N}$, $d:\mathbb{N}\to\mathbb{N}$, and $c:\mathbb{N}\to\mathbb{N}$ such that, for each $\Delta\in\mathbb{N}$ and each $G\in\mathcal{G}_{\Delta}$ there exists a graph $H$ of treewidth at most $t(\Delta)$ and maximum degree $d(\Delta)$ and a path $P$ such that $G\subseteq H\boxtimes P\boxtimes K_{c(\Delta)}$?
rows.reason: Precise, substantive open question but explicitly anonymous ('a number of researchers'); fails the named-attribution promotion bar, so it anchors the studying-paper edge; candidate 2208.10074#00 is the distinct separator-based product-structure statement.

rows.canonical_name: Bounded proper orientation number of planar graphs (folklore)
rows.statement: The proper orientation number $\vec{\chi}(G)$ is bounded above by a finite constant on the class of all planar graphs.
rows.reason: Recognized folklore conjecture, but folklore = no named third-party attribution, so it fails the promotion bar and serves as an edge endpoint; oriented-chromatic-number candidate is a different parameter.

rows.canonical_name: Brass et al. simultaneous embeddability problem
rows.statement: Is there a set $\mathcal{G} = \{G_1, G_2\}$ consisting of two planar graphs of the same order such that $\mathcal{G}$ is not simultaneously embeddable?
rows.attributed_to: Brass, Cenek, Duncan, Efrat, Erten, Ismailescu, Kobourov, Lubiw and Mitchell
rows.reason: Named nine-author problem with self-contained statement (existence of two same-order planar graphs that are not simultaneously embeddable); in the embedding/drawing family the corpus formalizes under topological-graph-theory; needs a simultaneous-embedding primitive.

rows.canonical_name: Brass–Moser–Pach problems on unit distances in higher dimensions
rows.statement: For every $d\geq 3$ and every $d$-norm $\|.\|$, is $U_{\|.\|}(n)$ asymptotically at least $\Omega(n\log n)$?
rows.reason: Named metric-geometry problems (unit distances under d-norms) requiring a real-space point/distance layer — the corpus's established out-of-scope pattern for unit-distance geometry (cf. erdos:704/705).

rows.canonical_name: Brass's question on unit distances in normed planes
rows.statement: Does there exist a $2$-norm $\|.\|$ with $U_{\|.\|}(n)\leq O(n\log n)$?
rows.reason: Named metric-geometry question (2-norm with U(n)≤O(n log n)) requiring a real-plane point/distance layer; out of scope like the other unit-distance rows; candidates do not match.

rows.statement: Does there exist a $k$-pairable state of size $n = \mathrm{poly}(k)$?
rows.attributed_to: Bravyi, Sharma, Szegedy, and de Wolf
rows.reason: Quantum-information problem about pairable state size poly(k); out of scope for the graph-theory repos and no candidate matches.

rows.statement: $f(n, e+3, e) = o(n^2)$ for any fixed $e \geq 3$.
rows.reason: Famous named 3-uniform-hypergraph extremal conjecture f(n,e+3,e)=o(n^2); none of the erdos candidates match (Ramsey/critical-edges/independent-set-sequence).

rows.statement: If the edge set of $K_{n,n}$ is partitioned into sets $E_1, E_2, \ldots, E_n$ of size $n$ each, then there exists a matching in $K_{n,n}$ consisting of one edge from all but possibly one $E_i$.
rows.reason: Famous named conjecture (near-transversal of Latin squares as a matching in edge-partitioned K_{n,n}); the same-paper candidate 1.14 is a different generalized bound and erdos/opg rows are unrelated.

rows.canonical_name: Bubeck–Linial problem 4 (tree inducibility bounded away from 1)
rows.statement: Does there exist $\varepsilon > 0$ such that the inducibility of every tree that is neither a star nor a path is at most $1 - \varepsilon$?
rows.reason: Named third-party inducibility problem with self-contained statement; no candidate matches and it fits extremal density theory.

rows.canonical_name: Bubeck–Linial problem 5 (universal convergent sequence of trees)
rows.statement: Does there exist a convergent sequence $(T_n)_{n\in\mathbb{N}}$ of trees in which the limit density $\lim_{n\to\infty} d(S, T_n)$ of every tree $S$ is positive?
rows.reason: Named third-party problem on limit densities of trees, self-contained; no candidate matches.

rows.canonical_name: Bucić–Letzter–Sudakov problem (oriented Ramsey number of bounded-degree acyclic digraphs)
rows.statement: Is it true that $\overrightarrow{r}_1(H) = O_\Delta(n)$ for every acyclic digraph $H$ with $n$ vertices and maximum degree $\Delta$?
rows.reason: Named third-party digraph conjecture that oriented Ramsey number is linear in n for bounded-degree acyclic digraphs; the derived and erdos candidates are unrelated.

rows.statement: A doubly exponential upper bound holds for the multidimensional Erdős–Szekeres numbers $M_d(n)$ in every dimension $d$.
rows.reason: Named conjecture on monotone patterns in sequences of tuples (order/Ramsey combinatorics), not a graph statement and not covered by any of the 14 graph repos.

rows.canonical_name: Bukh problem on coloring random subgraphs
rows.statement: Does there exist a constant $c$ so that for every graph $G$, the expected chromatic number of its random subgraphs satisfies: $$E(\chi(G_{1/2})) > c\,\frac{\chi(G)}{\log \chi(G)}\,?$$
rows.reason: Statement is verbatim the OpenProblemGarden entry: does there exist c with E(χ(G_{1/2})) > c·χ(G)/log χ(G).

rows.canonical_name: Burcroff conjecture (α = IR for products of balanced complete multipartite graphs)
rows.statement: If $G = \prod_{i=1}^{n} K[u_i, t_i]$ is a direct product of balanced complete multipartite graphs, then $\alpha(G) = IR(G)$.
rows.reason: Author's own Conjecture 1.1 (balanced case); it is a special case of the already-tracked general Conjecture 1.2 (arxiv:1904.02595#00), so it serves only as an implication endpoint.

rows.statement: For a connected graph $G$ of order $n$, $b(G) \leq \lceil \sqrt{n} \rceil$.
rows.reason: Famous named Bonato–Janssen–Roshanbin conjecture b(G) ≤ ⌈√n⌉ with a self-contained ball-cover characterization; no candidate matches (all tournament/oriented-coloring rows).

rows.canonical_name: Burr–Erdős problem on 2-Ramsey complete sequences (gap problem)
rows.statement: Can the gap between the upper bound $|A \cap [n]| \leq C\log^3 n$ for the sparsest entirely 2-Ramsey complete sequence and the lower bound showing no 2-Ramsey complete sequence satisfies $|A \cap [n]| \leq c\log^2 n$ for all sufficiently large $n$ be narrowed?
rows.reason: Additive-combinatorics bound-narrowing question (log^3 n vs log^2 n) on integer sequences; out of scope for the graph repos and phrased as gap improvement, no candidate matches.

rows.canonical_name: Burr–Erdős problem on r-Ramsey complete sequences (r ≥ 3)
rows.statement: For $r \geq 3$, does there exist an $r$-Ramsey complete sequence $A$ with $|A \cap [n]| = n^{o(1)}$? More generally, find any non-trivial upper bound on the density of the sparsest $r$-Ramsey complete sequence.
rows.reason: Additive-combinatorics existence/density question on integer sequences; out of scope for the graph repos and no candidate matches.

rows.canonical_name: Burr–Erdős question on Ramsey complete polynomial sequences
rows.statement: Which polynomial sequences $(P(m))_{m \geq 1}$ are $r$-Ramsey complete?
rows.reason: Open-ended 'which polynomial sequences are r-Ramsey complete' question in additive combinatorics; out of scope and not a crisp standalone statement.

rows.canonical_name: Burr's conjecture (oriented trees in n-chromatic digraphs)
rows.statement: Every digraph with chromatic number $2k-2$ contains every oriented tree of order $k$ as a subdigraph.
rows.reason: Matches the OpenProblemGarden entry verbatim: every digraph with chromatic number ≥ 2k-2 contains every oriented tree of order k.

rows.canonical_name: Butler et al. question on hat guessing number of K_{n,n}
rows.statement: Does there exist a constant $\alpha > 0$ independent of $n$, such that $HG(K_{n,n}) \geq n^\alpha$ for sufficiently large $n$?
rows.reason: Paper-local 'Question 1.1' with vague 'et al.' attribution asking whether HG(K_{n,n}) ≥ n^α; value is as an implication endpoint, no candidate matches.

rows.canonical_name: Bárány–Kalai problem on convex-set separation partitions
rows.statement: Determine or estimate the least integer $f=f(d,s,t)$ such that for any set $P$ of $f$ points in $\mathbb{R}^{d}$ there is a partition $P=A\cup B$ such that any $s$-convex set containing $A$ must intersect any $t$-convex set containing $B$.
rows.reason: Discrete-geometry estimation problem about points in R^d and s/t-convex sets; out of scope for the graph repos and the same-paper candidate 1.4 is the more general r-ary version.

rows.statement: Let $n, k$ be positive integers. Suppose that $G$ is a simple digraph on $n$ vertices with $\delta^+(v) \geq \lceil n/k \rceil$ for all $v \in V(G)$; then $G$ contains a directed cycle of length at most $k$.
rows.reason: Equivalent formulation of the OpenProblemGarden entry: minimum outdegree ≥ ⌈n/k⌉ forces a directed cycle of length ≤ k.

rows.canonical_name: Cames van Batenburg et al. conjecture (fractional coloring of subcubic triangle-free graphs)
rows.statement: Every subcubic triangle-free graph avoiding $F^{(1)}_{14}$, $F^{(2)}_{14}$, $F_{11}$, $F_{22}$, $F^{(1)}_{19}$, and $F^{(2)}_{19}$ as subgraphs is fractionally $8/3$-colorable.
rows.reason: Highly paper-dependent (specific forbidden gadgets F_11, F_14, F_19, F_22) so cannot stand alone; the same-paper candidate 1.6 is a distinct forbidden-set/fraction; kept as an endpoint for its two studying papers.

rows.statement: For which integers $k$ is the class of digraphs whose induced directed cycles all have length exactly $k$ $\vec{\chi}$-bounded?
rows.reason: Open-ended paper-local 'Question 3.2/3.3' ('for which k') whose value is as an implication endpoint; candidates are derived technical gadgets, not the question itself.

rows.statement: For which integers $t \geq 3$ is it true that the class of $t$-chordal digraphs (digraphs in which every induced directed cycle has length exactly $t$) with bounded clique number has bounded dichromatic number? That is, for which $t$ is this class $\vec{\chi}$-bounded?
rows.reason: Open-ended 'for which t' question serving as an implication endpoint; candidates are derived gadgets and a distinct holes-in-bounded-clique conjecture, not this question.

rows.canonical_name: Caro's set mapping question on q(m,k,d)
rows.statement: Let $q(m,k,d)$ be the maximum $q$ such that for every mapping $f:\binom{M}{k}\to\binom{M}{k}$ with $|M|=m$ and $|X\cap f(X)|\leq d$ for all $X\in\binom{M}{k}$, there is a subset $Q\subset M$ with $|Q|=q$ and $f(X)\not\subset Q$ for any $X\in\binom{Q}{k}$. Estimate $q(m,k,d)$; in particular, determine whether the logarithmic factor in the upper bound $q(m,k,d)\leq c'(m\log m)^t$ is necessary.
rows.reason: Estimation question ('estimate q(m,k,d); can the log factor be removed') in the author's own paper; too open-ended to be a standalone statement target and the same-paper candidate addresses a different aspect.

rows.statement: Is it true that $f(n) \geq n/2 - o(n)$?
rows.reason: Statement 'is f(n) ≥ n/2 - o(n)?' references a paper-specific function f undefined out of context; too paper-dependent to stand alone and no candidates.

rows.statement: If $G$ is a $k$-degenerate graph on $n$ vertices, then $R_{k+2}(G)$ has diameter $O(n^2)$.
rows.reason: Famous named conjecture: the (d+2)-recoloring graph of a d-degenerate graph has diameter O(n^2); none of the candidates match (erdos:619 diameter-augmentation, the two arxiv rows are distinct planar/Kr-free variants).

rows.statement: Any two $k$-colorings of a $d$-degenerate graph on $n$ vertices are equivalent up to $O(n^2)$ trivial Kempe changes (single-vertex recolorings) when $k > d + 2$.
rows.reason: Restates Cereceda's conjecture promoted at cid 142; no candidate is that conjecture (the same-paper row is a distinct polynomial-Kempe-sequence question), so it is kept only as an implication endpoint to avoid double-promotion.

rows.statement: Characterize all trees with minimum ABC-index (ABC-minimal trees), i.e., for each order $n$, describe every tree $T$ of order $n$ that minimizes $\mathrm{ABC}(T) = \sum_{uv \in E(T)} \sqrt{\frac{d_u + d_v - 2}{d_u d_v}}$.
rows.reason: Open-ended 'characterize all minimizers' request over a real-valued mathematical-chemistry index (ABC), not a self-contained proposition and outside the graph-theory formalization scope; no candidate matches.

rows.statement: Every finite metric space $M = (V, d)$ satisfies the de Bruijn-Erd\H{o}s property: $M$ has a universal line, or at least $|V|$ distinct lines.
rows.reason: Famous named conjecture (de Bruijn–Erdős property: every finite metric space has a universal line or at least |V| distinct lines); candidates are only derived helper lemmas, and no corpus row states the metric-space conjecture itself. [verify downgraded: Identical proposition to cid 146 (general metric-space de Bruijn–Erdős property: n points give a universal line or >= n lines); the candidate rows are derived helper lemmas of the graph-metric approach, not the conjecture, so it aliases the promoted cid-146 row.] [reclassified: no exact corpus row exists; promote the main Chen-Chvatal/de Bruijn-Erdos conjecture]

rows.statement: Every metric space on $n$ points, where $n \geq 2$, either has at least $n$ distinct lines or has a universal line.
rows.reason: Same famous conjecture as cid 145; candidate erdos:1066 is unit-distance independent sets (not a match) and arxiv:1606.06011#00 is only a graph-metric F0/pendant reformulation (Conjecture 2.2), so promote the canonical metric-space statement.

rows.statement: There exists a constant $c$ such that for every integer $r \geq 0$, every planar graph $G$, and every $r$-ball hypergraph $H$ of $G$, we have $\tau(H) \leq c \cdot \nu(H)$.
rows.reason: Named third-party conjecture on bounded τ/ν ratio for r-ball hypergraphs of planar graphs (Erdős–Pósa-type); erdos:616 is a different hypergraph-covering problem and no candidate matches.

rows.statement: Every graph definable in NIP structures has a clique or anti-clique of polynomial size.
rows.reason: Model-theoretic Erdős–Hajnal over graphs definable in NIP structures; requires model-theory (NIP definability) infrastructure, out of scope for the graph-theory formalization; no candidates.

rows.statement: There exist functions $f,\,g:\mathbb{N}\to\mathbb{N}$ such that for all integers $k\geq 1$ and $d\geq 1$, for every graph $G$, either $G$ contains $k$ cycles where the distance between each pair of cycles is greater than $d$, or there exists a subset $X$ of vertices of $G$ with $|X|\leq f(k)$ such that $G-B_{G}(X,g(d))$ is a forest.
rows.attributed_to: Chudnovsky and Seymour; also Ahn, Gollin, Huynh, and Kwon
rows.reason: Named coarse Erdős–Pósa conjecture for cycles (k far-apart cycles vs a hitting set whose ball-deletion leaves a forest); the coarse-Menger and erdos candidates are distinct propositions, none matches.

rows.canonical_name: Chudnovsky–Seymour–Trotignon question on subcubic induced subdivisions
rows.statement: Is there a subcubic graph $H$ such that deciding whether an input graph contains $H$ as an induced subdivision ($H$-\textsc{ISC}) is NP-complete?
rows.reason: Named third-party complexity question (existence of a subcubic H with NP-complete H-induced-subdivision containment); self-contained given standard definitions, no candidate matches; complexity flagged via formalizability.

rows.canonical_name: Chung's question on efficient generalized regularity
rows.statement: Can good (i.e., slowly-growing) upper bounds be derived for the number of parts in the partition supplied by the clustering regularity lemma? More generally, is there an efficient $(H,F)$-regularity lemma (one where the number of parts in the partition is a slow-growing function of the regularity parameter $1/\varepsilon$) for some pair $(H,F)$ with $F \subsetneq H$?
rows.reason: Vague/meta research-direction question ('can good bounds be derived... is there an efficient (H,F)-regularity lemma'), not a crisp proposition; no candidate matches.

rows.canonical_name: Chvátal–Tuza question on maximum induced cycles
rows.statement: What is the maximum possible number of induced cycles in a graph on $n$ vertices?
rows.reason: Open determination question ('what is the maximum number of induced cycles') with no conjectured value, so not a standalone formalizable statement (companion to the crisp cid 153); no candidate matches.

rows.canonical_name: Chvátal–Tuza conjecture on maximum odd induced cycles
rows.statement: The maximum possible number of odd induced cycles in a graph on $n$ vertices is $3^{n/3}$.
rows.reason: Named conjecture with a definite extremal value (max number of odd induced cycles in an n-vertex graph is 3^{n/3}); opg:odd_cycles_and_low_oddness is about cubic-graph oddness, no candidate matches.

rows.statement: Determine the computational complexity of computing the circuit diameter of a polytope given in halfspace-description.
rows.reason: 'Determine the complexity of the circuit diameter of a polytope' — polyhedral/LP problem outside the graph-theory scope and an open complexity determination; the candidate complexity rows are graph-specific and do not match.

rows.statement: If $g$ is a positive integer, then $\tilde{c}(g) = c(\lfloor g/2 \rfloor)$.
rows.reason: Statement c̃(g)=c(⌊g/2⌋) references paper-local functions c, c̃ that are undefined in the record; too paper-dependent to stand alone as a formalizable target; no candidates.

rows.canonical_name: Clique minor conjecture for bounded independence number
rows.statement: If $\alpha(G) \leq r$ then $G$ has a clique minor of order $n/r$.
rows.reason: Named third-party conjecture (α(G)≤r implies a clique minor of order n/r), a Hadwiger-type strengthening; arxiv:1509.06563#00 is a χ-boundedness hole statement and opg clique-count rows are unrelated, so promote.

rows.statement: The class of clock-free graphs is clean.
rows.attributed_to: Abrishami, Alecu, Chudnovsky, Hajebi, Spirkl, and Vušković
rows.reason: Statement relies on paper-local predicates ('clock-free', 'clean') defined only in 2311.05719; too paper-dependent to stand alone; the clean-class candidates concern different families and do not match.

rows.attributed_to: Albrechtsen, Huynh, Jacobs, Knappe and Wollan; and independently Georgakopoulos and Papasoglu
rows.reason: Famous named conjecture (general k,d form); all candidate rows are weaker/variant forms (surfaces, c=2, bounded-degree, anti-complete) — none states the general conjecture, so promote the canonical statement.

rows.statement: For all integers $k,c\geq 1$ there exists $\ell>0$ with the following property. Let $G$ be a graph and let $S,T\subseteq V(G)$. Then either there are $k$ paths between $S,T$, pairwise at distance at least $c$; or there is a set $X\subseteq V(G)$ with $|X|\leq k-1$ such that every path between $S,T$ contains a vertex with distance at most $\ell$ from some member of $X$.
rows.attributed_to: Albrechtsen, Huynh, Jacobs, Knappe, and Wollan; and independently Georgakopoulos and Papasoglu
