[verify downgraded: Same proposition (variable d→c) as the promoted general Coarse Menger conjecture (cid 158); paper-local restatement of 2508.14332 whose tracked row is only the c=2 special case, so it serves as an implication endpoint.]

rows.statement: For every proper ideal $I$ there exist $\varepsilon > 0$ and $c > 0$ such that every $\varepsilon$-coherent graph $G \in I$ satisfies $\omega(G)\alpha(G) \geq |G|^c$.
rows.reason: Explicitly a coherent reformulation of Erdős–Hajnal (ω·α ≥ n^c is the standard equivalent form); the canonical EH conjecture is tracked at opg:the_erdos_hajnal_conjecture.

rows.statement: Determine the complexity of finding a path in the vertex colouring reconfiguration graph $R_\ell(G)$ between two given $\ell$-colourings of a graph $G$ of maximum degree $\Delta$, for all pairs $(\ell, \Delta)$.
rows.reason: Open-ended complexity-classification program ('determine the complexity for all pairs (ℓ,Δ)'), not a single formalizable proposition; the coloring-conjecture candidates are unrelated.

rows.canonical_name: Combinatorial algorithm for optimal colouring of perfect graphs
rows.statement: Construct a ``combinatorial'' polynomial-time algorithm that yields an optimal colouring of a perfect graph.
rows.reason: Algorithm-existence problem whose key term 'combinatorial' is informal/undefined; candidates concern MIS/MWIS (not optimal colouring) so no match; not a formalizable theorem target.

rows.statement: Is $W_3$-\textsc{Subdivision} polynomial-time solvable or NP-complete, where $W_3$ is the wheel digraph obtained from the directed 3-cycle $\vec{C}_3$ by adding a dominating centre vertex?
rows.reason: Named third-party complexity question (is digraph W3-subdivision poly-time or NP-complete); self-contained given the digraph definitions, no candidate matches; complexity flagged via formalizability. [verify downgraded: Named but a P-versus-NP-complete dichotomy determination with no definite single proposition to target; better kept as an endpoint for the studying paper's edge than a standalone statement.]

rows.canonical_name: Conlon–Fox–Sudakov problem on 3-uniform hypergraph Ramsey numbers
rows.statement: Show that for any $q \geq 2$, there exists $c_q$ such that $r_3(H; q) \leq 2^{2^{c_q\sqrt{m}}}$ for any 3-uniform hypergraph $H$ with $m$ edges and no isolated vertices.
rows.reason: Named conjecture with self-contained statement (double-exponential q-colour Ramsey bound r_3(H;q) ≤ 2^{2^{c√m}}); the erdos candidates are different hypergraph-coloring/Ramsey problems, no match.

rows.canonical_name: Conlon–Fox–Rödl question on hedgehog Ramsey numbers
rows.reason: Named third-party question on the hedgehog hypergraph Ramsey number r(H_t;2)=t^{2+o(1)}; the tripartite off-diagonal candidate is a different statement, no match.

rows.statement: For every graph $H$ there exist $\varepsilon, \sigma > 0$ such that for every $H$-free graph $G$ on $n > 1$ vertices, and all $c$ with $0 \leq c \leq 1/2$, $V(G)$ contains two disjoint subsets $A, B$ with $|A| \geq \varepsilon c^{\sigma} n$ and $|B| \geq \varepsilon n$, such that the edge-density between $A, B$ is either at most $c$ or at least $1 - c$.
rows.reason: Named Erdős–Hajnal-strengthening conjecture (every H-free graph has large A,B with edge-density ≤c or ≥1−c); the erdos/coarse-menger candidates are unrelated, no match.

rows.statement: For every graph $H$ there exist $c_1, c_2 > 0$ such that for every $H$-free graph $G$ with $|G| \geq 2$, and all $x \in (0, 1/2)$, there exist disjoint $A, B \subseteq V(G)$ with $|A|, |B| \geq x^{c_1}|G|^{c_2}$ such that $B$ is $x$-sparse to $A$ in one of $G, \bar{G}$.
rows.reason: Named CFS sparse-pair conjecture (every H-free graph has large A,B with B x-sparse to A in G or its complement); distinct formulation from cid 166 and no candidate matches, so promote.

rows.canonical_name: Consecutive hole lengths conjecture (bounded clique number)
rows.statement: For all $\kappa, \ell \geq 0$, there exists $c \geq 0$ such that for every graph $G$, if $\omega(G) \leq \kappa$ and $\chi(G) > c$, then $G$ has holes of $\ell$ consecutive lengths.
rows.reason: Studied statement (for all clique bounds and lengths, large chi forces holes of that many consecutive lengths) is the same proposition as the tracked informal conjecture 1509.06563#00 generalizing 1.3 to bounded clique number.

rows.statement: A class $\mathcal{C}$ of graphs is $(\operatorname{tw},\omega)$-bounded if and only if it has bounded $\operatorname{tree-\alpha}$.
rows.reason: Named iff characterization ((tw,omega)-bounded exactly when bounded tree-independence number); the same-paper candidate #00 is only a specific instance, so no corpus row matches and the general statement is a hard-structural boundedness claim over graph classes.

rows.statement: The cat can localise the mouse up to distance $O(\log n)$ on any connected graph of order $n$.
rows.reason: Real conjecture with paper-local numbering (Conjecture 1.1) whose statement is an O(log n) pursuit/localization-game bound needing a nonstandard game primitive; better kept as an implication endpoint than a standalone target.

rows.statement: Given a linear program $\max_{\mathbf{x}\in P}\mathbf{c}^{\intercal}\mathbf{x}$ and a feasible initial basis, can one find a shortest monotone path in the feasible basis graph to an optimal basis in polynomial time?
rows.reason: A polynomial-time algorithmic question about shortest monotone paths in the LP feasible-basis graph; out of scope for statement waves as it requires a computation model.

rows.canonical_name: Dean's problem on FPT pathwidth parameterized by treewidth
rows.statement: Is pathwidth fixed-parameter tractable when parameterized by treewidth? That is, does there exist a computable function $f$ and an algorithm that computes $\mathrm{pw}(G)$ for an $n$-vertex graph $G$ in $f(\mathrm{tw}(G)) \cdot n^{O(1)}$ time?
rows.reason: An FPT/parameterized-complexity question about computing pathwidth in f(tw)*poly time; not a formalizable graph-theoretic proposition without a computation model.

rows.statement: Are tournaments delineated, i.e., is every hereditary subclass of tournaments monadically NIP if and only if it has bounded twin-width?
rows.reason: Question equating monadic NIP with bounded twin-width over hereditary tournament classes; model-theoretic and over infinite classes, out of scope for the corpus.

rows.canonical_name: Demaine et al. token-sliding reachability question (interval/chordal graphs)
rows.statement: Can determining whether two independent sets are in the same connected component of $TS_k(G)$ be decided in polynomial time for interval graphs, and more generally for chordal graphs?
rows.reason: A polynomial-time decidability question for independent-set reconfiguration connectivity; requires a computation model, out of scope for statement waves.

rows.statement: For oriented graphs $L$ and $R$ we have $\operatorname{inv}(L \to R) = \operatorname{inv}(L) + \operatorname{inv}(R)$.
rows.reason: Named self-contained conjecture that inversion number is additive over the dijoin of oriented graphs; no candidate matches and it needs the digraph inversion-number primitive.

rows.statement: If $F$ is a directed forest and $H$ is a transitive tournament, then $\{H, F\}$ is $\vec{\chi}$-finite.
rows.reason: Named conjecture that a directed forest plus a transitive tournament gives a dichromatic-bounded (chi-vec-finite) class; distinct from the tournament backedge-forest characterization candidate, and a boundedness claim over an excluded-substructure class.

rows.canonical_name: Dom-Enum problem (output-polynomial enumeration of minimal dominating sets)
rows.statement: Can the minimal dominating sets of a graph be enumerated in output-polynomial time?
rows.reason: An output-polynomial enumeration-complexity question with no candidate match; requires a computation model, out of scope for statement waves.

rows.statement: Bounded merge-width classes are polynomially $\chi$-bounded.
rows.reason: Named self-contained conjecture that bounded merge-width classes are polynomially chi-bounded; no candidate matches and it is a boundedness statement over infinite width-defined classes.

rows.canonical_name: Drier–Linial conjecture (Hajós number of random lifts of complete graphs)
rows.statement: For $\ell\geq\Omega(n)$ almost all $\ell$-lifts of $K_{n}$ have Hajós number equal to $\Theta(n)$.
rows.reason: Named conjecture on the Hajós number of almost all large lifts of K_n; the sole candidate (Question 5) is unrelated, and the Theta(n)/almost-all statement is asymptotic-probabilistic.

rows.canonical_name: Dujmović et al. question (Thue choice number bounded by pathwidth)
rows.statement: Is the Thue choice number $\pi_l(G)$ bounded by a function of the pathwidth of $G$, for every graph $G$ of bounded pathwidth?
rows.reason: Named self-contained question asking whether the Thue (nonrepetitive) choice number is bounded by a function of pathwidth; no candidate matches and it is a parameter-boundedness statement over all graphs.

rows.statement: There exists a function $f : \mathbb{N} \to \mathbb{N}$ such that every graph $G$ with 2-tree-width at most $k$ has chromatic number at most $f(k)$.
rows.attributed_to: Dujmović, Joret, Morin, Norin, and Wood
rows.reason: Named question (descriptive title) that bounded 2-tree-width implies bounded chromatic number; no candidate matches and it is a boundedness-by-width-parameter statement.

rows.statement: Is there a function $f$ such that every graph $G$ that has two $k$-orthogonal tree-decompositions is $f(k)$-colorable?
rows.attributed_to: Dujmovic, Joret, Morin, Norin, and Wood
rows.reason: Paper-local numbered variant (Problem 3.3) closely overlapping the 2-tree-width colouring question by the same authors; kept as an implication endpoint per the D7 default for paper-local numbered near-duplicates.

rows.canonical_name: Dvořák conjecture (cheap balanced separators with few outliers for bounded-expansion graphs)
rows.statement: For every polynomial $p$, there exists a function $q : \mathbb{N} \to \mathbb{N}$ such that the following holds. Let $G$ be a graph with expansion bounded by $p$ and let $\rho : V(G) \to \mathbb{R}^+_0$ be a cost assignment for $G$. For every integer $t \geq 1$, $G$ has a balanced separator which is $(\rho/t)$-cheap with $q(t)$ outliers.
rows.reason: Named self-contained conjecture on cheap balanced separators with boundedly many outliers for graphs of bounded expansion; no candidate matches and it quantifies over polynomial-expansion classes.

rows.canonical_name: Dvořák–Mnich conjecture (fractional chromatic number of planar girth-5 graphs)
rows.statement: There exists a real number $c < 3$ such that every planar graph of girth at least five has fractional chromatic number at most $c$.
rows.reason: Named conjecture that planar girth-at-least-5 graphs have fractional chromatic number bounded below 3; candidate 1302.2158#00 is a distinct precolored-cycle list-coloring statement, so it is promoted (same conjecture as cid 186).

rows.canonical_name: Dvořák–Kráľ–Nejedlý–Škrekovski conjecture (square of planar girth-5 graphs)
rows.statement: There exists $\Delta_0$ such that if $G$ is a planar graph with girth at least 5 and $\Delta(G) \geq \Delta_0$ then $\chi(G^2) \leq \Delta(G) + 2$.
rows.reason: Named conjecture that high-maximum-degree planar girth-5 graphs satisfy chi(G^2) <= Delta+2; candidates (separator dichotomy 2309.07905, unit-distance/girth Erdős problems) do not match.

rows.canonical_name: Dvořák–Mnich conjecture (fractional chromatic number of planar girth-5 graphs)
rows.statement: For some $\varepsilon > 0$, planar graphs of girth at least five have fractional chromatic number at most $3 - \varepsilon$.

rows.statement: Does there exist $\varepsilon > 0$ such that every planar graph $G$ and assignment $L$ of lists of size (a) five in general, (b) four if $G$ is triangle-free, (c) three if $G$ has girth at least five is (weighted) $\varepsilon$-flexible?
rows.reason: Named conjecture on epsilon-flexibility of planar graphs with list sizes 5/4/3 by girth; candidate 1612.08698#00 is a different d-degenerate flexibility problem, and formalizing needs the flexibility primitive.

rows.statement: For all integers $k$ and $\ell$ with $0 < \ell < \binom{k}{2}$, we have $\mathrm{ind}(k,\ell) \leq \frac{1}{e} + o_k(1)$.

rows.statement: Is the following true? For all integers $t,c\geq 1$, there exists $d\geq 1$, such that if a graph $G$ satisfies $\chi(G)\geq d$ and has no clique with $t$ vertices, then there are subsets $A,B\subseteq V(G)$ with $\chi(G[A]),\chi(G[B])\geq c$, such that there are no edges between $A$ and $B$.

rows.statement: For every connected graph $G$ of order $n$, $$\min\{\mathcal{E}_{2}^{+}(G),\mathcal{E}_{2}^{-}(G)\}\geq n-1.$$
rows.reason: Named conjecture min{E2+,E2-} >= n-1 for connected graphs; same-paper candidate #00 (Conjecture 2, E-_p monotonicity) is a different statement, and it needs eigenvalue squared-sum primitives (a spectral variant of cid 191).

rows.statement: For every connected graph $G$ of order $n$, we have $$\min\{s^{+}(G),s^{-}(G)\}\geq n-1.$$
rows.reason: Named conjecture min{s+(G),s-(G)} >= n-1 for connected graphs; candidate 2506.07264#04 (Conjecture 9.4, s+ >= n for omega>=3) is a distinct statement, and it needs positive/negative squared-eigenvalue-sum primitives.

rows.statement: Fix $\delta \geq 1$ and any graph $H$. Let $G$ be a graph on $n$ vertices with minimum degree at least $\delta$. There exists a constant $c(\delta, H)$ such that, for $n \geq c(\delta, H)$, we have $$\hom(G, H) \leq \max\left\{\hom(K_{\delta+1}, H)^{\frac{n}{\delta+1}},\, \hom(K_{\delta,\delta}, H)^{\frac{n}{2\delta}},\, \hom(K_{\delta,n-\delta}, H)\right\}.$$
rows.reason: Named third-party (Engbers) conjecture with a self-contained upper bound on hom(G,H) for min-degree-delta graphs; no candidate matches (candidates are about induced subgraphs/edges), so it enters the waves.

rows.canonical_name: Engbers' Question on H-Colourings of Connected Graphs
rows.statement: For every $\delta \geq 3$ and every graph $H$, does $K_{\delta,n-\delta}$ uniquely maximise $\hom(G, H)$ among all connected graphs $G$ on $n$ vertices with minimum degree at least $\delta$, for $n$ sufficiently large?
rows.reason: A named but open uniqueness/extremal question that is a variant/refinement of Engbers' main conjecture (cid 192); no candidate matches, best kept as an implication endpoint for paper 1611.02911.

rows.canonical_name: Engbers' Question on Maximising H-Colourings Without Connectivity
rows.statement: For a fixed graph $H$ and $\delta \geq 1$, which graph $G$ on $n$ vertices with minimum degree at least $\delta$ maximises $\hom(G, H)$ for $n$ sufficiently large (without assuming connectivity of $G$)?
rows.reason: Open-ended 'which graph maximises hom(G,H)' determination with no proposed extremiser; not a crisp proposition, so studies-backlog.

rows.canonical_name: Engbers' Question on Maximising Proper q-Colourings of Connected Graphs
rows.statement: For fixed $\delta \geq 3$ and $q > 2$, does $K_{\delta,n-\delta}$ uniquely maximise the number of proper $q$-colourings among all connected graphs on $n$ vertices with minimum degree at least $\delta$, for $n$ sufficiently large?
rows.reason: The proper q-colouring special case of the cid 193 Engbers question; an auxiliary variant serving as an endpoint, not a standalone target.

rows.canonical_name: Eppstein's Open Problem 1 (Linear Subgraph Characterisation by Genus)
rows.statement: Characterise the subgraphs occurring $O(n)$ times in graphs of given genus.
rows.reason: Open-ended 'characterise the subgraphs occurring O(n) times' problem — a characterisation task, not a formalizable proposition; candidates all unrelated.

rows.canonical_name: Eppstein's Open Problem 2 (Nonlinear Subgraph Characterisation by Genus)
rows.statement: Characterise the subgraphs occurring a number of times which is a nonlinear function of $n$ in graphs of given genus.
rows.reason: Companion characterisation problem (nonlinear occurrence count); open-ended and non-propositional, so studies-backlog.

rows.statement: Let $k\geq 4$. For every $n\in\mathbb{N}$ let $f_{k}(n)$ denote the largest integer $r\geq 1$ such that there exists a $(k,r)$-graph on $n$ vertices ($f_{k}(n):=0$ if no such graph exists for any $r\geq 1$). Is it true that $f_{k}(n)\rightarrow\infty$ for $n\rightarrow\infty$?
rows.reason: Named Erdős problem (does f_k(n) -> infinity for (k,r)-graphs) studied by two papers; distinct from candidate erdos:667, requires recovering the (k,r)-graph primitive but is a genuine standalone open problem. [verify downgraded: Paper-local Problem 1.1/1.2 whose statement depends on the undefined (k,r)-graph notion; not self-contained, but a real Erdős problem serving as endpoint for the two studying papers.]

rows.statement: Is it true that for every $r\in\mathbb{N}$ there exists a number $F(r)$ such that every graph of chromatic number at least $F(r)$ contains $r$ edge-disjoint cycles on the same vertex set?
rows.reason: Exact restatement of erdos:641: a function f with chromatic number >= f(k) forcing k edge-disjoint cycles on the same vertex set.

rows.canonical_name: Erdős–Moser Problem on the Acyclic Number of Oriented Graphs
rows.statement: What is the least integer $\operatorname{TT}(k)$ such that every oriented graph on $\operatorname{TT}(k)$ vertices has acyclic number at least $k$ ? What is $\vec{\alpha}(n)=\min\{\vec{\alpha}(D)\mid D\text{ is an oriented graph of order $n$}\}$ ?
rows.reason: Pure determination ('what is TT(k)? what is the minimum acyclic number?') with no conjectured value; no candidate matches, so studies-backlog.

rows.statement: If $\{a_1, \ldots, a_n\}$ is a set of positive integers with $a_1 < \cdots < a_n$ and all subset sums distinct, then $a_n \geq c \cdot 2^n$ for some constant $c > 0$.
rows.reason: A crisp but additive-number-theory conjecture (a_n >= c*2^n) out of scope for the graph-theory corpus; no candidate matches.

rows.canonical_name: Erdős' Question on Locally Ramsey Graphs (m_G(log n) = (log n)^3 case)
rows.statement: Does there exist an $n$-vertex graph $G$ with $m_G(\log n) = (\log n)^3$?
rows.reason: Same paper (2004.04718) and function as cid 212; asks the g(n)=(log n)^3 instance that erdos:805 explicitly poses (every (log n)^3-subset contains a clique and an independent set of size log n) — a verified restatement even though the fuzzy matcher missed 805.

rows.statement: Determine, up to a constant factor, the size Ramsey number $\hat{r}(S_n^{(k)})$ of the starburst graph $S_n^{(k)}$ (obtained from $K_k$ by adding $n$ pendant edges to every vertex of $K_k$), as a function of both $k$ and $n$.
rows.reason: Cluster groups three 'determine up to a constant factor' size-Ramsey problems (starburst/complete-bipartite/book); asymptotic determinations without a single conjectured value, so studies-backlog.

rows.canonical_name: Erdős–Gimbel Problems on Cochromatic Number of Random Graphs
rows.statement: For every $n\in\mathbb{N}$, let $G_{n}\sim G(n,\frac{1}{2})$ be an Erdős-Rényi random graph on $n$ vertices. Is it true that, almost surely, for $n\rightarrow\infty$?
rows.reason: Representative statement is fragmentary/truncated ('Is it true that, almost surely, for n -> infinity?') and bundles two problems; cannot cleanly verify against erdos:625, so studies-backlog.

rows.statement: Every graph $G$ with $\omega(G)<5$ and $\zeta(G)>3$ satisfies $\chi(G)\leq\zeta(G)+2$.
rows.reason: Identical proposition to erdos:762: omega(G)<5 (no K_5) and zeta(G)>3 (zeta>=4) imply chi(G) <= zeta(G)+2.

rows.statement: Determine the exact value of the extremal function $f$ introduced by Erdős and Graham giving the maximum size of a subset of $[n]$ that avoids a given subset sum.
rows.reason: Determination of an additive-number-theory extremal function (max subset of [n] avoiding a subset sum); out of scope and value-determination, so studies-backlog.

rows.canonical_name: Erdős–Graham–Montgomery–Rothschild–Spencer–Straus Question on ℓ₂ vs Large Sets
rows.statement: For every natural number $n$, is there a set $K \subset E^1$ and a red/blue-coloring of $E^n$ containing no red copy of $\ell_2$ and no blue copy of $K$? More generally, for every natural number $d$, is there a natural number $n$ depending only on $d$ such that $E^n \not\to (\ell_2, K)$ for every $K \subset E^d$?
rows.attributed_to: Erdős, Graham, Montgomery, Rothschild, Spencer and Straus
rows.reason: Euclidean Ramsey question over E^n (geometric congruent copies); out of scope for the combinatorial graph-theory corpus, distinct from the tracked sibling arxiv:1705.02166#01.

rows.statement: For every natural number $n$, does there exist a natural number $m$ depending only on $n$ such that for every set $K \subset E^n$ of size $m$ there is a two-coloring of $E^n$ with no monochromatic copy of $K$?
rows.attributed_to: Erdős, Graham, Montgomery, Rothschild, Spencer and Straus
rows.reason: Companion Euclidean Ramsey problem (two-colouring E^n avoiding a monochromatic copy of any size-m configuration); geometric and out of scope, so studies-backlog.

rows.statement: For every hereditary property $\mathcal{P}$ which is not satisfied by all graphs, there exists a constant $\varepsilon(\mathcal{P}) > 0$ such that every graph of $n$ vertices with property $\mathcal{P}$ has a clique or an independent set of size at least $n^{\varepsilon(\mathcal{P})}$.
rows.reason: The canonical Erdős–Hajnal conjecture (polynomial clique-or-independent-set in hereditary classes); matches the already-formalized OPG row.

rows.statement: For every tournament $H$ there exists $\tau > 0$ such that every $H$-free tournament $G$ has a transitive subtournament with at least $|G|^{\tau}$ vertices.
rows.reason: Distinct named proposition (H-free tournaments have a transitive subtournament of size |G|^tau); equivalent-but-not-identical to the graph version and not among the candidates (only multicolour EH), so promote.

rows.statement: Determine the minimum independence number of a 3-uniform hypergraph on $N$ vertices in which there are at most two edges among any four vertices; equivalently, determine $r(K_4^{(3)}\setminus e,\, K_n^{(3)})$.
rows.reason: Determination of a specific 3-uniform hypergraph Ramsey number r(K_4^(3)-e, K_n^(3)) with no conjectured formula; no candidate matches, so studies-backlog.

rows.canonical_name: Erdős–Hajnal Problem on Locally Ramsey Graphs
rows.statement: Over all $n$-vertex graphs $G$, what is the smallest possible value of $m$ for which any $m$ vertices of $G$ contain both a clique and an independent set of size $\log n$?
rows.reason: Same problem as erdos:805: smallest m so every m-subset contains both a clique and an independent set of size log n (the 'for which g(n)' local Ramsey question).

rows.statement: Let $p(m,k,l)$ be the maximum $p$ such that for every mapping $f:\binom{M}{k}\to\binom{M}{l}$ with $|M|=m$ and $X\cap f(X)=\emptyset$ for all $X\in\binom{M}{k}$, there is a subset $P\subset M$ with $|P|=p$ and $f(X)\cap P=\emptyset$ for all $X\in\binom{P}{k}$. Determine the exact dependence of $p(m,k,l)$ on $m$.
rows.reason: Determination of the exact dependence of the free-set function p(m,k,l) on m; a value-determination with a bespoke primitive, so studies-backlog.

rows.statement: Determine $f(n,k,r)$, the minimum number of edges in an $r$-uniform hypergraph $H$ with vertex set $[n] = \{1,\ldots,n\}$ such that every $k$-set in $[n]$ is covered in $H$ (where a set $A \subset X$ is covered if there exists $u \notin A$ such that $B \cup \{u\} \in H$ for every $B \in A^{(r-1)}$).
rows.reason: Determination of the covering function f(n,k,r) for r-uniform hypergraphs; no conjectured value and distinct from candidate erdos:616, so studies-backlog.

rows.statement: If $G$ is a graph, then $\chi'_s(G) \leq 1.25\,\Delta(G)^2$.
rows.reason: Famous named conjecture chi'_s(G) <= 1.25*Delta(G)^2; a crisp inequality not matched by any candidate, requiring the strong-edge-colouring primitive. [verify downgraded: χ'_s(G) ≤ 1.25Δ² is exactly the Erdős–Nešetřil bound already tracked as the OPG strong edge colouring conjecture (also erdos:149); same proposition despite not being in the matcher's candidate list.]

rows.statement: Any subset $A$ of $\mathbb{N}$ with divergent harmonic sum, i.e., $\sum_{a \in A} 1/a = \infty$, must contain arbitrarily long arithmetic progressions.
rows.reason: Additive number theory (divergent-reciprocal-sum sets contain long APs); the true erdosproblems home #3 is not tracked and no candidate matches; out of scope for the graph-theory corpus.

rows.canonical_name: Erdős's Question on the Subset-Sum Density Constant c₂
rows.statement: What is the largest real number $c_2$ such that every two-coloring of the positive integers has a color class for which the upper logarithmic density of its set of subset sums is at least $c_2$? More generally, what is $c_r := \min_{\mathbb{N}=A_1\sqcup\cdots\sqcup A_r} \max_{i\in[r]} \bar{d}_{\ell}(\Sigma(A_i))$?
rows.reason: Additive number theory about logarithmic density of subset sums under partitions of ℕ; no candidate matches and out of scope for a graph-theory corpus.
(1/(2√2)+o(1))n^{3/2} equals (n/2)^{3/2}, so this is exactly erdos:573 'ex(n;{C₃,C₄}) ~ (n/2)^{3/2}' (missed by the matcher).

rows.canonical_name: Erdős's Question on the (3,k) Turán Exponent f_{3,k}
rows.statement: For each fixed integer $k > 3$, is the limit $f_{3,k} := \lim_{n\to\infty} \frac{\log f_{3,k}(n)}{\log n}$ always strictly less than $2$?
rows.reason: Statement relies on paper-local notation f_{3,k}(n) that is never defined in the record, so it is not self-contained; no candidate matches.

rows.statement: There is a constant $C = C(r)$ depending only on $r$ such that $f_r(k) \leq C^k$.
rows.reason: Famous named set-system conjecture (f_r(k) ≤ C(r)^k); only a near-sunflower analogue (arxiv:2010.05992#00) is tracked, which is a different proposition, so promote.

rows.canonical_name: Erdős–Rothschild Problem on Edges in Many Triangles (h(n,c))
rows.statement: Determine or estimate $h(n,c)$, the maximum number such that any $n$-vertex graph with at least $cn^2$ edges in which every edge is contained in a triangle must contain an edge lying in at least $h(n,c)$ triangles.
rows.reason: h(n,c) is exactly f_c(n) of erdos:80: max m such that every n-vertex graph with ≥cn² edges (each edge in a triangle) has an edge in ≥m triangles (a book of size m).

rows.statement: For all positive integers $a$, $b$, and $m$, every $(a:b)$-choosable graph is also $(am:bm)$-choosable.
rows.reason: Exact match with erdos:632: every (a,b)-choosable graph is (am,bm)-choosable for all m≥1.

rows.canonical_name: Erdős's Problem on Vertex-Critical Graphs Far from Edge-Criticality
rows.statement: Is it true that for every $k \geq 4$ and $r$ there is a vertex-critical $k$-chromatic graph which remains $k$-chromatic if any $r$ of its edges are omitted?
rows.reason: Same as erdos:944: for k≥4, r, a k-chromatic graph with every vertex critical but no critical edge-set of size ≤r (remains k-chromatic after deleting any r edges).

rows.statement: For each $n$, is there a (least) positive integer $f(n)$ so that every finite tournament whose edges are coloured with $n$ colours contains a set $S$ of $f(n)$ vertices with the property that for every vertex $u$ not in $S$ there is a monochromatic path from $u$ to a vertex of $S$?
rows.reason: The tournament/complete-digraph case of the arc-colored monochromatic-reachability conjecture (stable sets collapse to single vertices in a tournament); the OPG row is the tracked ESSW home. Given candidates (erdos:599 etc.) do not match.

rows.statement: For every $k$, there exists an integer $f(k)$ such that if $T$ is a complete multidigraph whose arcs are the union of $k$ quasi-orders, then $\gamma(T) \leq f(k)$.
rows.reason: Standard known-equivalent quasi-order reformulation (arcs = union of k quasi-orders, γ(T)≤f(k)) of the same ESSW arc-colored monochromatic-reachability conjecture already tracked at the OPG row.

rows.statement: $\lim_{m \to \infty} (r(3; m))^{1/m} < \infty$
rows.reason: r(3;m)^{1/m} bounded is the conjectural content of erdos:183 'determine lim_{k→∞} R(3;k)^{1/k}' — same multicolour-triangle-Ramsey growth object (erdos:77 is 2-colour diagonal, different).

rows.canonical_name: Erdős–Shelah Problem on Generalized Ramsey Thresholds
rows.statement: For positive integers $p \geq 3$ and $2 \leq q \leq \binom{p}{2}$, determine how the minimum number of colors $m$ needed so that any $m$-coloring of $K_n$ forces every $p$ vertices to span at least $q$ distinct colors depends on $n$, $p$, and $q$.
rows.reason: Open determination problem ('determine how the m-colour threshold forcing q colours on every p-set depends on n,p,q'), not a crisp yes/no proposition; no candidate matches.

rows.statement: For every finite set $\mathcal{F}$ of graphs, $\mathrm{ex}(n,\mathcal{F}) \geq c \cdot \min_{F\in\mathcal{F}} \mathrm{ex}(n,F)$ for some constant $c>0$.
rows.reason: ex(n,F) ≥ c·min_{F} ex(n,F) is equivalent to 'there exists G∈F with ex(n;G) = Θ(ex(n;F))' = erdos:180 (erdos:575 is the bipartite-restricted variant).

rows.statement: Every finite set of integers $A$ having large enough cardinality, satisfies $\max(|A+A|, |AA|) \geq |A|^{2-\varepsilon}$, where $\varepsilon \to 0$ as $|A| \to \infty$.
rows.reason: Additive combinatorics (max(|A+A|,|AA|) ≥ |A|^{2-o(1)}); candidate erdos:808 is the distinct graph-restricted variant; out of scope for the graph-theory corpus.

rows.statement: For two $n$-element sets of integers $A = \{a_1, \ldots, a_n\}$ and $B = \{b_1, \ldots, b_n\}$ with $S = \{a_i + b_i \mid 1 \leq i \leq n\}$ and $P = \{a_i \cdot b_i \mid 1 \leq i \leq n\}$, $$|P| + |S| = \Omega(n^{1/2+c})$$ for some constant $c > 0$.
rows.reason: Additive-combinatorics matching variant (|P|+|S| = Ω(n^{1/2+c}) for pointwise sums/products); no candidate matches; out of scope.

rows.statement: Let $G$ be a graph. If $|E(G)| > \frac{1}{2}(k-2)|V(G)|$, then $G$ contains every tree of order $k$.
rows.reason: '|E(G)| > (k-2)/2·|V(G)| forces every tree of order k' is erdos:548 reparametrized (tree order k ↔ k+1): ≥(k-1)/2·n+1 edges force every tree on k+1 vertices.

rows.canonical_name: Esperet–Joret Question on Clustered Colouring of Triangle-Free Bounded-Degree Graphs on Surfaces
rows.statement: Consider a surface $\Sigma$ and a positive integer $\Delta$. Is it true that the class of triangle-free graphs of maximum degree $\Delta$ than can be drawn on $\Sigma$ has clustered chromatic number at most two?
rows.reason: Named third-party (Esperet–Joret) self-contained yes/no question on clustered chromatic number ≤2 for triangle-free bounded-degree graphs embeddable on a surface; no candidate matches (the bounded-degree χ candidate is about ordinary χ).

rows.canonical_name: Esperet–Joret Problem on Boxicity and Euler Genus
rows.statement: Determine the maximum boxicity of a graph with Euler genus $g$. It is known that every graph with Euler genus $g$ has boxicity $O(\sqrt{g\log g})$ and that there exist graphs of Euler genus $g$ with boxicity $\Omega(\sqrt{g\log g})$.
rows.reason: Open determination problem asking for the exact max boxicity in terms of Euler genus, with matching bounds already known up to constants (Θ(√(g log g))); not a crisp promotable conjecture; no candidate matches.

rows.canonical_name: Esperet–Ochem Conjecture 3 (χ⋆ of Surface Graph Classes)
rows.statement: For every surface $\Sigma$, $\chi^\star(\mathcal{G}(\Sigma)) = 4$.
rows.reason: Paper-local numbered conjecture ('Conjecture 3') using undefined notation χ⋆(G(Σ))=4; not self-contained, valuable only as an edge endpoint for the studying paper.

rows.canonical_name: Esperet–Ochem Conjecture 7 (col⋆ of Surface Graph Classes)
rows.statement: For every surface $\Sigma$, $\mathrm{col}^\star(\mathcal{G}_5(\Sigma)) = 2$.
rows.reason: Paper-local numbered conjecture ('Conjecture 7') using undefined notation col⋆(G₅(Σ))=2; not self-contained (candidate arxiv:1710.02727#00 'Conjecture 4' is a different statement); edge endpoint only.

rows.statement: There is a polynomial $f_H$ such that $\chi(G) \leq f_H(\omega(G))$ for every $H$-free graph $G$.
rows.reason: 'Esperet's Conjecture 1.2' (polynomial χ(G)≤f_H(ω(G)) on H-free classes) is a phrasing of Esperet's polynomial-χ-boundedness conjecture tracked at arxiv:1910.00697#00 ('χ-bounded but not polynomially χ-bounded?').

rows.statement: For every $\chi$-bounded class $\mathcal{C}$, can the bounding function $f$ (satisfying $\chi(G)\leq f(\omega(G))$ for every induced subgraph $G$ of a member of $\mathcal{C}$) always be chosen to be a polynomial?
rows.reason: 'Can the bounding function of every χ-bounded class be chosen polynomial?' is precisely the proposition of arxiv:1910.00697#00 (Esperet's conjecture, stated as the existence question of a counterexample).

rows.statement: For every $\chi$-bounded class $\mathcal{C}$, the bounding function $f$ (satisfying $\chi(G) \leq f(\omega(G))$ for all $G \in \mathcal{C}$) can be chosen to be a polynomial.
rows.reason: Exact restatement (every χ-bounded class is polynomially χ-bounded) of Esperet's conjecture tracked at arxiv:1910.00697#00.

rows.canonical_name: Explicit Construction of Short Minimal Codes
rows.statement: Give an explicit construction of $\mathbb{F}_q$-linear minimal codes of length $n$ and dimension $k$ such that $n = O(qk)$, i.e., $n$ is at most linear in $k$ for fixed $q$.

rows.reason: Coding-theory construction problem (F_q-linear minimal codes with n=O(qk)); not a named graph-theory conjecture and out of scope for the graph corpus; no candidate matches.

rows.statement: $\chi(\mathcal{A}_{n})\in O(\log n)$.
