rows.statement: There exists some $n\geq 3$ for which $p_{\mathrm{max}}(\Z^{n})=4-2\sqrt{3}$.
rows.reason: Statement hinges on the undefined percolation parameter p_max(Z^n); paper-specific and candidates (derived limits, Ramsey) are unrelated.

rows.statement: $p_{\mathrm{giant}}=\tfrac{1}{2}$, i.e., for all $p>\tfrac{1}{2}$, under every model in $\mathcal{D}_{\geq p}(Q_{n})$ the hypercube contains a component of size at least a constant fraction of all vertices with high probability.
rows.reason: Hypercube-percolation giant-component threshold p_giant=1/2 requires a full random model definition; candidate rows are unrelated (T-joins, VC-dimension).

rows.statement: The arrangement graph of every simple arrangement of great-circles on the sphere is 3-colorable.
rows.reason: Verified identical to the OPG row: every arrangement graph of great circles is 3-colourable.

rows.statement: Does Theorem 3 still hold with $q$ parts instead of $q+1$ parts? That is, does almost every $G_{n,1/2}$ have a vertex partition into $q$ sets $A_1, \ldots, A_q$ such that in each induced subgraph $G[A_i]$ all degrees are $x \bmod q$?
rows.reason: Paper-local question extending its own Theorem 3 about degree-mod-q partitions of G_{n,1/2}; value is as an implication endpoint, no candidate matches.

rows.statement: Does $U(n) = \binom{n}{2}$ for all $n$?
rows.reason: Statement depends entirely on the undefined quantity U(n); not self-contained and no candidates.

rows.canonical_name: Flandrin et al. Conjecture (neighbour-sum-distinguishing edge colouring)
rows.statement: If $G$ is a connected graph of order at least three different from the cycle $C_5$, then $\chi'_{\Sigma}(G) \leq \Delta(G) + 2$.
rows.reason: Named self-contained conjecture that chi'_Sigma(G) ≤ Delta(G)+2 for connected G of order ≥3 other than C5; not covered by any candidate.

rows.canonical_name: Folklore Conjecture (high-girth regular graphs of near-optimal order)
rows.statement: For any positive integers $g$ and $d$, there exists a $d$-regular graph $G$ of girth at least $g$, and order $\Theta\!\left((d-1)^{\left(\frac{1}{2}+o(1)\right)g}\right)$.
rows.attributed_to: Folklore (Bollobás and Szemerédi for the cubic case)
rows.reason: Folklore (not a named third-party attribution), asymptotic Theta bound, and the cluster merges two distinct statements (regular high-girth graphs and a linear-hypergraph bound).

rows.canonical_name: Fomin–Thilikos Open Problem (approximating pathwidth by treewidth)
rows.statement: Is there a polynomial-time algorithm that approximates the pathwidth of an $n$-vertex graph $G$ to within a factor $g(\mathrm{tw}(G))$ for some function $g$, with running time polynomial in $n$?
rows.reason: Algorithmic open problem about existence of a polynomial-time g(tw)-approximation; out of scope for a combinatorial statement wave.

rows.statement: A graph $A$ is forcing if and only if it is bipartite and contains a cycle.
rows.reason: Named conjecture characterizing forcing graphs (bipartite and containing a cycle); candidate opg rows are minor-forcing (unrelated) and the same paper's arxiv:2210.16971#01 is the distinct directed version.

rows.canonical_name: Fox's Pure-Pair Conjecture for Perfect Graphs
rows.statement: For every sufficiently large positive integer $n$ and every $n$-vertex perfect graph $G$, there is a pure pair $A, B$ in $G$ with $|A|, |B| \geq n^{1-o(1)}$.
rows.reason: Named self-contained conjecture on n^{1-o(1)}-size pure pairs in perfect graphs; distinct from candidate pure-pair rows (blockade/congestion/H-free) which do not concern perfect graphs.

rows.statement: For every $d>0$, there exists $c>0$ such that every $n$-vertex graph of VC-dimension at most $d$ has a clique or stable set of size at least $n^{c}$.
rows.reason: Verified identical to arxiv:1912.02342#00: bounded VC-dimension graphs contain a polynomial-size clique or independent set.

rows.statement: Is $m_{3}(n)=\Omega\left(\frac{n}{\log n}\right)$? More generally, can the lower bound $m_{2^{k}}(n)\geq\frac{c^{k}n}{(\log n)^{2k-1}}$ (obtained by iterating the $k=2$ case) be improved?
rows.reason: Statement depends on an undefined paper-specific function m_k(n); not self-contained and candidates are unrelated.

rows.statement: For every graph $H$ there exists $c>0$ such that for every $\varepsilon$ with $0<\varepsilon\leq 1/2$ and every $H$-free graph $G$, there exists $X\subseteq V(G)$ with $|X|\geq\varepsilon^{c}|G|$ such that one of $G[X],\overline{G}[X]$ has edge-density at most $\varepsilon$.
rows.reason: The epsilon^c-size epsilon-homogeneous-set form for H-free graphs is the polynomial Rödl property, verified same as arxiv:1803.03588#00 (Fox–Sudakov, implies Erdős–Hajnal).

rows.statement: In Rödl's theorem (Theorem 1.4), the parameter $\delta$ can be taken to be a polynomial function of $x$ (for every fixed $H$).
rows.reason: Directly matches candidate arxiv:1803.03588#00: delta in Rödl's theorem can be taken polynomial in the density parameter.

rows.statement: Every hereditary family of graphs that is not the family of all graphs satisfies the polynomial Rödl property.
rows.reason: Hereditary-family polynomial Rödl property is the same Fox–Sudakov conjecture as arxiv:1803.03588#00; its degree-bounding candidates concern a different invariant.

rows.canonical_name: Fractional Majority Colouring Weight Question (Kreutzer–Oum–Seymour–van der Zypen–Wood)
rows.statement: What is the smallest constant $K$ such that every digraph admits a fractional majority coloring with total weight at most $K$?
rows.attributed_to: Kreutzer, Oum, Seymour, van der Zypen and Wood
rows.reason: Auxiliary value-determination question (smallest total-weight constant K) refining majority colouring; a value question rather than a standalone proposition, no candidate matches.

rows.canonical_name: Frankl's Question on Counting Maximum VC Classes
rows.statement: How many classes of a given VC dimension $d$ are there? How many maximum classes of a given VC dimension $d$ are there?
rows.reason: Open-ended enumeration question (how many classes / maximum classes of VC dimension d) rather than a crisp formalizable proposition.

rows.canonical_name: Fraughnaugh–Locke Conjectures (independence in triangle-free subcubic graphs)
rows.statement: If $G$ is a triangle-free subcubic $n$-vertex graph containing none of $F_{11}$, $F^{(1)}_{14}$, $F^{(2)}_{14}$, $F^{(1)}_{19}$, $F^{(2)}_{19}$, $F_{22}$ as a subgraph, then $\alpha(G) \geq \frac{3}{8} n$.
rows.reason: Paper-local numbered conjectures (Conj 3/5) whose statement references paper-specific forbidden subgraphs F_11, F_14, etc.; value is as an edge endpoint.

rows.statement: For every $\alpha \in (0, 1/2)$ there exists $k$ and $\tau > 0$ such that if $G$ is a graph on $n$ vertices with maximum independent set of size $\alpha n$, then there exist pairwise disjoint subsets of vertices $A_1, A_2, \ldots, A_r$ in $G$ such that (1) $|A_i| = k$ for all $i$, (2) $|\bigcup_{i=1}^r A_i| \geq \tau n$, (3) every maximum independent set in $G$ intersects every set $A_i$.
rows.reason: Paper-local numbered (Conj 1.1/1.6) cluster with a truncated, merged statement; per default posture on paper-local numbered records choose edge-anchor.

rows.canonical_name: Friedman–Krivelevich Open Problem (cycle lengths mod k in expanders)
rows.statement: Find conditions on $\alpha > 0$ guaranteeing that sufficiently large $\alpha$-expanding graphs contain cycles of length congruent to a given residue $r$ modulo $k$; in particular, determine whether cycles of length $0 \pmod{k}$ can be guaranteed in $\alpha$-expanding graphs.
rows.reason: Open-ended research direction ('find conditions on alpha') rather than a definite proposition; candidates are unrelated open problems.

rows.canonical_name: Fujiwara–Papasoglu Question (asymptotic dimension of planar graphs)
rows.statement: Does the class of planar graphs have asymptotic dimension at most $2$?
rows.reason: Named self-contained question — does the class of planar graphs have asymptotic dimension at most 2 — with no matching candidate row.

rows.statement: Every connected graph on $n$ vertices can be decomposed into $\lceil \frac{n}{2} \rceil$ paths.
rows.reason: Verified same as OPG row: every connected n-vertex graph decomposes into ceil(n/2) paths.

rows.statement: Every connected graph on $n$ vertices can be decomposed into $\lceil \frac{n}{2} \rceil$ paths.
rows.reason: Identical statement to the OPG connected-graph-into-paths row (ceil(n/2) paths).

rows.statement: Let $G$ be a connected graph and let $r,v\in V(G)$. If $w\in\mathbb{R}_{\geq 0}^{V(G)}$ is edge-reachable from $\mathds{1}_{\{r\}}$, must we have $w(v)\leq\frac{1}{d_{G}(r,v)+1}$?
rows.reason: Named question but its statement hinges on the paper-local 'edge-reachable weight vector' process (needs-primitive), too paper-dependent to stand alone; no candidate matches.

rows.statement: There exist functions $f,g$ such that, for all $k,d\in\mathbb{N}$, every graph $G$ and vertex set $\mathcal{A}\subseteq V(G)$ contains one of the following: $k$ $\mathcal{A}$-paths $P_{1},\dots,P_{k}$ such that $\operatorname{dist}_{G}(P_{i},P_{j})\geqslant d$ for all distinct $i,j\in[k]$; or a set $Z\subseteq V(G)$ with $|Z|\leqslant f(k)$ such that $G-N_{G}[Z,g(d)]$ has no $\mathcal{A}$-path.
rows.reason: Named Geelen conjecture (coarse min-max for pairwise-distant A-paths); candidates are Coarse Menger / exact A-B Menger (erdos:599), a distinct dual, so not covered.

rows.statement: There exists a general induced arithmetic removal lemma.
rows.reason: Representative statement is a content-free placeholder ('there exists a general induced arithmetic removal lemma'), too fragmentary to promote or confidently alias.

rows.statement: For every $k$, there exists an integer $f(k)$ such that if $D$ is a multidigraph whose arcs are the union of $k$ quasi-orders, then $D$ has a dominating set which is the union of $f(k)$ stable sets.
rows.reason: The quasi-order/dominating-set form is the standard equivalent of the OPG monochromatic-reachability form (transitive closure of each color class = a quasi-order; monochromatic-path domination = quasi-order-arc domination).

rows.statement: For every graph $H$ and all $c\geq 0$, there exists $L,C\geq 0$, such that if a graph $G$ does not contain $H$ as a $c$-fat minor, then $G$ admits an $(L,C)$-quasi-isometry to a graph with no $H$ minor.
rows.reason: Well-known named coarse-minor conjecture (fat-minor-free implies quasi-isometric to an H-minor-free graph); none of the paper-local candidates match.

rows.statement: The size of pairs of set systems satisfying both the Two Families hypotheses of Bollob\'{a}s and an intersection condition on the first family is bounded by the Ahlswede-Khachatrian bound on the size of $t$-intersecting families.
rows.attributed_to: Gerbner, Keszegh, Methuku, Abhishek, Nagy, Patkós, Tompkins, and Xiao
rows.reason: Named multi-author extremal set-system conjecture bounding Bollobás two-family pairs by the AK t-intersecting bound; the sole candidate (circular choosability) is unrelated.

rows.statement: Every 4-chromatic planar graph has fractional chromatic number strictly greater than $3$.
rows.reason: Clean named conjecture (every 4-chromatic planar graph has fractional chromatic number > 3); no candidate matches.

rows.statement: Is there a polynomial-time algorithm to test the 3-colorability of triangle-free graphs embeddable in a fixed surface?
rows.reason: Named third-party problem with a self-contained statement (poly-time testing of 3-colorability of triangle-free graphs on a fixed surface); candidates are 4-colorability/linear-time-output variants, not this.

rows.canonical_name: Gishboliner–Steiner–Szabó conjecture on the dichromatic Mader number
rows.statement: $\mathrm{mader}_{\tilde{\chi}}(\overleftrightarrow{K}_t) \leq Ct^2$ for some absolute constant $C$.
rows.reason: Named digraph conjecture bounding the dichromatic Mader number of bidirected complete digraphs by Ct^2; no candidate matches.

rows.canonical_name: Glover's conjecture on Klein bottle obstructions
rows.statement: More than $10^6$ obstructions for the Klein bottle will be obtained by pasting together two obstructions for the projective plane by identifying two vertices in all possible ways.
rows.reason: Essentially an enumerative/construction-specific prediction ('more than 10^6 obstructions obtained by pasting projective-plane obstructions'), out-of-scope as a clean statement target.

rows.canonical_name: Goemans' open problem on spanning tree extension complexity
rows.statement: Improve either the lower bound $\mathrm{xc}(P(G)) \geq |E(G)| - |V(G)|$ or the upper bound $\mathrm{xc}(P(G)) \in O(|V(G)| \cdot |E(G)|)$ for the extension complexity of the spanning tree polytope of a connected graph $G$.
rows.reason: An 'improve the bound' open problem on polytope extension complexity, not a crisp formalizable proposition and out-of-scope for the statement waves.

rows.statement: Any family of a constant fraction of all graphs on $[n]$, where $n$ is sufficiently large, contains two graphs $G_1, G_2$ such that $G_2$ is a subgraph of $G_1$ and the symmetric difference of the two graphs (the set of all edges of $G_1$ that are not in $G_2$) forms a clique.
rows.reason: Named Gowers conjecture with a self-contained statement over the subgraph poset; the erdos candidates (925/88/803/883) are unrelated Ramsey/coprime/almost-regular problems.

rows.statement: Is there any pair $(q,r)$ for which one can prove a power savings $G_{q,r}(n)\leqslant n^{q-r+1-\delta}$, for some absolute constant $\delta>0$?
rows.reason: Provability-flavored question about a power saving for the paper-defined quantity G_{q,r}(n); paper-specific primitive and meta framing make it too paper-dependent to stand alone.

rows.statement: $N_2(G) = n-1$ for almost all graphs $G$ on $n$ vertices.
rows.reason: Named Graham conjecture (N_2(G)=n-1 for almost all n-vertex graphs) on the addressing number; self-contained modulo the addressing primitive, no candidate.

rows.statement: Determine $M(\delta)$, the maximum constant such that for every $\varepsilon > 0$ and every sufficiently large $n$, every $A \subseteq G \times G$ with $|A| \geq \delta|G|^2$ contains at least $(M(\delta) - \varepsilon)|G|^2$ corners $(x,y),(x+d,y),(x,y+d) \in A$ for some nonzero $d \in G$.
rows.reason: A 'determine the optimal constant M(delta)' open problem in additive combinatorics, an open value rather than a formalizable yes/no proposition.

rows.canonical_name: Green's problem on polynomial bounds for arithmetic triangle removal
rows.statement: For every $\epsilon > 0$ and prime $p$, does there exist a polynomial bound on $\delta = \delta(\epsilon, p) > 0$ such that if $X, Y, Z \subset \mathbb{F}_p^n$ and the number of triangles in $X \times Y \times Z$ is at most $\delta N^2$ (where $N = p^n$), then one can delete $\epsilon N$ elements from $X$, $Y$, and $Z$ so that no triangle remains?
rows.reason: A 'does a polynomial bound exist' question about arithmetic triangle removal in F_p^n; asymptotic/additive research direction, borderline out-of-scope, no candidate matches.

rows.statement: Every graph of pathwidth less than $a$ containing no $2^b$-vertex path has treedepth $O(ab)$.
rows.reason: Named conjecture (pathwidth < a with no 2^b-vertex path implies treedepth O(ab)); the same-paper candidate is only an auxiliary sharpness open problem, not this statement.

rows.canonical_name: Growth rate of list Ramsey numbers (Alon–Bucić–Kalvari–Kuperwasser–Szabó)
rows.statement: Which side of the bounds $e^{\sqrt{k}\log(\chi(H)-1)/(4r)} \leq R_\ell(H,k) \leq (1-\pi(H)+o(1))^{-km(H)}$ is closer to the truth for a fixed $r$-graph $H$ that is not $r$-partite?
rows.attributed_to: Alon, Buci\'c, Kalvari, Kuperwasser, and Szab\'o
rows.reason: A 'which side of the bounds is closer to the truth' question, not a crisp proposition; the only match is the same paper's own record.

rows.canonical_name: Gruslys' conjecture (perfect induced H-packings of powers)
rows.statement: Let $G$ be a finite vertex-transitive graph and let $H$ be an induced subgraph of $G$. If $|V(H)|$ divides $|V(G)|$, then there exists a positive integer $n$ such that $G^n$ admits a perfect induced $H$-packing.
rows.reason: Named Gruslys conjecture (vertex-transitive G with induced H and |V(H)| dividing |V(G)| implies some power G^n has a perfect induced H-packing); no candidate matches.

rows.statement: For $k \geq 1$, let $H$ be a non-empty subgraph of $Q_k$. Then there exists a positive integer $n$ such that the edges of $Q_n$ can be covered by edge-disjoint copies of $H$ (the copies of $H$ are not required to be induced).
rows.reason: Named conjecture that any nonempty subgraph of Q_k edge-decomposes some hypercube Q_n; no candidate matches.

rows.canonical_name: Grytczuk's conjecture (nonrepetitive chromatic number of minor-closed classes)
rows.statement: For every graph $X$, there is an integer $k$ such that every $X$-minor-free graph $G$ satisfies $\pi(G) \leq k$.
rows.reason: Named Grytczuk conjecture that every minor-closed class has bounded nonrepetitive (Thue) chromatic number; candidates concern tree-alpha/eta/rooted-minor bounds, not pi.

rows.statement: If $G$ is a 2-connected cubic graph embedded in the torus and $G$ is not 3-edge-colorable, then $G$ contains two edges whose removal gives a planar graph.
rows.reason: Named Grünbaum conjecture (a non-3-edge-colorable 2-connected cubic torus graph has two edges whose removal yields a planar graph); all candidates are unrelated derived rows.

rows.canonical_name: Grünbaum's conjecture (torus cubic graphs, refined face version)
rows.statement: If $G$ is a 2-connected cubic graph embedded in the torus and $G$ is not 3-edge-colorable, then $G$ contains two edges on the same face of the embedding, whose removal gives a planar graph in which the four endpoints of the removed edges lie on the boundaries of two faces.
rows.reason: Stronger paper-specific restatement of the same Grünbaum torus conjecture promoted at cid 285 (adds same-face and two-face-boundary conditions); best kept as an implication endpoint to avoid a duplicate statement target.

rows.canonical_name: Grünbaum's problem on edge density of 4-critical planar graphs
rows.statement: Determine the precise value of $L := \limsup |E(G)|/|V(G)|$ over all $4$-critical planar graphs $G$.
rows.reason: A 'determine the limsup L' open value problem, not a crisp formalizable proposition; no candidate matches.

rows.statement: For every graph $G$ and every integer $d\geq 0$, we have $\chi(G)=\chi^{d}(G\boxtimes K_{d+1})$.
rows.reason: Named 2025 third-party conjecture (χ(G)=χ^d(G⊠K_{d+1})) not matched by either candidate (a hypergraph-Hadwiger row and a poly-χ-boundedness row); needs strong-product and the χ^d variant as primitives.

rows.statement: For all integers $k, \ell > 0$ there exists $n(k, \ell)$ such that every graph $G$ with no clique of cardinality more than $k$ and no odd hole of length more than $\ell$ has chromatic number at most $n(k, \ell)$.
rows.reason: Named Gyárfás conjecture (bounded clique + bounded-length odd holes ⟹ bounded χ) with no matching corpus row; requires an odd-hole (induced odd cycle) primitive.

rows.statement: For all $\kappa, \ell \geq 0$ there exists $c$ such that for every graph $G$, if $\omega(G) \leq \kappa$ and $\chi(G) > c$ then $G$ has an odd hole of length at least $\ell$.
rows.reason: Contrapositive-equivalent restatement (large χ + bounded clique ⟹ long odd hole) of the same Gyárfás odd-hole conjecture promoted at cid 289; kept only as an edge endpoint to avoid a duplicate statement target.

rows.statement: Let $\mathcal{C}$ be the class of graphs $G$ such that $\alpha(H)\omega(H) \geq |V(H)| - 1$ for every induced subgraph $H$ of $G$. Then $\mathcal{C}$ is $\chi$-bounded.
rows.reason: Named Gyárfás χ-boundedness conjecture over the α·ω-defined class; no candidate (forbidden-tree/edge-critical rows) matches, and it is expressible with standard α, ω, χ invariants.

rows.statement: Fix $k \geq 3$. Let $G$ be any graph with $n$ vertices and $\delta(G) \geq \left(1 - \frac{k-1}{k^2}\right)n$. If the edges of $G$ are $k$-coloured, then there exists a monochromatic component of order at least $\frac{n}{k-1}$.
rows.reason: Named third-party conjecture on large monochromatic components under a min-degree threshold in k-edge-colourings; none of the erdos candidates match and it is a clean finite combinatorial statement.

rows.statement: Let $c \geq 0$, and let $\mathcal{C}$ be a class of graphs such that $\chi(H) \leq \omega(H) + c$ for all $G \in \mathcal{C}$ and every induced subgraph $H$ of $G$. Let $\mathcal{C}'$ be the class of complements of members of $\mathcal{C}$. Then $\mathcal{C}'$ is $\chi$-bounded.
rows.reason: Named Gyárfás conjecture that complements of a (χ≤ω+c)-class form a χ-bounded class; no candidate matches and it uses standard χ, ω, complement notions.

rows.statement: For every finite family $\mathcal{F}$ of axis-parallel rectangles in $\mathbb{R}^2$, the ratio $\tau(\mathcal{F})/\nu(\mathcal{F})$ is bounded by a constant.
rows.reason: A genuinely geometric conjecture on τ/ν for axis-parallel rectangles in ℝ² (no candidate matches); out of scope for a finite-graph formalization campaign, so parked.

rows.statement: Given two graphs $F_1$ and $F_2$ the class of graphs with no induced $F_1$ or $F_2$ has bounded chromatic number if and only if one of $F_1$, $F_2$ is a complete graph and the other is a forest.
rows.reason: Famous base Gyárfás–Sumner conjecture; corpus search shows only polynomial/tournament variants are tracked (candidates are the polynomial forms), so the base statement is promoted.

rows.statement: $H$-minor-free graphs are $(v(H)-1)$-colorable.
rows.reason: Seymour's H-generalization (H-minor-free ⟹ (v(H)−1)-colorable); distinct from the sole candidate opg:list_hadwiger_conjecture (a list-colouring variant), so promoted; needs the minor primitive.

rows.statement: Let $t \geq 1$ be an integer, and let $\mathcal{G}$ be a minor-closed class of graphs. Then $\chi(\mathcal{G}) \leq t$ if and only if $K_{t+1} \notin \mathcal{G}$.
rows.reason: Base Hadwiger conjecture; corpus check confirms only fractional/list variants exist (no base row), and no candidate matches, so promoted; needs the graph-minor primitive.

rows.statement: For every integer $t \geq 1$ there exists $\varepsilon > 0$ such that every $K_{t,t}$-free graph $G$ has a clique cover of size $O(|G|^{2-\varepsilon})$.
rows.reason: Named Hajebi conjecture (K_{t,t}-free graphs have clique cover O(n^{2−ε})) not matched by any candidate; the O(n^{2−ε}) bound requires asymptotics.

rows.statement: Every $5$-chromatic graph contains a subdivision of $K_{5}$.
rows.reason: Classic named Hajós conjecture at k=5 (every 5-chromatic graph contains a K_5 subdivision) with no candidates; requires the topological-minor/subdivision primitive.

rows.statement: The crossing number of the complete graph $K_n$ is $$H(n) = \frac{1}{4}\left\lfloor\frac{n}{2}\right\rfloor\left\lfloor\frac{n-1}{2}\right\rfloor\left\lfloor\frac{n-2}{2}\right\rfloor\left\lfloor\frac{n-3}{2}\right\rfloor.$$
rows.reason: The opg row states cr(K_n)=¼⌊n/2⌋⌊(n−1)/2⌋⌊(n−2)/2⌋⌊(n−3)/2⌋ verbatim — the exact Harary–Hill formula; the matcher missed it but it is the same proposition, so alias.

rows.statement: There exists an absolute constant $K>0$ such that for every sufficiently large $d\in\mathbb{N}$, every triangle-free $d$-degenerate graph $G$ satisfies $\chi_{f}(G)\leq K\frac{d}{\log d}$.
rows.reason: Named Harris conjecture (triangle-free d-degenerate graphs are fractionally O(d/log d)-colorable) unmatched by candidates; the d/log d 'sufficiently large d' bound needs asymptotics.

rows.statement: For every oriented graph $H$, there is $\epsilon>0$ such that every $H$-free oriented graph $D$ of order $n$ satisfies $\vec{\alpha}(D)\geq n^{\epsilon}$ and $\vec{\chi}(D)\leq n^{1-\epsilon}$.
rows.reason: Named oriented Erdős–Hajnal-type conjecture (H-free oriented graphs have polynomial independence and small dichromatic number); no candidate matches and the n^ε bounds need asymptotics.

rows.statement: Let $D$ be a digraph of digirth at least $3$ and maximum degree $\Delta$. Then $\vec{\chi}(D) \leq \left\lceil \frac{\tilde{\Delta}(D)}{2} \right\rceil + 1 \leq \left\lceil \frac{\Delta}{4} \right\rceil + 1$.
rows.reason: Named digraph-colouring conjecture bounding the dichromatic number by digirth/max-degree terms; no candidate matches and it needs dichromatic-number and digirth primitives.

rows.canonical_name: Hat-Guessing Number Bounded by Hadwiger Number (Alon–Chizewer)
rows.statement: Whether the hat guessing number of graphs can be bounded in terms of their Hadwiger number $h(G)$.
rows.reason: Attributed open problem (∃f: HG(G)≤f(h(G))); candidates are different hat-guessing questions, and a parallel hat-guessing/degeneracy belief is already tracked in-corpus, so promoted; needs the hat-guessing game and minor primitives.

rows.statement: Does there exist a constant $d$ such that every planar graph in which every two distinct triangles are at distance at least $d$ is 3-colorable?
rows.reason: Famous named Havel problem with a precise statement (∃d making distance-d-triangle planar graphs 3-colorable); no candidate matches and it needs planarity infrastructure.

rows.statement: Planar graphs have bounded queue-number.
rows.reason: Candidate arxiv:1507.01120#00 ('whether planar graphs have constant queue number') is the same proposition as this bounded-queue-number conjecture, so alias.

rows.statement: Planar graphs have unbounded queue-number.
rows.reason: Asserts the opposite (planar graphs have UNBOUNDED queue-number) of the proposition tracked at arxiv:1507.01120#00, so it is the negation rather than the same proposition; kept as an edge endpoint (since refuted, it cannot alias).

rows.statement: Determine $\mathrm{disc}(\mathcal{A}_p)$ up to a constant factor for prime $p$, i.e., close the $O(\sqrt{\log p})$ multiplicative gap between the lower bound $c_1\sqrt{p}$ and the upper bound $c_2\sqrt{p\log p}$.
rows.reason: A 'determine up to a constant / close the √log p gap' discrepancy problem — not a boolean proposition and outside graph theory; parked.

rows.statement: Every subcubic triangle-free planar graph is fractionally $8/3$-colorable.
rows.reason: Named Heckman–Thomas conjecture (subcubic triangle-free planar graphs are fractionally 8/3-colorable); the only candidate (Conjecture 1.6, a 19/7 forbidden-subgraph variant from the same paper) is a different statement, so promoted; needs planarity.

rows.statement: Let $G$ be a connected graph with $\Delta(G)\leqslant 3$, then $\gamma(G)\leqslant 2\rho(G)+1$. The equality holds only for three well-defined graphs, one of which being the Petersen graph.
rows.reason: Named domination-vs-packing conjecture (γ(G)≤2ρ(G)+1 for subcubic connected G with an extremal characterization incl. Petersen); no candidate matches and it needs domination/packing primitives.

rows.statement: Heroes in $\mathrm{Forb}_{\mathrm{ind}}(\overrightarrow{K}_{1,2})$ are the same as heroes in tournaments.
rows.attributed_to: authors of references [1] and [14] in the paper
rows.reason: Specialized, vaguely-attributed ('references [1] and [14]') hero-classification statement tightly bound to the paper's own derived hero rows; kept as an edge endpoint rather than a standalone statement target.

rows.statement: The minimum number of crossings in any drawing of the complete graph $K_n$ in the plane is $$H(n) = \frac{1}{4} \left\lfloor \frac{n}{2} \right\rfloor \left\lfloor \frac{n-1}{2} \right\rfloor \left\lfloor \frac{n-2}{2} \right\rfloor \left\lfloor \frac{n-3}{2} \right\rfloor = \begin{cases} \frac{1}{64} n(n-2)^2(n-4), & n \text{ even};\\ \frac{1}{64}(n-1)^2(n-3)^2, & n \text{ odd.} \end{cases}$$
rows.reason: The OPG row states cr(K_n)=1/4·floor(n/2)floor((n-1)/2)floor((n-2)/2)floor((n-3)/2), which is exactly the Hill/Harary–Hill formula in the cluster statement.

rows.statement: A graph $G$ has every induced subgraph $2$-divisible if and only if $G$ contains no induced odd cycle of length at least five.
rows.reason: Named third-party conjecture (2-divisibility iff no induced odd hole ≥5); no candidate matches (erdős/OPG rows are unrelated), needs a 2-divisible primitive. [verify downgraded: The opg row states 'no induced odd cycle of length ≥5 ⟹ a 2-colouring with no monochromatic maximum clique', i.e. 2-divisibility; as the hypothesis is hereditary this is the same proposition as the Hoàng–McDiarmid divisibility characterization.]

rows.statement: Let $r\in\mathbb{N}$. Every digraph $D$ with $\delta^{+}(D)\geq r$ contains a sequence $C_{1},\ldots,C_{r}$ of directed cycles such that for each $i\in[r]$ it holds that $$\left|V(C_{i})\cap\bigcup_{1\leq j<i}V(C_{j})\right|\leq 1.$$
rows.reason: OPG row states out-degree ≥ k forces k directed cycles each meeting the union of previous ones in ≤1 vertex — identical to the cluster's δ⁺(D)≥r statement.

rows.statement: Any $k$-dicritical oriented graph $D$ satisfies $\frac{m(D)}{n(D)} \geq \frac{k^2}{2} - O(k)$.
rows.reason: Named conjecture on edge density of k-dicritical oriented graphs (m/n ≥ k²/2 − O(k)); no candidates and no OPG dicritical row exist; O(k) term needs asymptotics.

rows.statement: The 4-precoloring extension problem can be solved in polynomial time for the class of $P_6$-free graphs.
rows.reason: Named conjecture studied by two papers that 4-precolouring extension is poly-time on P₆-free graphs; candidates do not match; polynomial-time claim needs a computation model.

rows.statement: For fixed $r$, $\mathrm{ind}_r(k,\ell) \leq e^{-1} + o_k(1)$ for all $0 < \ell < \binom{k}{r}$.
rows.reason: Same named AHKT Edge-statistics Conjecture; candidate row is the r=2 form (ind(k,ℓ)≤1/e+ε) and the cluster is its r-uniform generalisation — one named conjecture, so alias per prefer-alias posture.

rows.statement: Every factorial hereditary class (i.e., hereditary class with growth $n!^{O(1)}$) admits an $O(\log n)$-adjacency labeling scheme.
rows.reason: Famous Kannan–Naor–Rudich conjecture (factorial hereditary classes admit O(log n) adjacency labelings); candidate rows are related paper-local questions, not the conjecture itself; labeling scheme needs a computation model.

rows.statement: An induced arithmetic removal lemma holds in general: for a fixed finite collection of $r$-colored arithmetic patterns, every coloring with $o(1)$ density of each such pattern can be recolored on an $o(1)$-fraction of the ambient space so that no such pattern remains.
rows.reason: Title is external reference numbering ('Conjecture 5.3 of [24]') and the statement is explicitly informal/hedged; the only close candidates are same-paper informal open-problem rows — too vague to stand as a formalization target.

rows.statement: There exists a constant-factor approximation algorithm for the problem of finding a shortest path between two perfect matchings on the perfect matching polytope of a bipartite graph.
rows.attributed_to: Ito, Kakimura, Kamiyama, Kobayashi, and Okamoto
rows.reason: Named five-author conjecture on a constant-factor approximation for shortest paths between perfect matchings on the bipartite matching polytope; no candidate matches; approximation-algorithm claim needs a computation model.

rows.canonical_name: Jaeger's Conjecture (high-girth planar graphs and odd-cycle homomorphisms)
rows.statement: Any planar graph $G$ of girth at least $4k$ admits a homomorphism to $C_{2k+1}$. In other words, $\chi_{c}(G)\leq\frac{2k+1}{k}$.
rows.reason: Named Jaeger conjecture that planar girth ≥4k implies a homomorphism to C_{2k+1} (χ_c ≤ (2k+1)/k); candidate 2206.13635 is a hypergraph-minor statement, no match; requires planarity.

rows.statement: Every 5-edge-connected graph is $Z_3$-connected.
rows.reason: 'Every 5-edge-connected graph is Z₃-connected' is distinct from OPG's modular orientation conjecture (4k-edge-connected mod-(2k+1) orientation) and the Z₅-antisymmetric-flow candidate; group connectivity needs a flow primitive.

rows.statement: For every prime number $p$, there is a constant $c(p)$ such that for any $t > c(p)$ linear bases $B_1, \ldots, B_t$ of $\mathbb{Z}_p^n$, the union $\bigcup_{s=1}^{t} B_s$ forms an additive basis of $\mathbb{Z}_p^n$.
rows.reason: Named JLPT additive-basis conjecture over Z_p^n; candidates (antisymmetric flow, list chromatic, erdős) do not match; needs an additive-basis primitive.

rows.canonical_name: Javadi–Hajebi Question (linear clique covers for bounded independence number)
rows.statement: Do all graphs $G$ with $\alpha(G)$ at most a constant admit clique covers of size $O(|G|)$?
rows.reason: Named yes/no question (bounded α forces clique cover of size O(|G|)) with an assertable proposition; no candidate matches; O(|G|) bound needs asymptotics.

rows.statement: Every planar graph $G$ satisfies $\mathrm{fvs}(G) \leq 2 \cdot \mathrm{cp}(G)$.
rows.reason: OPG row states every planar graph has cc(G) ≤ 2·cp(G) (feedback vertex set ≤ twice max cycle packing) — identical to the cluster's fvs ≤ 2·cp statement.

rows.canonical_name: Jovović–Koledin–Stanić Conjecture (spectral gap minimised by double comets)
rows.statement: In $T(n)$, the spectral gap $\lambda_1 - \lambda_2$ is minimized by a double comet $DC(k, k, \ell)$ such that $2k + \ell = n$.
rows.reason: Real named conjecture but titled by internal numbering (Conjecture 1.3) with paper-dependent notation (T(n), double comet DC(k,k,ℓ), unspecified matrix); value is as an edge endpoint, not a standalone target.

rows.canonical_name: Jungić et al. Conjecture on T_k
rows.statement: $T_k = \Theta(k^2)$
rows.reason: Statement 'T_k = Θ(k²)' is fragmentary — T_k is undefined out of context — too fragmentary to stand alone.

rows.statement: Every 6-connected graph with no $K_6$ minor can be made planar by deleting some vertex, and therefore has a vertex of degree at most six.
rows.reason: OPG row states every 6-connected K₆-minor-free graph is apex (planar plus one vertex) — the same proposition as the cluster's 'made planar by deleting some vertex'.

rows.statement: Is computing the diameter of the vertex-edge graph of a simple polytope NP-hard?
rows.reason: NP-hardness question about the diameter of a simple polytope's vertex-edge graph — a complexity question on polytopes with no home repo in the graph-theory statement waves; out of scope.

rows.statement: For every ternary graph, the number of stable sets with even cardinality and the number with odd cardinality differ by at most one. Equivalently, if $f_G = \sum_{A\text{ stable}} (-1)^{|A|}$, then $|f_G| \leq 1$.
rows.reason: Named conjecture that for every ternary graph the signed count of stable sets has |Σ(-1)^|A|| ≤ 1; no candidate matches; needs the ternary-graph / independence-polynomial-at-(-1) primitive.

rows.canonical_name: Kawarabayashi et al. Additive List-Colouring Approximation Conjecture
rows.statement: There exists $\alpha \geq 0$ with the following property: for every proper minor-closed class $\mathcal{G}$, there exists a polynomial-time algorithm taking as an input a graph $G \in \mathcal{G}$ and returning an integer $c$ such that $\chi_{\ell}(G) \leq c \leq \chi_{\ell}(G) + \alpha$.
rows.reason: Conjecture that list-chromatic number is additively approximable in polytime across all proper minor-closed classes; candidate 1707.03888#00 is the distinct chromatic/triangle-free variant; approximation claim needs a computation model.

rows.statement: Every graph $G$ without a $K_t$-minor satisfies $\chi_\ell(G) \leq \frac{3}{2}t$.
rows.reason: Same named List Hadwiger Conjecture (Kawarabayashi–Mohar): OPG row is the 'ct-list-colourable for some c' form and the cluster pins c=3/2; one named conjecture, so alias to the OPG anchor.

rows.statement: There exists a universal constant $c$ such that every graph with pathwidth $\Omega(k^c)$ has treewidth at least $k$ or contains a subdivision of a complete binary tree of height $k$.
rows.reason: Named conjecture relating pathwidth to treewidth or complete-binary-tree subdivisions; no candidate matches; needs pathwidth/treewidth/subdivision primitives.

rows.statement: What is the maximum possible average degree of a properly edge-coloured graph on $n$ vertices containing no rainbow cycle?
rows.reason: A 'what is the maximum average degree' extremal question with no conjectured value, so not an assertable proposition; its value is as the endpoint the studying paper's bounds point toward.

rows.statement: For every $r \in \mathbb{N}$, there is an integer $N_r$ such that every graph with at least $N_r$ vertices is reconstructible from its $(n-r)$-deck.
rows.reason: Named generalisation of reconstruction to (n−r)-decks with a size threshold N_r; distinct from OPG's classic Reconstruction Conjecture (n−1 deck) and Edge Reconstruction Conjecture; needs a deck/reconstruction primitive.

rows.statement: For every triangle-free graph there exists a probability distribution on its independent sets such that every vertex $v\in V(G)$ appears with probability at least $(1-o(1))\frac{\ln d_{G}(v)}{d_{G}(v)}$ in a random independent set chosen from the distribution. Here, the $o(1)$ term represents any function that tends to $0$ as the degree grows.
rows.reason: Named third-party conjecture (Kelly–Postle) on a probability distribution over independent sets of triangle-free graphs; self-contained but carries an intrinsic (1-o(1)) asymptotic term, and no candidate row matches.

rows.canonical_name: Kierstead et al. question on the 2-coloring number of planar graphs
rows.statement: Is the two-coloring number of all planar graphs bounded by $8$?
rows.reason: Named, self-contained bounded question (col_2 of planar graphs ≤ 8) about a generalized coloring number on planar graphs; none of the planar-coloring candidates state it.

rows.statement: A $4$-regular simple graph with $m$ edges has at most $1.99^{m/2}$ cycles.
rows.reason: Named conjecture giving a concrete numeric bound (1.99^{m/2}) on the number of cycles in 4-regular simple graphs; no candidate matches this cycle-enumeration statement.

rows.statement: The number of cycles in a simple graph with $m$ edges is $O(1.4^m)$.
rows.reason: Distinct Király conjecture bounding the number of cycles in any simple graph by O(1.4^m) — a big-O growth statement; unmatched by candidates.

rows.canonical_name: Knuth's problem on pivot Gray codes for arborescences
rows.statement: Does every directed graph $G$ admit a pivot Gray code on its arborescences rooted in a given vertex?
rows.reason: Paper-local numbered item ('Problem 2') on existence of a pivot Gray code over arborescences; specialized combinatorial-generation question whose value is as an implication endpoint, and the same-paper candidate is a different problem.

rows.canonical_name: Knuth's minrank problem for random graphs
rows.statement: Determine the asymptotic behavior of the typical $\mathrm{min\text{-}rank}_{\mathbb{R}}(G(n,p))$ for fixed $p \in (0,1)$.
rows.reason: Open-ended 'determine the asymptotic behavior of typical min-rank of G(n,p)' — a determination problem with no crisp falsifiable proposition to target.

rows.statement: Let $H_{1},\dots,H_{r}$ be graphs, and suppose that $m_{2}(H_{1})\geq\dotsb\geq m_{2}(H_{r})$ and $m_{2}(H_{2})>1$. There exist constants $C>c>0$ such that $G_{n,p}$ is a.a.s. Ramsey for $(H_{1},\dots,H_{r})$ if $p\geq Cn^{-1/m_{2}(H_{1},H_{2})}$, and a.a.s. not Ramsey if $p\leq cn^{-1/m_{2}(H_{1},H_{2})}$, where $$m_{2}(H_{1},H_{2})\coloneqq\max\left\{\frac{{\mathsf{e}}(J)}{{\mathsf{v}}(J)-2+1/m_{2}(H_{2})}:J\subseteq H_{1},{\mathsf{v}}(J)\geq 2\right\}.$$
rows.reason: Famous named conjecture on random-graph Ramsey thresholds (a.a.s. Ramsey at p=Θ(n^{-1/m_2})); no erdos/arxiv candidate states it, so promote.

rows.canonical_name: Korhonen–Lokshtanov question on induced disjoint paths in induced-minor-free graphs
rows.statement: Is Induced $k$-Disjoint Paths NP-hard in $H$-induced-minor-free graphs for some fixed $k$ and $H$?
rows.reason: Same-paper candidate arxiv:2502.05289#01 (informal question on the complexity gap for Induced Disjoint Paths) is the same NP-hardness question restated; alias to the tracked row.

rows.canonical_name: Korándi–Pach–Tomon conjecture (pure pairs in ordered bigraphs)
rows.statement: Let $H$ be an ordered bigraph such that both $H$ and its bicomplement are forests. Then there exists $\varepsilon > 0$ with the following property. Let $G$ be an ordered bigraph that does not contain $H$, with $|V_1(G)|, |V_2(G)| \geq n$; then there is a pure pair $(Z_1, Z_2)$ with $Z_i \subseteq V_i(G)$ and $|Z_i| \geq \varepsilon n$ for $i = 1, 2$.
rows.reason: Named Erdős–Hajnal-type conjecture giving a linear-size pure pair in H-free ordered bigraphs; unmatched by candidates (which are Menger/other), so promote. [verify downgraded: Paper-local numbered restatements of the KPT ordered-bigraph pure-pairs conjecture studied (and believed false) by 2101.03537; the canonical version is promoted at cid 345.]

rows.canonical_name: Korándi–Pach–Tomon conjecture (pure pairs in ordered bigraphs)
rows.statement: The statement of Theorem 1.7 still holds when $G$ and $H$ are ordered bigraphs (bigraphs $G$ with fixed linear orders on $V_1(G)$ and on $V_2(G)$) and containment is required to respect the linear orders: for every forest ordered bigraph $H$ and every $\tau$ with $0 < \tau \leq 1$, there exists $\varepsilon > 0$ such that if $G$ is an $H$-free ordered bigraph with at most $(1-\tau)|V_1(G)|\cdot|V_2(G)|$ edges, then there are anticomplete sets $Z_i \subseteq V_i(G)$ with $|Z_i| \geq \varepsilon|V_i(G)|$ for $i = 1, 2$.
rows.reason: Same named Korándi–Pach–Tomon ordered-bigraph pure-pairs conjecture studied by a second paper; shares canonical_name with cid 344 for downstream dedup, and no candidate row matches.

rows.statement: If $D$ is a $k$-dicritical digraph of order $n$ with $k \geq 4$ and $n \geq k$, then $m(D) \geq 2g_k(n)$ and equality implies that $D$ is a bidirected $k$-critical graph. As a consequence, $d_k(n) = 2g_k(n)$ when $n \geq k$ and $n \neq k+1$.
rows.reason: Named conjecture on the minimum edge count of k-dicritical digraphs; the Borodin–Kostochka candidate is a different (undirected chromatic) statement, so promote.

rows.canonical_name: Kowalik–Lužar–Škrekovski conjecture (induced forests in planar graphs of girth ≥ 5)
rows.statement: Every planar graph of girth at least five has an induced forest on at least seven-tenths of its vertices, i.e., $a(G) \geq 7|V(G)|/10$ for every planar graph $G$ of girth at least five.
rows.reason: The OPG candidate states the general n/2 planar induced-forest bound; this is the distinct girth-≥5, 7n/10 refinement, so promote rather than alias.

rows.canonical_name: Škrekovski conjecture (separation choosability of planar graphs)
rows.statement: Every planar graph has separation choosability at most $3$.
rows.reason: Named conjecture (separation choosability of planar graphs ≤ 3) with a self-contained statement and no candidate rows.

rows.statement: Every digraph is majority 3-colorable.
rows.attributed_to: Kreutzer, Oum, Seymour, van der Zypen and Wood
rows.reason: Well-known named conjecture 'every digraph is majority 3-colorable'; self-contained, needs the majority-coloring primitive, and has no candidate rows.

rows.canonical_name: Alon–Krivelevich conjecture (list chromatic number of bipartite graphs)
rows.statement: There is some absolute constant $C > 0$ such that any bipartite graph of maximum degree at most $\Delta$ is $k$-choosable if $k \geq C \log \Delta$.
rows.reason: OPG row states exactly 'list chromatic number of any bipartite graph of max degree Δ is at most c log Δ', identical to this Krivelevich–Alon conjecture.

rows.statement: There is no known combinatorial formula for the coefficients of Schur function plethysms (known as Kronecker coefficients) except in special cases.
rows.reason: Informal meta-observation about the absence of a combinatorial formula for Kronecker coefficients; algebraic combinatorics / representation theory, outside the graph-theory formalization scope.

rows.statement: Let $m>0$ be a real number. Every graph $G$ with $m(G)\leq m$ contains a forest $F\subseteq G$ such that $m_{2}(G-F)\leq m$.
rows.reason: Named conjecture on removing a forest to bound the 2-density m_2(G−F); self-contained given the density primitives m and m_2, unmatched by candidates.

rows.statement: There exist two functions $f$ and $\ell$ such that, for every integer $k$, every tournament $T$ with $\operatorname{dom}(T)\geq f(k)$ contains a subtournament $X$ with $|X|\leq\ell(k)$ and $\operatorname{dom}(X)\geq k$.
rows.reason: Named tournament conjecture on bounded-size subtournaments of large dom; the same-paper candidate (Conjecture 5.3) targets ω̄-clusters, a different parameter, so this dom-cluster version is promoted.

rows.canonical_name: Larsen–Nelson question on improved Johnson–Lindenstrauss dimension
rows.statement: For any $\varepsilon$, can the target dimension in the Johnson-Lindenstrauss Lemma be improved by replacing $\frac{\log n}{\varepsilon^2}$ with $t = \frac{\log(2 + \varepsilon^2 n)}{\varepsilon^2}$?
rows.reason: Question about the target dimension in the Johnson–Lindenstrauss lemma (random projection / metric embedding), outside graph-theory scope.

rows.canonical_name: Las Vergnas–Meyniel conjecture A (Kempe equivalence of colorings)
rows.statement: For every $t$ and every graph with no $K_{t}$-minor, every equivalence class of $t$-colourings contains some $(t-1)$-colouring.
rows.reason: Named conjecture that in K_t-minor-free graphs every Kempe class of t-colorings contains a (t-1)-coloring; the sole candidate (Question 1.7) is a different item, so promote.

rows.statement: For any $t$, any graph that admits a quasi-$K_{t}$-minor admits a $K_{t}$-minor.
rows.reason: Named conjecture that a quasi-K_t-minor implies a K_t-minor; the sole candidate (Question 1.6) is a different item, so promote with the quasi-minor primitive.

rows.statement: For every graph $G$, the genus polynomial $\Gamma_G(x) = \sum_{k \geq 0} g_k(G)x^k$ is log-concave.
rows.reason: Named Gross–Robbins–Tucker conjecture that the genus polynomial is log-concave; self-contained, needs an embedding/genus-count primitive, and has no candidate rows.

rows.canonical_name: Le's conjecture on induced paths in O₂-free graphs
rows.statement: There is a constant $c$ such that every $\mathcal{O}_{2}$-free $n$-vertex graph has at most $n^{c}$ distinct induced paths.
rows.reason: Named Le conjecture bounding distinct induced paths by n^c in O₂-free graphs; a polynomial-growth statement distinct from the induced-cycles version, unmatched by candidates. [verify downgraded: Paper-local numbered restatement of Le's induced-paths conjecture (same proposition promoted at cid 360); endpoint for paper 2206.00594; not separately tracked.]

rows.canonical_name: Le's conjecture on induced cycles in 2O-free graphs
rows.statement: There exists $c > 0$ such that every $2\mathcal{O}$-free graph $G$ has at most $|G|^c$ induced cycles.
rows.reason: Named Le conjecture bounding distinct induced cycles by |G|^c in 2O-free graphs; distinct from the induced-paths conjecture and unmatched by candidates.

rows.statement: There exists $c > 0$ such that every $2\mathcal{O}$-free graph $G$ has at most $|G|^c$ induced paths.
rows.reason: Named third-party conjecture (Le) with a self-contained statement bounding the number of induced paths by |G|^c in 2O-free graphs; no candidate matches (all are unrelated induced-subgraph/chi-boundedness rows).

rows.statement: Let $A$ be a finite set and $F$ be the free semigroup with alphabet $A$. If $S \subset F$ is product-free and $d^*(S) = 1/2$, then $S \subset O_\Gamma$ for some nonempty subset $\Gamma \subset A$.
rows.reason: Named conjecture but about product-free sets in free semigroups (additive/semigroup combinatorics), out of scope for the graph-theory corpus; no candidate matches.

rows.canonical_name: Lee's Conjecture on Random Spanning Trees
rows.statement: Let $d$ be sufficiently large, let $G$ be a connected graph with $n$ vertices and minimum degree at least $d$, and let $\mathcal{T}$ be a uniformly random spanning tree of $G$. Then, for every tree $T$ it holds that $$\mathbb{P}(\mathcal{T}\cong T)\leq n^{-\Omega(1)}.$$
rows.reason: Named third-party conjecture (Lee) with a self-contained statement; the same-paper candidates (Conj 4.1/4.2) are distinct numbered conjectures, not this one; probability + n^{-Omega(1)} growth flag asymptotics. [verify downgraded: Identical proposition to the already-tracked Conjecture 4.1 (arxiv:2603.17630#00): a min-degree-d graph's uniformly random spanning tree is isomorphic to any fixed tree with probability n^{-Ω(1)}.]

rows.statement: For all positive integers $l$ and $k$, $f(l, k) = l^{k-1}$.
rows.reason: Statement 'f(l,k)=l^{k-1}' references an undefined paper-specific function f, so it is not self-contained and cannot stand alone; no candidates.

rows.statement: Every graph with chromatic number $k$ contains a weak immersion of the complete graph $K_{k}$.
rows.reason: Famous named conjecture (immersion analogue of Hadwiger): chromatic number k forces a weak K_k immersion; no opg row and the arxiv Conjecture 1.8 candidate is unverifiable, so promote rather than alias; needs a weak-immersion primitive.

rows.canonical_name: Letzter–Morrison Problem on Zero-Sum Directed Cycles
rows.statement: Prove an asymptotically optimal upper bound for $n(\mathbb{Z}_p^d)$, the smallest $n$ such that every $\mathbb{Z}_p^d$-labelling of the complete digraph on $n$ vertices contains a zero-sum directed cycle.
rows.reason: Open-ended Problem asking to 'prove an asymptotically optimal upper bound' for n(Z_p^d) with no crisp conjectured value; research direction, not a standalone proposition.

rows.statement: $p(t)$ tends to $0$ as $t$ grows.
rows.reason: Statement 'p(t) tends to 0' references an undefined paper-specific quantity p(t), not self-contained; no candidates.

rows.canonical_name: Li et al. Conjecture on Flow-Critical Graphs
rows.statement: For any connected-flow-critical graph $G$ on at least seven vertices with $n_3$ vertices of degree 3, we have $|E(G)| < \frac{5|V(G)|}{2} + n_3$.
rows.reason: Named third-party conjecture giving an edge-count bound |E(G)| < 5|V(G)|/2 + n_3 for connected-flow-critical graphs; candidates (circular flow number rows, same-paper Problem 1.6) are distinct; flow-critical definition needs nowhere-zero flows. [verify downgraded: Paper-local numbered conjectures with vague attribution ('Li et al.') bounding edges of connected-flow-critical graphs; value is as endpoint for 2205.07498/2502.01451; circular-flow-number candidates are unrelated.]

rows.statement: For every $k \in \mathbb{N}$ there exists an integer $g(k) \in \mathbb{N}$ such that every digraph $D$ with $\delta^+(D) \geq g(k)$ contains $k$ vertex-disjoint directed cycles of pairwise distinct lengths.
rows.reason: Named third-party conjecture: high min out-degree forces k vertex-disjoint directed cycles of pairwise distinct lengths; distinct from the Erdős–Pósa long-directed-cycles candidate; all primitives (digraph, out-degree, disjoint cycles, lengths) are standard.

rows.statement: For every forest $H$, there exists $\varepsilon > 0$ such that for every graph $G$ with $|G| > 1$ that is both $H$-free and $\bar{H}$-free, there is a pure pair $(A, B)$ with $|A|, |B| \geq \varepsilon|G|$.
rows.reason: Named third-party conjecture on linear-size pure pairs in H-free and complement-H-free graphs (forest H); no candidate matches; needs a pure-pair primitive.

rows.canonical_name: Lin et al. Conjecture on t-Minimal Trees
rows.statement: For $t \geq 88$, a $t$-minimal tree has $t + \lfloor t/11 \rfloor - 1$ vertices.
rows.reason: Statement relies on the undefined paper-specific notion 't-minimal tree', so it is not self-contained; candidates are all unrelated Erdős problems.

rows.statement: Let $G$ be a graph of maximum degree $\Delta$. Then, $la(G) \leq \left\lceil \frac{\Delta + 1}{2} \right\rceil$.
rows.reason: Famous named conjecture (Akiyama–Exoo–Harary): la(G) <= ceil((Delta+1)/2); candidates are unrelated coloring rows and none is the linear arboricity conjecture; needs a linear-arboricity primitive.

rows.canonical_name: Linial's Problem on Topological Turán Numbers for 3-Graphs
rows.statement: For any 3-graph $H$, does there exist a constant $C_H > 0$ such that any $n$-vertex 3-graph with at least $C_H n^{\alpha}$ edges (for some universal exponent $\alpha$ independent of $H$) contains a homeomorphic copy of $H$?
rows.reason: Open Problem asking whether a universal exponent alpha exists forcing homeomorphic copies in 3-graphs; no specified value, heavy hypergraph-topology + asymptotics, not a crisp standalone target.

rows.statement: Every simple graph $G$ satisfies $\chi'(G) = \chi'_\ell(G)$ and $\chi''(G) = \chi''_\ell(G)$.
rows.reason: Conjunction of the list edge coloring conjecture (chi'=chi'_l) and list total coloring conjecture (chi''=chi''_l); primary named conjecture matches opg:edge_list_coloring_conjecture (total part is the already-tracked opg:list_total_colouring_conjecture).

rows.statement: If $G$ is a graph, then $\chi'_{\ell}(G) = \chi'(G)$.
rows.reason: chi'_l(G)=chi'(G) is verbatim the OPG edge list coloring conjecture (edge chromatic number equals list edge chromatic number).

rows.statement: $\chi'(G) = \mathrm{ch}'(G)$ for any graph $G$.
rows.attributed_to: unknown (discussed in references [1, 5, 18] of the paper)
rows.reason: chi'(G)=ch'(G) is the same proposition as opg:edge_list_coloring_conjecture (chromatic index equals list chromatic index).

rows.statement: There exists an absolute constant $c > 0$ such that every $K_t$-minor free graph $G$ satisfies $\chi_\ell(G) \leq ct$.
rows.reason: Exists c>0 with chi_l(G)<=ct for every K_t-minor-free graph matches opg:list_hadwiger_conjecture verbatim ('K_t-minor-free graphs are ct-list-colourable for some constant c').

rows.statement: [Verbatim statement not reproduced in the provided input; see H. Liu, R. Morris, and N. Prince, Discrete Math.\ 308 (2008), no.\ 22, 5096–5121.]
rows.reason: Verbatim statement was not reproduced in the input (only a bibliographic pointer), so there is no self-contained proposition to formalize.

rows.statement: There are only finitely many 3-connected triangle-free cubic $n$-vertex graphs $G$ with $\alpha(G) < \frac{3}{8} n$.
rows.reason: Named third-party conjecture (Locke 1986): only finitely many 3-connected triangle-free cubic graphs have alpha(G) < 3n/8; no candidate matches; needs an independence-number primitive.

rows.statement: The genus distribution of every graph is log-concave.
rows.reason: Famous named conjecture (Gross–Robbins–Tucker): every graph's genus distribution is log-concave; same-paper Conjecture 11 candidate is a distinct refinement; needs rotation-system/genus-distribution primitives.

rows.statement: How often is the sandpile group $S(G)$ cyclic, that is $\mu(G) = 1$?
rows.reason: Vague research question ('how often is the sandpile group cyclic') with no crisp proposition; candidates are all unrelated derived/arxiv rows.

rows.canonical_name: Lovász Conjecture on r-Partite Hypergraph Matchings
rows.statement: Every $r$-partite, $r$-uniform hypergraph with at least one edge contains $r-1$ vertices whose deletion reduces the matching number.
rows.reason: Named third-party conjecture: every r-partite r-uniform hypergraph with an edge has r-1 vertices whose deletion reduces the matching number; candidates (Lovász path-removal, vertex-transitive Hamiltonicity) are different Lovász conjectures; standard hypergraph-matching primitives.

rows.canonical_name: Lovász–Nešetřil–Pultr Problem on Product Dimension of Clique Factors
rows.statement: Estimate $Q(s, r)$, the product dimension of $K_s(r)$ (the vertex-disjoint union of $r$ copies of the complete graph $K_s$), for values of $s \geq 3$.
rows.reason: Open estimation Problem for Q(s,r) with no conjectured value; candidates (Erdős–Faber–Lovász, product-structure) are unrelated; research direction, not a standalone proposition.

rows.statement: There exists an integer $p$ such that every $p$-strongly connected digraph has an even directed cycle.
rows.reason: Named third-party conjecture: exists p such that every p-strongly-connected digraph has an even directed cycle; opg/derived candidates are different (path-removal, Erdős–Pósa) and the arxiv Conjecture 1.2 candidate is unverifiable; standard digraph primitives.

rows.statement: There exists a least integer $\mathrm{mader}_{\delta^+}(TT_k)$ such that every digraph $D$ with $\delta^+(D) \geq \mathrm{mader}_{\delta^+}(TT_k)$ contains a subdivision of $TT_k$.

rows.statement: For every acyclic digraph $F$ there exists a number $K=K(F)$ such that every digraph $D$ with $\delta^{+}(D)\geq K$ contains a subdivision of $F$.
rows.reason: General named Mader conjecture (for every acyclic digraph F, large out-degree forces a subdivision of F) is strictly broader than the TT_k OPG row and not otherwise tracked. [verify downgraded: The general 'every acyclic digraph F' form is equivalent to the transitive-tournament OPG row: a subdivision of TT_k (k=|V(F)|) contains a subdivision of any acyclic F, and conversely, so it is the same proposition (prefer the OPG target over the arxiv TT_k copy).]

rows.statement: Is there a characterisation of digraphs that have a majority 2-coloring, or a polynomial time algorithm to recognise such digraphs?
rows.attributed_to: Kreutzer, Oum, Seymour, van der Zypen and Wood
rows.reason: Identical wording to Open Problem 5 of the source paper 'Majority Colourings of Digraphs' already tracked as arxiv:1608.03040#05.

rows.canonical_name: Mandache's Question on the Optimal Exponent for m(δ)
rows.statement: What is the optimal exponent $\alpha$ such that $m(\delta) \sim \delta^\alpha$ as $\delta \to 0$, where $m(\delta)$ denotes the minimum triforce density $\int_{[0,1]^6} W(x',y,z)W(x,y',z)W(x,y,z')\,dx\,dy\,dz\,dx'\,dy'\,dz'$ among all measurable $W:[0,1]^3\to[0,1]$ with $\int W = \delta$?
rows.reason: A graphon-integral asymptotic-exponent determination question (analysis/measure theory) with no matching candidate, out of scope for the Rocq graph-theory campaign.

rows.statement: Prove a nontrivial lower bound for $\chi_c(G(n, 1/2))$ with high probability.
rows.reason: Vague 'prove a nontrivial lower bound for chi_c(G(n,1/2))' with no crisp threshold and no matching candidate row.

rows.statement: There are no $(r,t)$-Ruzsa-Szemer\'edi graphs with both $rt = \Theta\!\left(\binom{n}{2}\right)$ and $r \geq n^{\Omega(1)}$.
rows.reason: Named third-party conjecture (no (r,t)-RS graph with rt=Theta(n^2) and r>=n^Omega(1)); none of the discrepancy/hypercube candidates match.

rows.statement: $\operatorname{cop}(G)=\mathcal{O}(\sqrt{n})$ for every $n$-vertex connected graph $G$.
rows.reason: Famous standalone conjecture cop(G)=O(sqrt(n)); no candidate rows offered, self-contained asymptotic statement.

rows.statement: What is the computational complexity of computing a maximum independent set in even-hole-free graphs?
rows.reason: Same open complexity problem ('the complexity of computing a maximum independent set in an even-hole-free graph is not known') already tracked as arxiv:1912.11246#01.

rows.statement: For every simple graph $G$, all $(\Delta(G) + 2)$-edge-colourings are Kempe-equivalent.
rows.reason: Named Mohar conjecture with self-contained statement on Kempe equivalence of edge-colourings; no candidates, distinct from the vertex-colouring version.

rows.canonical_name: Mohar's Conjecture (Kempe equivalence of k-colourings of k-regular graphs)
rows.statement: For $k \geq 3$, all $k$-colourings of a $k$-regular graph that is not complete are Kempe equivalent.
rows.reason: Named Mohar conjecture on Kempe equivalence of k-colourings of non-complete k-regular graphs; Erdős/Question candidates are unrelated.

rows.statement: The only snark that is embeddable on the projective plane is the Petersen graph.

rows.canonical_name: Mubayi's Question on the Triangle–Book Tradeoff
rows.statement: For each $\beta \in (1/6, 1/4)$, give a more precise determination of the optimal $\gamma = \gamma(\beta) > 0$ such that every graph on $n$ vertices with at least $\lfloor n^2/4 \rfloor + 1$ edges and book number at most $\beta n$ contains at least $\gamma n^3$ triangles.
