rows.statement: Let $G$ be a connected graph and let $B_1, \ldots, B_k$ be all its non-complete blocks of respective orders $n_1, \ldots, n_k$. Then $$\eta(G) \geq \sum_{i=1}^{k}(2n_i - 6).$$
rows.reason: Named 2012 conjecture eta(G)=Sz(G)-W(G) >= sum(2n_i-6) over non-complete blocks; distinct from the paper's Conjecture 5 (eta>=2n for 2-connected).

rows.canonical_name: Nassar–Yuster Problem (acyclic k-chromatic subgraph of tournaments)
rows.statement: What is the minimum integer $g\mathopen{}\mathclose{{}\left(k}\right)$ such that every $g\mathopen{}\mathclose{{}\left(k}\right)$-vertex tournament has an acyclic $k$-chromatic subgraph?
rows.reason: Paper-local Problem 1.2 value-determination question, distinct from the paper's Problem 1.1 (chromatic oriented graphs) and Conjecture 4.1 (asymptotic bound).

rows.statement: Given an $m \times (n+1)$ $\{0,1\}$-matrix $D$ (which may or may not itself be dope), does there exist a polynomial $P$ and an $m$-tuple $\Lambda$ of distinct complex numbers such that $D$ appears in $D_P(\Lambda)$ as the submatrix consisting of the first $n+1$ columns?
rows.reason: A {0,1}-matrix/polynomial-evaluation extension question (additive/algebraic number theory), not graph theory; candidates unrelated.

rows.canonical_name: Nathanson's Open Question 5 (Tightness of Degree Bound)
rows.statement: Is the degree bound $\deg(P) \leq m(n+2)$ in the extension problem (Theorem 7) essentially tight?
rows.reason: Paper-local tightness question about a polynomial degree bound deg(P)<=m(n+2), same out-of-scope matrix-polynomial setting as cid 398.

rows.canonical_name: Neumann-Lara's Problem on the Maximum Dichromatic Number of Oriented Graphs
rows.statement: What is $\vec{\chi}(n)=\max\{\vec{\chi}(D)\mid D\text{ is an oriented graph of order $n$}\}$?
rows.reason: Merge of three paper-local numbered Neumann-Lara records with a value-determination rep ('what is chi_vec(n)'); no single clean proposition and the derived twin-width candidate does not match.

rows.statement: If $G$ is a planar graph, then $\vec{\chi}(G) \leq 2$.
rows.reason: Famous clean conjecture that every planar graph has dichromatic number <= 2; the derived small-case tournament row is not the general statement. [verify downgraded: 'Every planar digraph has dichromatic number <= 2' is exactly the OPG Two Colour Conjecture (2-partition of an orientation of a planar graph into acyclic sets); the fuzzy matcher only offered a narrow derived tournament cell, but the OPG row is the true match and already formalized in v1.]

rows.canonical_name: Nikiforov–Rousseau Question on the Book p-Goodness Threshold
rows.statement: What is the best possible $n$ (in terms of $k$ and $p$) such that $B_{k,n}$ is $p$-good, i.e., such that $r(K_p, B_{k,n}) = (p-1)(n-1)+1$?
rows.reason: Value-determination question ('best possible n for which B_{k,n} is p-good') with no conjectured formula; the same-paper candidates are different sub-questions.

rows.canonical_name: Nikiforov–Rousseau–Schelp Conjecture on Quasirandomness of Extremal Book Colourings
rows.statement: For any $k \geq 2$ and any $0 < \theta < \tfrac{1}{2}$, there exists $c = c(\theta, k) > 0$ such that if a $2$-coloring of $K_N$ is not $\theta$-quasirandom for $N$ sufficiently large, then it contains a monochromatic $B^{(k)}_n$ with $n = (2^{-k} + c)N$.
rows.reason: Named three-author conjecture with a self-contained statement linking non-quasirandom 2-colourings to large monochromatic books; no candidate matches.

rows.statement: A hereditary class of structures is nowhere FO dense if and only if first-order model checking is fixed-parameter tractable on it, equivalently, if and only if the class is monadically NIP.
rows.reason: Named conjecture equating nowhere-FO-dense with FPT FO model checking and monadic NIP (parameterized complexity + finite model theory), out of scope; candidates are different logic questions.

rows.canonical_name: NP-completeness of k-Inversion for k >= 3 (Bang-Jensen–da Silva–Havet)
rows.statement: For each $k \geq 3$, the problem of deciding whether a general oriented graph $D$ satisfies $\operatorname{inv}(D) \leq k$ is NP-complete.
rows.reason: NP-completeness assertion requiring a computation/reduction model not available in this campaign; no matching candidate.

rows.statement: For any $n \geq 4$ and $\ell \geq \lfloor n/2 \rfloor + 1$, any two trees on $n$ vertices with the same $\ell$-deck are isomorphic.
rows.reason: Named conjecture on ℓ-deck reconstruction of trees, strictly more specific than the general Reconstruction Conjecture OPG row; needs an ℓ-deck primitive.

rows.canonical_name: O(n²) Edge Bound Conjecture for Separated Multigraphs
rows.statement: Any separated single-crossing topological multigraph with $n$ vertices has at most $O(n^2)$ edges.
rows.reason: Named Kaufmann–Pach–Tóth–Ueckerdt conjecture on edge counts of separated single-crossing topological multigraphs; no candidate matches, requires formalizing drawings/crossings.

rows.canonical_name: O(n log n) Mixing Time Conjecture for Glauber Dynamics
rows.statement: The Glauber dynamics for $k$-colourings of a graph $G$ with maximum degree $\Delta$ has mixing time $O(n \log n)$, provided that $k \geq \Delta + 2$.
rows.reason: Named (Jerrum; Salas–Sokal) but the claim is a Markov-chain mixing-time bound (probability/statistical physics) with no natural graph-theory repo and no matching candidate, so it is out-of-scope and parked.

rows.canonical_name: Complexity of Odd Cycle Transversal in P5-free Graphs
rows.statement: What is the computational complexity of the Odd Cycle Transversal problem in $P_5$-free graphs?
rows.reason: Paper-local 'Problem 4.4' with garbled attribution posing a computational-complexity question studied by 2004.09425; value is as an implication-edge endpoint, not a standalone statement (the triangle-free OCT candidates do not match).

rows.statement: Let $G$ be a graph. If $\chi(G)\geqslant t$, then $G$ has $K_{t}$ as an odd minor. Equivalently, if $G$ does not have $K_{t}$ as an odd minor, then $\chi(G)<t$.
rows.reason: Famous named conjecture (Gerards–Seymour: chi(G)>=t implies an odd K_t minor) with no existing corpus row (list/fractional Hadwiger differ, and candidate 2206.13635#00 is the hypergraph Hadwiger bound); promoted (duplicate of the same conjecture at cid 411, dedupe downstream). [verify downgraded: Same proposition as cluster 411 (Gerards–Seymour): 'chi(G)>=t implies an odd K_t-minor' is the contrapositive of 'no odd K_t-minor implies (t-1)-colourable'; the two clusters split only on the possessive-s, so this aliases the canonical promoted row from cid 411.] [reclassified: no exact corpus row; promote]

rows.statement: For every integer $t \geq 2$, if $G$ is a graph not containing an odd $K_t$-minor, then $G$ is properly $(t-1)$-colorable.
rows.reason: Same famous Gerards–Seymour odd-minor conjecture as cid 410 (3 studying papers, canonical phrasing), no existing S1/S2/S5 alias target among candidates, so promoted as the canonical Odd Hadwiger row.

rows.canonical_name: Aboulker–Aubian–Charbit–Lopes Question on Certifying Tournament Clique Number
rows.statement: Is large clique number in tournaments always certified by a bounded-size set? Specifically, if $T$ is a tournament of sufficiently large clique number $\overrightarrow{\omega}(T)$, must $T$ contain a subtournament of large clique number belonging to one of two simple families of tournaments?
rows.reason: Externally attributed question but the statement references undefined paper-specific 'two simple families of tournaments', so it is not self-contained (the Erdős candidates 944/75/151/130 are unrelated).

rows.canonical_name: Abu-Khazneh–Barát–Pokrovskiy–Szabó Question on Covers of Intersecting r-partite Hypergraphs
rows.statement: Does there exist a constant $K$ such that for every $r$ there exists an intersecting $r$-partite $r$-uniform hypergraph $H$ with $\tau(H) \geq r - K$?
rows.reason: Named third-party, self-contained Ryser-adjacent question (exists K, for all r an intersecting r-partite r-uniform H with tau(H)>=r-K) not matched by any candidate; needs the hypergraph transversal-number primitive.

rows.canonical_name: Agrawal et al. Question on WML k-CIS in P5-free Graphs
rows.statement: Can the WML$k$CIS problem be solved in polynomial time for $P_5$-free graphs (and more generally for $(P_5+rK_1)$-free graphs)?
rows.attributed_to: Agrawal, Lima, Lokshtanov, Rzążewski, Saurabh, and Sharma
rows.reason: Complexity (poly-time solvability) question about the paper-specific problem WML k-CIS; out-of-scope and not self-contained despite external attribution.

rows.canonical_name: Aldous–McDiarmid–Scott Conjecture on Random Multicommodity Flow in the Hypercube
rows.statement: For the $d$-dimensional hypercube $Q_d$ with independent random edge-capacities each distributed like $C$, the limiting optimal uniform multicommodity flow volume depends only on $E[C]$ (and not on the full distribution of $C$).
rows.reason: Named conjecture but its content is a limiting/asymptotic statement about random edge-capacities (probability), out-of-scope for the graph-theory repos and unmatched by candidates.

rows.statement: Is it true that every connected graph $G$ has a tree decomposition whose indexing tree is a subgraph of $G$, and whose width is bounded by some function of the pathwidth of $G$?
rows.attributed_to: Blanco, Cook, Hatzel, Hilaire, Illingworth, and McCarty
rows.reason: Named third-party, self-contained structural question (every connected graph has a tree-decomposition whose tree is a subgraph, width bounded by a function of pathwidth) with no matching candidate.

rows.statement: Is there a critical exponent $\gamma > 0$ such that, for some constant $C > 0$, $\mathbb{E}[\tau] \sim C(1/2 - p)^{-\gamma}$ as $p \nearrow 1/2$, for a large family of parking processes?
rows.attributed_to: Damron, Gravner, Junge, Lyu, and Sivakooff
rows.reason: A bundle of probability/statistical-physics conjectures (finiteness of E[tau], growth rate, critical exponent) about a vaguely specified 'large family of parking processes', out-of-scope with no candidates.

rows.canonical_name: Dvořák–Yepremyan Question on Clique Minors in K_s-free Graphs
rows.statement: Does every $K_s$-free $n$-vertex graph $G$ with $\alpha(G) \leq r$ (with $n/r$ large enough) have a clique minor of order polynomially larger than $n/r$?
rows.reason: Named third-party, self-contained Hadwiger-type question (K_s-free graphs with bounded independence number have clique minors polynomially larger than n/r) unmatched by candidates; the 'polynomially larger' claim is asymptotic.

rows.canonical_name: Esperet–Raymond Question on Improvability of Expansion Bounds
rows.statement: Can either of the bounds $\max\!\left(\tfrac{1}{2\varepsilon}-1,\,0\right)\leq b_\varepsilon\leq\tfrac{1}{\varepsilon}-1$ and $B_\varepsilon\leq\tfrac{1}{\varepsilon}-1$ be improved (in particular, in terms of the multiplicative constants in the exponents)?
rows.reason: References paper-specific quantities b_epsilon and B_epsilon and asks only whether the stated bounds 'can be improved', so it is not a self-contained proposition (the Esperet-conjecture candidate 2202.05557#02 is a different statement).

rows.canonical_name: Esperet–Labourel–Ochem Problem on Induced Universal Graphs of Bounded Degree
rows.statement: Close the gap between the lower bound $g_v(\mathcal{H}(n,\Delta)) \geq c\, n^{\Delta/2}$ and the upper bound $O(n^{\Delta/2+1/2-1/\Delta})$ for $g_v(\mathcal{H}(n,\Delta))$ when $\Delta$ is odd.
rows.reason: A 'close the gap / determine the exponent' problem for g_v(H(n,Delta)) with odd Delta rather than a crisp true/false conjecture, and no candidate matches (the other 'gap' candidates are unrelated).

rows.canonical_name: Illingworth–Scott–Wood Problem on Absolute-Constant Treewidth in Product Structure
rows.statement: Is $f(\mathcal{G})$ upper bounded by an absolute constant, where $f(\mathcal{G})$ is the minimum integer such that for some $c$, every $n$-vertex graph $G\in\mathcal{G}$ is contained in $H\boxtimes K_{\!m}$ for some graph $H$ with treewidth at most $f(\mathcal{G})$, with $m\leqslant c\sqrt{n}$?
rows.reason: The general version of the ISW product-structure problem, quantifying the parameter f over arbitrary graph classes with notation-overloaded phrasing, is too abstract to be a single self-contained proposition (the concrete planar case is cid 422).

rows.canonical_name: Illingworth–Scott–Wood Problem on Planar Graph Product Structure
rows.statement: Is $f(\mathcal{L})\leqslant 2$, where $\mathcal{L}$ is the class of planar graphs?
rows.reason: Concrete named conjecture (f(planar)<=2: planar graphs embed in H box K_m with tw(H)<=2 and m<=c*sqrt(n)) with a self-contained statement not matched by any candidate; requires planarity. [verify downgraded: rep_statement 'Is f(L)≤2' is fragmentary — the parameter f is undefined here, so the proposition cannot stand alone; candidate 2208.10074#00 is a different separator-based product-structure conjecture.]

rows.canonical_name: Kamčev–Liebenau–Wood–Yepremyan Question on Size-Ramsey Numbers of Planar Graphs
rows.statement: What are general bounds on the size-Ramsey numbers of bounded-degree planar graphs?
rows.reason: An open 'what are general bounds' determination question rather than a self-contained proposition; the planar-graph candidates concern unrelated parameters.

rows.canonical_name: Li–Ma–Shi–Wang–Wu Question on Edge Density of 3-Flow-Critical Graphs
rows.statement: What is the minimum number of edges in an $n$-vertex $3$-flow-critical graph (other than $K_2$ and $K_4$)?
rows.attributed_to: Li, Ma, Shi, Wang, and Wu
rows.reason: Asks to determine the minimum edge count of 3-flow-critical graphs with no conjectured value, so it is an extremal-determination question rather than a crisp promotable statement (candidates unrelated).

rows.canonical_name: Liu–Wood Question on Clustered 3-Colouring of Planar Graphs
rows.statement: Is it true that planar graphs in which no two vertices have many common neighbors are $3$-colorable with bounded clustering?
rows.reason: Paper-local '(Theorem 3)' record with a vague quantifier ('many common neighbours'); real and studied but its value is as an implication-edge endpoint (no candidate matches).

rows.canonical_name: Watanabe–Ishii–Sawa Question on N3 of Odd Cycles
rows.statement: Does the formula for $N_3(C_{2n+1})$ established by Watanabe, Ishii, and Sawa hold for all (larger) values of $n$?
rows.reason: Asks whether an unstated paper-specific formula for N_3(C_{2n+1}) extends to larger n; not self-contained and no candidate matches.

rows.canonical_name: Araujo–Havet–Linhares Sales–Silva Conjecture on Bounded Proper Orientation Number of Outerplanar Graphs
rows.statement: All outerplanar graphs $G$ have bounded $\vec{\chi}(G)$.
rows.attributed_to: Araujo, Havet, Linhares Sales, and Silva
rows.reason: Named third-party conjecture with a self-contained statement (outerplanar graphs have bounded proper orientation number) unmatched by the cop-number/queue-number candidates; needs the proper-orientation-number primitive.

rows.statement: $\gamma_d = \frac{1}{d+1}$
rows.reason: The statement 'gamma_d = 1/(d+1)' references an undefined paper-specific constant gamma_d, so it is not self-contained; no candidates.

rows.canonical_name: Alon–Ben-Eliezer–Shangguan–Tamo Question on Degeneracy Bound for Hat Guessing Number
rows.statement: Does there exist a function $f : \mathbb{N} \to \mathbb{N}$ such that if $G$ is $d$-degenerate, then $\mathrm{HG}(G) \leq f(d)$?
rows.reason: Identical proposition to the tracked row arxiv:2107.05995#00 (same paper): exists f with HG(G)<=f(d) for every d-degenerate G.

rows.canonical_name: Bosek–Dudek–Farnik–Grytczuk–Mazur Conjecture: Hat Guessing Number at most Hadwiger Number
rows.statement: The hat guessing number of any graph is at most its Hadwiger number, that is, the order of the largest clique minor of the graph.
rows.attributed_to: Bosek, Dudek, Farnik, Grytczuk, and Mazur
rows.reason: Named third-party conjecture with a self-contained statement (HG(G) is at most the order of the largest clique minor) not matched by the same-paper candidate rows; needs the hat-guessing-number primitive.

rows.canonical_name: Erdős–Sárközy Question on a Homogeneous Szemerédi–Vu Theorem
rows.statement: Is there a homogeneous version of the Szemerédi–Vu theorem, i.e., does the conclusion that $\Sigma(A)$ contains a long arithmetic progression hold under the additional requirement that the progression is witnessed monochromatically (or homogeneously) with respect to a coloring of $A$?
rows.reason: An out-of-scope additive-number-theory question about a vaguely specified 'homogeneous/monochromatic' strengthening of subset-sum APs; not self-contained and no candidate (erdos:71/717) matches.

rows.statement: $L(d)=\binom{d}{\lfloor d/2\rfloor}$
rows.reason: Statement is not self-contained (L(d) undefined) and belongs to extremal set theory / the daisy problem, so it cannot stand alone.

rows.canonical_name: Planar hat guessing number conjecture (Bosek et al.)
rows.statement: The maximum possible hat guessing number of a planar graph is $4$.
rows.attributed_to: Bosek, Dudek, Farnik, Grytczuk, and Mazur
rows.reason: Named self-contained conjecture (max hat guessing number of a planar graph equals 4) not matched by any candidate; requires the hat-guessing-number primitive.

rows.canonical_name: Constant bound on the hat guessing number of planar graphs
rows.statement: Whether or not planar graphs allow for a constant bound on their hat guessing number.
rows.attributed_to: Alon and Chizewer, Bosek et al., Bradshaw
rows.reason: Phrased as a yes/no existence question and subsumed by the sharper promoted conjecture (cid 433), so it does not stand alone.

rows.canonical_name: Complexity of deciding digraph degreewidth at most 1 (Keeney–Lokshtanov)
rows.statement: What is the computational complexity of deciding whether a digraph has degreewidth at most $1$?
rows.reason: An open computational-complexity classification question, out of scope for the statement waves.

rows.canonical_name: Bounded proper orientation number of planar graphs (Araújo–Havet–Linhares Sales–Silva)
rows.statement: Which classes of graphs have bounded proper orientation number? In particular, is $\overrightarrow{\chi}(G)$ bounded by a constant for all planar graphs $G$?
rows.attributed_to: Araujo, Havet, Linhares Sales, and Silva
rows.reason: Recognized formalizable open conjecture that planar graphs have bounded proper orientation number, not matched by any candidate; needs the proper-orientation-number primitive.

rows.canonical_name: FPT status of List Edge Chromatic Number and List Total Chromatic Number
rows.statement: Are List Edge Chromatic Number and List Total Chromatic Number fixed-parameter tractable when parameterised by the treewidth bound $k$?
rows.attributed_to: Fellows, Fomin, Lokshtanov, Rosamond, Saurabh, Szeider and Thomassen
rows.reason: An FPT/complexity open question, out of scope for the mathematical statement waves.

rows.canonical_name: FPT status of List Hamilton Path
rows.statement: Is List Hamilton Path fixed-parameter tractable when parameterised by the pathwidth of the graph?
rows.attributed_to: Fellows, Fomin, Lokshtanov, Rosamond, Saurabh, Szeider and Thomassen
rows.reason: FPT/complexity open question parameterized by pathwidth, out of scope for the statement waves.

rows.canonical_name: Complexity of Near-Bipartiteness for diameter-3 graphs
rows.statement: What is the complexity of Near-Bipartiteness for graphs of diameter 3?
rows.attributed_to: Yang and Yuan; Brandstädt et al.
rows.reason: Computational-complexity open question, out of scope for the statement waves.

rows.statement: For any finite family $\mathcal{F}$ of ordered binary matrices and any $\varepsilon > 0$ there exists $\delta = \delta(\mathcal{F}, \varepsilon)$ such that any $n \times n$ binary matrix which is $\varepsilon$-far from $\mathcal{F}$-freeness contains at least $\delta n^{a+b}$ copies of some $a \times b$ matrix from $\mathcal{F}$.
rows.reason: Named self-contained removal-lemma conjecture (essentially ordered bipartite removal) not matched by candidates, which are only its follow-up open problems; inherently asymptotic (ε-far, δ·n^{a+b}).

rows.canonical_name: Ortega–Rué–Serra conjecture on k-product-free sets in the free group
rows.statement: Let $k \geq 2$ be an integer, $A$ be a finite set, and $F$ be the free group with alphabet $A$. If $S \subset F$ is $k$-product-free, then $d^*(S) \leq 1/\rho(k)$, where $\rho(k) = \min\{\ell \in \mathbb{Z}^+ : \ell \nmid k-1\}$.
rows.reason: Named conjecture but in free-group/additive combinatorics (upper density in the free group), out of scope for the graph-theory corpus.

rows.canonical_name: Ossona de Mendez–Oum–Wood defective chromatic number conjecture
rows.statement: For every graph $H$, $\chi_{\Delta}(\mathcal{M}_H) = \mathrm{td}(H) - 1$.
rows.attributed_to: Ossona de Mendez, Oum, and Wood
rows.reason: Recognized named conjecture that the defective chromatic number of H-minor-free graphs equals td(H)−1; needs defective-chromatic-number and tree-depth primitives.

rows.statement: $f(H) = \mathrm{td}(H) - 1$.
rows.attributed_to: Ossona de Mendez, Oum, and Wood
rows.reason: Same conjecture as cid 442 but stated fragmentarily (f(H)=td(H)−1 with f undefined), so it is a duplicate parked in favor of the promoted row.

rows.canonical_name: Pach–Tóth conjecture (topological graphs with no k pairwise disjoint edges)
rows.statement: For every fixed $k$, the maximum number of edges in an $n$-vertex simple topological graph with no $k$ pairwise disjoint edges is at most $O_k(n)$.
rows.reason: Named self-contained conjecture bounding edges of simple topological graphs with no k pairwise disjoint edges, not matched by candidates; requires plane-drawing/topological-graph machinery.

rows.statement: The Exact Matching problem (EM) is NP-complete.
rows.reason: A complexity-theoretic (NP-completeness) conjecture, out of scope for the graph-theory statement corpus.

rows.statement: Is \textsc{b-Chromatic Core}, parameterized by $k$ (the order of the desired core), W[1]-hard or fixed-parameter tractable?
rows.reason: W[1]-hard-versus-FPT open question, out of scope for the statement waves.

rows.statement: Is \textsc{Grundy Coloring} fixed-parameter tractable, i.e., solvable in time $f(k) \cdot n^{O(1)}$, when parameterized by $k$ (the Grundy number)? Alternatively, does it admit an algorithm running in time $n^{k^{O(1)}}$?
rows.attributed_to: raised in multiple prior works (refs [37, 26, 20, 4] in the paper)
rows.reason: FPT open question about Grundy coloring parameterized by the Grundy number, out of scope for the statement waves.

rows.canonical_name: Pardey–Rautenbach conjecture on colour-balanced perfect matchings
rows.statement: For all integers $k\geq 2$ and $t\geq 1$, every colour-balanced $k$-edge-coloured $K_{2kt}$ admits a perfect matching $M$ satisfying $f_{c}(M)=O(k^{2})$.
rows.reason: Paper-local numbered Conjecture 1.1 with an undefined primitive (f_c), best kept as an implication endpoint rather than a standalone statement target.

rows.canonical_name: Caro–Patkós–Tuza question on ex_{b,c} for families of trees
rows.statement: Determine $ex_{b,c}$ for specific families of trees.
rows.reason: Vague paper-local numbered request to 'determine ex_{b,c}' with a paper-specific undefined parameter, too fragmentary to stand alone.

rows.canonical_name: Patchell–Spiro conjecture (asymptotic optimality of CAT count)
rows.statement: The construction giving $(3/2)n^2 - O(n)$ occurrences of the word CAT in an $n \times n$ grid (allowing horizontal, vertical, and diagonal occurrences) is asymptotically optimal; that is, the maximum number of CAT occurrences is $(3/2)n^2 + o(n^2)$.
rows.reason: A combinatorics-on-words grid pattern-counting conjecture, out of scope for the graph-theory corpus.

rows.canonical_name: Patchell–Spiro conjecture on words with all distinct letters
rows.statement: For a word $w$ of length $r\geq 2$ in which all letters are distinct, the maximum number of occurrences of $w$ in a labeling of $(\mathbb{Z}/n\mathbb{Z})^d$ is at most $\bigl(3^{d-1}/(r-1)\bigr)n^d$, with equality achieved by an appropriate generalization of the Patchell–Spiro labeling.
rows.reason: Combinatorics-on-words counting problem on the discrete torus (Z/nZ)^d, out of scope for the graph-theory corpus.

rows.statement: Is there an $n_0 \in \mathbb{N}$ such that for all $n \geq n_0$, $R_n = [n, r(K_n)]$ and $\mathcal{R}^c_n = [\lceil \tfrac{4}{3}n \rceil - 1,\, r(K_n)]$?
rows.reason: Paper-local numbered Ramsey question using paper-specific range notation (R_n, R^c_n), best treated as an implication endpoint rather than a standalone target.

rows.canonical_name: Pavez-Signé–Piga–Sanhueza-Matamala conjecture on Ramsey numbers of k-connected graphs
rows.statement: For every $k \geq 2$ and every function $f : \mathbb{N} \to \mathbb{N}$ such that $n \leq f(n) \leq r(K_n)$ for all $n$, there is a sequence $(G_n)_{n \in \mathbb{N}}$ of $k$-connected graphs such that $|V(G_n)| = n$ and $r(G_n) = \Theta(f(n))$.
rows.reason: Self-contained named conjecture that any growth function n≤f(n)≤r(K_n) is realizable as r(G_n)=Θ(f(n)) by k-connected graphs; uses only standard Ramsey notation and not matched by candidates.

rows.statement: The complete graph $K_n$ has a perfect 1-factorization for all even $n > 2$.
rows.reason: Famous named conjecture that K_n has a perfect 1-factorization for all even n>2, not matched by the fuzzy 'perfect' candidates; needs the perfect-1-factorization primitive.

rows.statement: For any planar graph $G$ of maximum degree $\Delta \geq 5$, we have $\mathrm{la}(G) = \left\lceil \frac{\Delta}{2} \right\rceil$.
rows.reason: Named self-contained conjecture that la(G)=⌈Δ/2⌉ for planar G with Δ≥5, not matched by candidates; requires planarity and the linear-arboricity primitive.

rows.statement: Does there exist a constant $c>0$ such that every complete geometric graph on $n$ vertices can be partitioned into at most $cn$ plane matchings?
rows.attributed_to: Araujo, Dumitrescu, Hurtado, Noy, and Urrutia
rows.reason: Named problem (Araujo–Dumitrescu–Hurtado–Noy–Urrutia) on partitioning the complete geometric graph into O(n) plane (non-crossing) matchings; self-contained topological-graph statement not covered by the erdos/derived candidates, but needs a geometric-graph/non-crossing-matching primitive. [verify downgraded: Clean named problem but geometric (real-coordinate points, non-crossing straight-line matchings) — out-of-scope, consistent with sibling Problems 5.1/5.2 from the same paper (2507.10840) parked as out-of-scope.]

rows.statement: Every 3-connected plane graph with maximum face size $\Delta^\star$ has a cyclic coloring with at most $\Delta^\star + 2$ colors.
rows.reason: Named third-party conjecture on cyclic colouring of 3-connected plane graphs with Δ*+2 colours; candidates (Borodin–Kostochka, Reed list-analogue, etc.) are unrelated colouring conjectures, so it is new but requires plane-graph/face structure.

rows.statement: Every $3$-connected, internally $4$-connected pentagraph is three-colourable.
rows.reason: Named third-party conjecture that every 3-connected internally 4-connected pentagraph is 3-colourable; no candidates, self-contained modulo the specialized 'pentagraph' primitive.

rows.statement: The bound in the Bogolyubov–Ruzsa lemma can be improved to $c_r(K) = K^{-O_r(1)}$.
rows.reason: Famous named conjecture but additive combinatorics (Bogolyubov–Ruzsa bound), not graph theory; out-of-scope-v2, no statement owed. Candidates are only lexical 'polynomial' collisions.

rows.statement: For any graph $H$ there are constants $C_{H},D_{H}$ such that for any $0<\varepsilon\leq 1/2$, any $n$-vertex graph with at most $\varepsilon^{D_{H}}n^{|H|}$ induced copies of $H$ contains an $\varepsilon$-homogeneous set with at least $\varepsilon^{C_{H}}n$ vertices.
rows.reason: Named conjecture (Fox–Nguyen–Scott–Seymour) on ε-homogeneous sets from few induced H-copies; Erdős–Hajnal-flavoured, distinct from the Rödl conjectures, not matched by the erdos/opg candidates; asymptotic ε-power bounds.

rows.statement: For every graph $H$, there exists $d>0$ such that for every $\varepsilon\in(0,\frac{1}{2})$, and every $H$-free graph $G$, there is an $\varepsilon$-restricted subset of $V(G)$ with size at least $\varepsilon^{d}\lvert G\rvert$.
rows.reason: Flagship named conjecture (Fox–Sudakov): every H-free graph has an ε-restricted subset of size ε^d|G|; no candidate row matches; asymptotic polynomial bound.

rows.statement: Every proper hereditary class of graphs has the polynomial Rödl property.
rows.reason: Paper-local 'Conjecture 1.4' hereditary-class restatement of the same Fox–Sudakov Polynomial Rödl Conjecture (cid 461); near-duplicate, so it anchors the studying paper's edge rather than owning a separate statement.

rows.statement: Is the 3-coloring problem solvable in polynomial time in the class of $P_t$-free graphs, for every fixed $t > 7$?
rows.attributed_to: community open problem; surveyed in Golovach et al. [8]
rows.reason: Genuine graph-theory open problem the paper proves partial results toward, but a poly-time-algorithm (computation-model) question with no clean proposition to formalize; value is as the paper's implication endpoint.

rows.canonical_name: Polynomial-time Algorithm for the Crossing-Exchange-and-Smoothing Relation
rows.statement: For each fixed link $L$, there is a polynomial-time algorithm that solves $\succcurlyeq L$. That is, there is a polynomial $p(n)$ and an algorithm $A_L$ that takes as input any diagram $D$ with $n$ crossings, and answers whether or not $D \succcurlyeq L$ in at most $p(n)$ steps.
rows.reason: Attributed only to 'authors of reference [42]'; about link/knot diagrams plus a computation model — out-of-scope knot theory, not a graph statement.

rows.canonical_name: Polynomial-time Solvability of Totally Δ-modular Integer Programs
rows.statement: Can integer programs defined by totally $\Delta$-modular matrices be solved in polynomial time whenever $\Delta$ is a constant?
rows.reason: Totally Δ-modular = bounded-subdeterminant matrices; this is exactly the tracked 'bounded sub-determinant integer programs are polynomial-time solvable' conjecture (arxiv:1908.06300#00).

rows.statement: Determine $\dim(\Delta)$ asymptotically, where $\dim(\Delta)$ is the maximum dimension of a poset whose comparability graph has maximum degree at most $\Delta$. The known bounds are $\dim(\Delta) \geq \Omega(\Delta \log \Delta)$ and $\dim(\Delta) \leq O(\Delta \log^2 \Delta)$.
rows.attributed_to: Erdős, Kierstead, and Trotter; Füredi and Kahn
rows.reason: Named but open-ended determination problem ('determine dim(Δ) asymptotically') with only a Ω(Δ log Δ)–O(Δ log^2 Δ) gap, i.e. no crisp proposition to own; order-theory adjacent. Candidates unrelated.

rows.statement: The number of edges of every $r$-quasiplanar graph of $n$ vertices is $O_r(n)$.
rows.attributed_to: Combinatorial geometry community; see Brass, Moser, and Pach [BrMP05]
rows.reason: Classical named combinatorial-geometry conjecture (Brass–Moser–Pach): r-quasiplanar graphs have O_r(n) edges; no candidates; needs a topological-drawing/no-r-pairwise-crossing primitive.

rows.statement: Is there a mapping from Dictator to XOR that is $O(1)$-local and $O(1)$-bi-Lipschitz?
rows.reason: Analysis of Boolean functions (locality/bi-Lipschitz maps between Dictator and XOR), not graph theory; out-of-scope-v2 despite a same-paper informal-question candidate.

rows.canonical_name: Rao–Shinkar Question 6.2 (bi-Lipschitz Dictator→random balanced function mapping)
rows.statement: Is it true that with high probability there is a $O(1)$-bi-Lipschitz mapping from Dictator to a uniformly random balanced function $f$?
rows.reason: Boolean-function/property-testing question about bi-Lipschitz maps to a random balanced function; out-of-scope non-graph combinatorics; candidates are spurious matches.

rows.statement: Is there a mapping $\phi$ from XOR to Majority such that $\mathrm{avgStretch}(\phi) = O(\sqrt{n})$?
rows.reason: Boolean-function mapping/average-stretch question (XOR to Majority), not graph theory; out-of-scope-v2.

rows.canonical_name: Rao–Shinkar Question 6.4 (Lipschitz embedding realizing XOR as Majority)
rows.statement: Is there a Lipschitz embedding $\phi : \{0,1\}^n \to \{0,1\}^{\mathrm{poly}(n)}$ such that $\mathrm{XOR}(x) = \mathrm{Majority}(\phi(x))$ for all $x \in \{0,1\}^n$?
rows.reason: Lipschitz Boolean-cube embedding realizing XOR via Majority; analysis of Boolean functions, out-of-scope non-graph combinatorics.

rows.statement: Every poset tournament is a rebel.
rows.attributed_to: Chudnovsky, Kim, Liu, Seymour and Thomassé
rows.reason: Named conjecture (Chudnovsky–Kim–Liu–Seymour–Thomassé) in the Erdős–Hajnal-for-tournaments line ('every poset tournament is a rebel'); no candidate matches; needs the 'rebel'/'poset tournament' primitives.

rows.statement: For $n \geq 3$, two graphs $G$ and $H$ of order $n$ are isomorphic if and only if $\mathcal{D}(G) = \mathcal{D}(H)$.
rows.reason: Classic Kelly–Ulam reconstruction conjecture, exactly the v1 OPG row opg:reconstruction_conjecture.

rows.statement: Does every graph have a chordal partition such that each part is bipartite?
rows.reason: Third-party-attributed (Reed, Seymour) self-contained question — does every graph have a chordal partition with each part bipartite; candidates (Hoàng–Reed, hypergraph partition) do not match; needs a 'chordal partition' primitive.

rows.statement: If $G$ is a graph of maximum degree at most $\Delta$ with no clique of size greater than $\omega$, then $\chi(G) \leq \left\lceil \frac{1}{2}(\Delta + 1 + \omega) \right\rceil$.
rows.reason: χ(G) ≤ ⌈(Δ+1+ω)/2⌉ is exactly Reed's ω,Δ,χ conjecture, the v1 OPG row opg:reeds_omega_delta_and_chi_conjecture.

rows.statement: For every proper ideal $I$, there exists $c > 0$ such that every graph $G \in I$ satisfies $\omega(G)\alpha(G) \geq |G|^c$.
rows.reason: The 'for every proper ideal ω(G)α(G) ≥ |G|^c' statement is the standard equivalent reformulation of Erdős–Hajnal (single-graph form), i.e. opg:the_erdos_hajnal_conjecture, not the multicolour variant.

rows.statement: Given an integer $n \geq 5$ and a graph $G$ with crossing number at least $\operatorname{cr}(K_n)$, does it follow that the crossing number of its cone $CG$ is at least $\operatorname{cr}(K_{n+1})$?
rows.reason: Named yes/no question (Richter): cr(G)≥cr(K_n) ⟹ cr(CG)≥cr(K_{n+1}); the same-paper candidate 'Problem 1' is a distinct extremal-function (find f(k)) problem, so no true alias — promote as a self-contained crossing-number statement.

rows.statement: For every positive integer $c$, there exists an integer $D(c)$ such that every $c$-crossing-critical graph has maximum degree fewer than $D(c)$.
rows.reason: Named conjecture (Richter): c-crossing-critical graphs have bounded maximum degree D(c); the same-paper candidate is about optimal D_c values, a finer distinct question, so promote; needs a crossing-number/crossing-critical primitive.

rows.statement: For every surface $S$, there is a constant $r$ such that every 2-edge-connected graph embedded in $S$ with representativity at least $r$ has a nowhere-zero 4-flow.
rows.reason: Named conjecture (Robertson): high-representativity 2-edge-connected graphs embedded in any surface have a nowhere-zero 4-flow; candidates are circular/3-flow rows that do not match; needs nowhere-zero-flow plus surface-embedding primitives.

rows.statement: Every 2-edge-connected graph in the torus whose representativity is at least 3, has a nowhere-zero 4-flow.
rows.reason: Named third-party conjecture (Robertson) with self-contained statement; none of the flow candidates (3-flow, circular-flow) match; requires torus-embedding + representativity primitives plus nowhere-zero flow.

rows.canonical_name: Rossmanith's Conjecture on Planar Vertex Deletion
rows.statement: An upper bound of the form $f(m) \leq \frac{1}{6}m + o(m)$ holds for the class of planar graphs, where $f(m)$ is the least integer such that every $m$-edge graph $G$ has a set $X$ of at most $f(m)$ vertices with $G - X$ planar.
rows.reason: Named conjecture bounding the planar vertex-deletion number by m/6+o(m); no candidate matches; the o(m) asymptotic term is the dominant formalization blocker.

rows.statement: Does the ratio $G(n)/G(n-1)$ tend to $4$ as $n \to \infty$?
rows.reason: Open-ended ratio question whose sequence G(n) is undefined in the record; not a self-contained proposition.

rows.statement: Every $r$-partite $r$-uniform hypergraph $H$ satisfies $\tau(H) \leq (r-1)\nu(H)$, where $\tau(H)$ denotes the minimum size of a vertex cover and $\nu(H)$ denotes the maximum size of a set of pairwise disjoint edges in $H$.
rows.reason: OPG row states exactly tau(H) <= (r-1)nu(H) for r-uniform r-partite hypergraphs — identical proposition; prefer the opg target.

rows.canonical_name: Saad–Wolf Conjecture on Common Linear Equations
rows.statement: Let $k \geq 2$ be an integer. A linear equation of the form $a_1x_1 + \cdots + a_{2k}x_{2k} = 0$ is common in $\mathbb{F}_q^n$ if and only if we can partition $\{a_1, \ldots, a_{2k}\}$ into $k$ pairs, each summing to $0$.
rows.reason: Named additive-combinatorics conjecture about common linear equations in F_q^n; outside the graph-theory formalization scope and no matching candidate.

rows.statement: There are graphs with large girth that do not admit KT orientations.
rows.reason: Statement relies on the undefined paper-specific 'KT orientation' primitive; not self-contained as given.

rows.statement: Let $G$ be a graph of genus $g$. Then $c(G) \leq g + 3$.
rows.reason: Named conjecture c(G) <= g+3; the surface-genus cop rows in the corpus are continuous Riemannian analogues, not this discrete bound; needs genus + cop-number primitives.

rows.statement: Is it true that $R(p; m) = 2^{O(m)}$ for every fixed $p \geq 3$?
rows.reason: Classic named problem asking whether the multicolor Ramsey number R(p;m)=2^{O(m)}; erdos candidates 920/921/923/926 are unrelated; the 2^{O(m)} bound needs asymptotics.

rows.canonical_name: Scott's Problem on Odd-Subgraph Partitions of Random Graphs
rows.statement: For $n$ even, what is the probability that $G_{n,1/2}$ can be partitioned into two sets, each inducing odd subgraphs?
rows.reason: Open-ended 'what is the probability' question about G(n,1/2), paper-internal numbering; not a decidable self-contained proposition.

rows.statement: For every $k$, there exists $K$ such that if $T$ and $G$ are respectively a tournament and a graph on the same set of vertices with $G$ of chromatic number at least $K$, then there is a vertex $v$ such that $G$ induces on $N^+_T(v)$ a subgraph of $G$ of chromatic number at least $k$.
rows.reason: Named Scott–Seymour conjecture on chromatic number of out-neighborhoods; erdos candidates (cochromatic/infinite-cardinal) do not match; primitives (tournament, chromatic number, out-neighborhood) are all clean.

rows.statement: For every graph $H$, the class of graphs which do not contain any subdivision of $H$ as an induced subgraph is $\chi$-bounded.
rows.reason: Named Scott conjecture that induced-subdivision-free classes are chi-bounded; candidate arxiv:1907.04585#00 is an algorithmic QPTAS statement, not this; induced-subdivision primitive is the blocker.

rows.statement: For every apex-forest $H_1$ and outerplanar graph $H_2$ there is an integer $p$ such that every 2-connected graph of pathwidth at least $p$ contains $H_1$ or $H_2$ as a minor.
rows.reason: Named conjecture forcing an apex-forest or outerplanar minor by large pathwidth; the only candidate (Seymour's r-graph conjecture) is unrelated; needs pathwidth + minor primitives.

rows.statement: For every bipartite undirected graph $H$ and every undirected graph $G$, $t_H(G) \geq t_{K_2}(G)^{e(H)}$.
rows.reason: OPG row is the homomorphism-density form t_H(G) >= t_{K_2}(G)^{e(H)} for bipartite H — identical proposition; prefer the opg target.

rows.statement: For every integer $t\geq 1$, there exists a constant $C_{t}$ such that every even-hole-free graph $G$ with no clique of size $t$ satisfies $\operatorname{tw}(G)\leq C_{t}\log|V(G)|$.
rows.reason: Corpus row Conjecture 1.7 states exactly that (even hole, K_t)-free graphs have logarithmic treewidth = even-hole-free, no K_t, tw <= C_t log n; exact restatement of the studied conjecture.

rows.statement: For every integer $t$, there exists a constant $c_{t}$ such that every even-hole-free graph $G$ with no diamond and no clique of size $t$ satisfies $\operatorname{tw}(G)\leq c_{t}$.
rows.reason: Named conjecture that (even hole, diamond, K_t)-free graphs have constant treewidth; closest corpus rows fix K4 as a question (2001.01607#00) or use tree-alpha/log-treewidth (2305.16258#00/#01), so no exact match; treewidth is the primitive.

rows.canonical_name: Sitharam–Willoughby Conjecture on Excluded Minors for f_∞ <= 2
rows.statement: $W_4$ is an excluded minor for $f_\infty(G) \leq 2$, and $W_4$ is the only excluded minor for $f_\infty(G) \leq 2$.
rows.reason: Statement hinges on the undefined combinatorial-rigidity parameter f_∞; not self-contained and outside the graph-theory core scope.

rows.canonical_name: Sivaraman's Conjecture (Cop Number of P_t-Free Graphs)
rows.statement: Two cops can win on connected $P_5$-free graphs; more generally, the cop number of connected $P_t$-free graphs is at most $t-3$ for $t \geq 5$.
rows.reason: Named conjecture that connected P_t-free graphs have cop number <= t-3; no matching candidate; the cops-and-robbers game is a required primitive.

rows.statement: Every small hereditary class of graphs — containing at most $n!\cdot c^n$ distinct labeled $n$-vertex structures for some constant $c$ — has bounded twin-width.
rows.reason: Corpus row 'The Small Conjecture' states verbatim that every small hereditary class has bounded twin-width — identical proposition.

rows.statement: The smallest order $3$-cop-win planar graph is the dodecahedron.
rows.attributed_to: reference [5] in the paper (exact authors not identified in extracted text)
rows.reason: Specific auxiliary conjecture (smallest 3-cop-win planar graph is the dodecahedron) with unidentified attribution, tied to the studying paper's classification rows; value is as an implication endpoint.

rows.canonical_name: Smith's Conjecture (Longest Cycles in r-Connected Graphs)
rows.statement: For all $r\geq 2$, every two longest cycles in an $r$-connected graph meet in at least $r$ vertices.
rows.reason: Classic named conjecture that any two longest cycles in an r-connected graph share >= r vertices; no candidate matches; needs vertex-connectivity + longest-cycle primitives.

rows.canonical_name: Sokołowski's Conjecture (Polynomial Neighborhood Complexity of Planar Graphs)
rows.statement: There exists a constant $c$ such that $|\{N^{r}[v]\cap A\mid v\in V(G)\}|=\operatorname{\mathcal{O}}(r^{c}|A|)$ for every planar graph $G$, every nonempty subset $A$ of vertices, and every nonnegative integer $r$.
rows.reason: Named conjecture bounding r-neighborhood complexity by O(r^c|A|) for planar graphs; no candidate matches; the multi-parameter big-O bound needs asymptotics.

rows.canonical_name: Souza's Conjecture on Blowup Ramsey Numbers
rows.statement: In the bound $B(G \xrightarrow{r} H; t) \leq c^t$, the exponential constant $c$ can be chosen to depend only on $H$ and $r$, and not on $G$.
rows.reason: Named conjecture that the exponential base in blowup Ramsey numbers depends only on H and r; no candidate matches; requires the blowup-Ramsey-number / arrow primitive.

rows.statement: For every graph $H$ there exists $\varepsilon > 0$ such that every $H$-free graph $G$ with $|G| > 1$ contains a pure $(\varepsilon|G|, \varepsilon|G|)$-pair.
rows.reason: Named conjecture that every H-free graph contains a linear-size pure pair; no candidate matches; linear ε-fraction threshold with a definable pure-pair notion.

rows.statement: For $n \geq 6$ and $\ell \geq \lfloor n/2 \rfloor + 1$, the connectedness of an $n$-vertex graph $G$ is determined by $\mathcal{D}_\ell(G)$.
rows.reason: Attributed, precise reconstruction statement that connectedness is determined by the ℓ-deck for n>=6, ℓ>=floor(n/2)+1; no candidate matches; needs the ℓ-deck primitive. [verify downgraded: Tagged an informal 'belief' rather than a stated conjecture, and its statement hinges on paper-specific ℓ-deck reconstruction framing; no candidate matches (erdos:883 coprime graphs, coarse Menger, etc.).]

rows.statement: In every digraph $G$, there is a 2-kernel $K$ such that at least half the vertices of $G$ belong to $K$ or have an in-neighbour in $K$.
rows.reason: Named third-party (Spiro) conjectures on quasi-kernel coverage studied by 2409.05039; self-contained finite digraph statement, no candidate row matches (erdos:616 is hypergraph covering).

rows.statement: Is every graph's stack-number bounded by a function of its queue-number? Or is every graph's queue-number bounded by a function of its stack-number?
rows.reason: Famous named third-party open problem; self-contained (bounded-by-a-function disjunction), none of the queue-number candidate rows state it.

rows.statement: For every graph $G$, the genus polynomial $\Gamma_G(x)$ has only real roots.
rows.reason: Named third-party conjecture with a precise statement; no candidate rows, but genus polynomial + real-rootedness needs surface-embedding enumeration outside current scope.

rows.statement: Unit interval graphs have chromatic symmetric functions that expand positively in the $e$-basis of the ring $\mathrm{Sym}$ of symmetric functions.
rows.reason: Famous named conjecture, no candidate rows; e-positivity of chromatic symmetric functions requires symmetric-function theory outside the graph-formalization scope.

rows.statement: Every oriented graph $G$ with $\delta^{0}(G)>k/2$ contains every orientation of the $k$-edge path.
rows.reason: Named third-party (Stein) conjecture; the same-paper candidate Question 5.1 differs (≥ vs >, excludes antidirected orientations), so it is a distinct self-contained statement.

rows.statement: Every planar graph without 4- and 5-cycles is 3-colorable.
rows.reason: Famous named conjecture (planar graphs without 4- and 5-cycles are 3-colorable); the lone candidate is a correspondence-coloring row, not a match.

rows.statement: A tournament $H$ has the strong Erdős-Hajnal property if and only if it has a backedge graph that is a forest.
rows.reason: Substantive central characterization conjecture (strong EH iff backedge forest); distinct from the plain Erdős–Hajnal and the same-paper Gyárfás–Sumner candidate.

rows.statement: For every $c > 0$ and $\varepsilon > 0$, there is a threshold $n_0$, such that if $n \geq n_0$ then for any $n$-element subset of integers $A \subset \mathbb{N}$ and any graph $G_n$ with $n$ vertices and at least $n^{1+c}$ edges, $|A +_{G_n} A| + |A \cdot_{G_n} A| \geq |A|^{1+c-\varepsilon}$.
rows.reason: erdos:808 states exactly the graph-restricted sum-product bound; max(|A+_G A|,|A·_G A|)≥|A|^{1+c-ε} and the sum form are equivalent under the ε-formulation.

rows.statement: Let $G$ be a graph with $\chi(G) = t$, and let $S$ be a colorful set in $G$. Then $G$ contains an $S$-rooted $K_t$-minor.
rows.reason: Named third-party strengthening of Hadwiger (S-rooted K_t-minor from a colorful set); candidates are unrelated 'strong X' rows, needs rooted-minor and colorful-set primitives.

rows.statement: A graph $G$ is strongly normal if and only if neither $G$ nor its complement contain a $C_5$ or a $C_7$ as an induced subgraph.
rows.reason: Named third-party induced-subgraph characterization of strongly normal graphs; no candidate matches, requires the normality/graph-entropy primitive.

rows.statement: Every oriented tree of order $n>1$ is $(2n-2)$-unavoidable.
rows.reason: Famous named conjecture (every oriented n-tree is (2n-2)-unavoidable); same-paper candidates are k-extension/blow-up variants, not the core statement.

rows.canonical_name: Sylvester's Question on twin-width of sparse random graphs
rows.statement: Is $\mathrm{tww}(G) = \Theta(\sqrt{e(G)})$ with high probability for sparse Erd\H{o}s-R\'enyi random graphs and random cubic graphs?
rows.reason: Named third-party question on twin-width; precise but probabilistic (whp, Θ over random graph ensembles), outside the deterministic formalization scope; no candidate matches. [verify downgraded: An open Question about Θ(√e(G)) twin-width with high probability over Erdős–Rényi and random cubic graphs; probabilistic/asymptotic and not a clean standalone formalizable statement.]

rows.canonical_name: Tang–Liu–Wang Conjecture (positive p-energy minimized by the path)
rows.statement: For every connected graph $G$ of order $n$ and $p\geq 2$, $$\mathcal{E}^{+}_{p}(G)\geq\mathcal{E}^{+}_{p}(P_{n}).$$
rows.reason: Paper-local Conjecture 1 of 2503.16882 (its companion Conjecture 2 is the E⁻_p candidate), niche spectral-energy quantity; value is as the studying paper's edge endpoint.

rows.statement: A graph with no $C_5$, $C_7$ and $\bar{C_7}$ as induced subgraph is normal.
rows.reason: Named third-party conjecture (no C5, C7, C7-bar induced implies normal); no candidate rows, requires the normality primitive.

rows.statement: Every digraph $G$ with no source has a 2-kernel $K$ with $|K| \leq |G|/2$.
rows.attributed_to: P. L. Erdős and L. A. Székely
rows.reason: Famous named conjecture (source-free digraph has a quasi-kernel of size ≤ n/2); candidate rows are unrelated, statement is self-contained and finite.

rows.canonical_name: Thomas's Conjecture (additive approximation of chromatic number on minor-closed classes)
rows.statement: There exists $\alpha \geq 0$ with the following property: for every proper minor-closed class $\mathcal{G}$, there exists a polynomial-time algorithm taking as an input a graph $G \in \mathcal{G}$ and returning an integer $c$ such that $\chi(G) \leq c \leq \chi(G) + \alpha$.
rows.reason: Named third-party conjecture; same-paper candidate is the triangle-free variant, distinct; poly-time additive approximation needs a computation model.

rows.canonical_name: Thomason's Conjecture on Book Ramsey Numbers
rows.statement: For all $n$ and $k$, $r(B^{(k)}_n) \leq 2^k(n + k - 2) + 2$.
rows.reason: Named third-party conjecture giving a concrete Ramsey-number bound for book graphs; candidates (book thickness, off-diagonal Ramsey) do not state it.

rows.canonical_name: Thomassen's Conjecture (exponentially many 3-colorings of triangle-free planar graphs)
rows.statement: There exists a positive real number $\beta$ such that every triangle-free planar graph $G$ has at least $2^{\beta|V(G)|}$ different 3-colorings.
rows.reason: Named third-party conjecture (∃β, ≥2^{β|V|} proper 3-colorings); candidates unrelated, self-contained planar coloring-count statement.

rows.canonical_name: Thomassen's Conjecture (disjoint dipaths in highly connected digraphs)
rows.statement: There exists $f_1(k)$ such that if $\kappa(D) \geq f_1(k)$, and $x_1, \ldots, x_k$ and $y_1, \ldots, y_k$ are distinct vertices of $D$, then $D$ contains $k$ disjoint dipaths $P_1, \ldots, P_k$ such that $P_i$ goes from $x_i$ to $y_i$ for all $i \in [k]$.
rows.reason: rep_statement is the digraph k-linkage/disjoint-dipaths conjecture; candidate 2108.12669#00 is a coloring-counting conjecture (cluster name-collision), so no alias — promote on the linkage statement.

rows.canonical_name: Thomassen's Question (exponentially many 3-colorings of triangle-free planar graphs)
rows.statement: Does every planar triangle-free graph have exponentially many 3-colorings?
rows.reason: Same proposition as the conjecture promoted at cid 521 (question form); cannot alias (no corpus row), value is as the studying paper's edge endpoint.

rows.statement: For fixed integers $k$ and $q$, can the $k$-colorability of graphs of girth $q$ embedded in a fixed surface be tested in polynomial time?
rows.reason: A research program (a family of questions over fixed k and girth q on surfaces), meta rather than a single standalone statement; specific instances are already tracked as arxiv rows.

rows.statement: For every $k \in \mathbb{N}$ there exists an oriented graph $D$ with $\operatorname{inv}(D) = 2\tau(D) = 2k$.
rows.reason: Named third-party tightness conjecture about the digraph inversion number with a descriptive title; requires the inv(D)/τ(D) primitives, candidates unrelated.

rows.canonical_name: Tiwary's Problem on convex independence in Minkowski sums
rows.statement: Does there exist a matching lower bound for $\mathrm{ci}(P + Q) = O\bigl((n+m)\log(n+m)\bigr)$ when $P$ and $Q$ are a convex $n$-gon and a convex $m$-gon, respectively?
rows.reason: Computational-geometry problem (matching lower bound for convex independence of polygon Minkowski sums); no graph-theory repo applies, out of the campaign domain.

rows.statement: Let $G$ be a connected $k$-chromatic graph with $k \geq 4$. Then $G$ has at most $k!(k-1)^{|G|-k}$ $k$-colourings. Moreover, the extremal graphs are precisely the graphs obtained from $K_k$ by adding trees rooted at each vertex of the clique.
rows.reason: Named third-party conjecture bounding proper k-colorings of connected k-chromatic graphs with an extremal characterization; candidates unrelated, statement self-contained. [verify downgraded: Same Tomescu coloring conjecture promoted at cid 529; internal-numbering title (Conjecture 1.1), so its value here is only as this paper's studies-edge endpoint, not a second standalone target.]

rows.canonical_name: Tomescu's conjecture (maximum number of proper k-colourings)
rows.statement: Let $G$ be a connected $k$-chromatic graph with $k \geq 4$. Then $G$ has at most $k!(k-1)^{|G|-k}$ $k$-colourings. Moreover, the extremal graphs are precisely the graphs obtained from $K_k$ by adding trees rooted at each vertex of the clique.
rows.reason: Named Tomescu 1971 conjecture bounding P_G(k) of a connected k-chromatic graph by k!(k-1)^{n-k}; no candidate matches (erdos:832/545 and arxiv:1905.05312 are unrelated); same conjecture as cid 529/530 so shares canonical_name for downstream dedup. [verify downgraded: Duplicate of the Tomescu coloring conjecture promoted at cid 529; internal-numbering title (Conjecture 1.1), retained only as this paper's studies-edge endpoint.]

rows.canonical_name: Tomescu's conjecture (maximum number of proper k-colourings)
rows.statement: For every connected graph $G$ on $n$ vertices with chromatic number $k \geq 4$, $$P_G(k) \leq k!(k-1)^{n-k},$$ with equality if and only if the 2-core of $G$ is a $k$-clique (i.e., $G$ is formed by attaching a tree to each vertex of the complete graph $K_k$).
rows.reason: Identical Tomescu conjecture (2-core = K_k extremal case); candidates erdos:832/545, opg:partial_list_coloring, arxiv:1905.05312 are all mismatches; same canonical_name as cid 528/530 for name-based dedup.

rows.canonical_name: Tomescu's conjecture (maximum number of proper k-colourings)
rows.statement: If $G$ is a connected graph on $n$ vertices and $\chi(G) = k \geq 4$, then $P_G(k) \leq k!(k-1)^{n-k}$, with equality if and only if the $2$-core of $G$ is a $k$-clique.
rows.reason: Identical Tomescu conjecture; all opg candidates (fractional powers, partial list, 3-edge-colouring) are unrelated; same canonical_name as cid 528/529 for dedup. [verify downgraded: Duplicate of the Tomescu coloring conjecture promoted at cid 529; retained only as this paper's studies-edge endpoint to avoid a duplicate standalone target.]

rows.statement: Determine the minimum number of edges in an $n$-vertex 3-graph that guarantees a homeomorphic copy of a triangulation of the torus.
rows.reason: Open-ended 'determine the minimum number of edges forcing a torus-triangulation homeomorph in a 3-graph' — a Turán-type value question with no definite claimed bound, so its value is as the studying paper's edge endpoint, not a standalone provable statement.

rows.statement: Every simple graph $G$ satisfies $\chi''(G) \leq \Delta(G) + 2$.
rows.reason: chi''(G) <= Delta(G)+2 is exactly opg:behzads_conjecture (verified source_text = Behzad's total colouring conjecture); matcher missed it here but listed it for the identical cluster 533.

rows.statement: $\chi_T(G) \leq \Delta(G) + 2$ for any graph $G$.
rows.reason: chi_T(G) <= Delta(G)+2 is the Total Colouring Conjecture, matching opg:behzads_conjecture (listed candidate, verified).

rows.statement: For every constant $\Delta \in \mathbb{Z}_{\geq 1}$, integer programs of the form $\max\{p^{\intercal}x : Mx \leq b,\, x \in \mathbb{Z}^{n}\}$, where $M$ is an integer matrix whose subdeterminants are all in $\{-\Delta, \dots, \Delta\}$, can be solved in polynomial time.
rows.reason: Poly-time solvability of IPs whose subdeterminants lie in {-Delta,...,Delta} is the bounded-subdeterminant IP conjecture already tracked as arxiv:1908.06300#00 (verified statement match).

rows.statement: Every tournament has the Erdős-Hajnal property.
rows.reason: 'Every tournament has the Erdős–Hajnal property' is the tournament form of the Erdős–Hajnal conjecture, provably equivalent to the graph version (opg:the_erdos_hajnal_conjecture); plan lists EH as a canonical alias case.

rows.statement: Is $\chi(G)$ bounded for every triangle-free sun-free graph $G$?
rows.reason: Paper-local Problems 1.1/1.4 (Trotignon) asking whether triangle-free sun-free graphs are chi-bounded — open 'is chi bounded?' questions from paper 2506.10227 whose own conjecture is already tracked at arxiv:2506.10227#00; value as study endpoint.

rows.statement: If $G$ is $\{ISK4, \text{triangle}\}$-free, then $\chi(G) \leq 3$.
rows.reason: Named Trotignon–Vušković conjecture that {ISK4, triangle}-free graphs are 3-colourable; candidates (erdos:151 clique-transversal, arxiv:2501.00567 Reed-type, arxiv:2202.05557 paths) are unrelated; needs induced-subdivision-of-K4 primitive.

rows.statement: Every 2-connected graph without a Petersen graph minor admits a nowhere-zero 4-flow.
rows.reason: 'Bridgeless (here 2-connected) graph with no Petersen minor has a nowhere-zero 4-flow' is Tutte's 4-flow conjecture, matching opg:4_flow_conjecture (verified).

rows.statement: Every 2-edge-connected cubic graph that does not topologically contain the Petersen graph is 3-edge-colorable.
rows.reason: Tutte's conjecture that 2-edge-connected cubic graphs with no Petersen topological minor are 3-edge-colourable; candidate opg:3_edge_coloring_conjecture is a different statement (edge-removal preserving 3-edge-colourability), so no match; needs topological-minor primitive. [verify downgraded: The name-matched opg:3_edge_coloring_conjecture is a different (edge-reduction) statement; this cubic no-Petersen-topological-minor 3-edge-colourability conjecture is the classically-equivalent cubic form of Tutte's already-tracked 4-flow conjecture.]

rows.statement: Every 4-edge-connected graph admits a nowhere-zero 3-flow.
rows.reason: 'Every 4-edge-connected graph has a nowhere-zero 3-flow' matches opg:3_flow_conjecture exactly (verified source_text).

Compound bundle (paper-local Conjecture 1.2) of the 3-flow, 4-flow and 5-flow conjectures, each already tracked individually; not a single new proposition, so its value is as the studying papers' edge endpoint.

rows.canonical_name: Tutte's Question on Same-Parity Components of the 4-Colouring Complex
rows.statement: Does there exist a triangulation of the plane whose 4-coloring complex has more than one component of the same parity?
rows.reason: Tutte's existence question relies on the paper-specific '4-colouring complex' machinery (and the paper's related Conjecture 8, arxiv:1912.07205#00, is a distinct statement), so it is too paper-dependent to stand alone and serves as a study endpoint.

rows.statement: For any graph $G$ it holds $\tau(G)\leq 2\mu(G)$.
rows.reason: Famous named Tuza conjecture tau(G) <= 2 mu(G) (triangle edge-cover vs triangle packing); no candidates; both parameters are finite optimisations giving a clean inequality. [verify downgraded: τ(G) ≤ 2μ(G) is verbatim the opg triangle-packing-vs-edge-transversal statement (≤2k edges destroy all triangles when there are ≤k edge-disjoint triangles), so it aliases despite the empty precomputed candidate list.]

rows.statement: Every oriented planar digraph $D$ is $2$-colourable.
rows.reason: 'Every oriented planar digraph is 2-colourable' (dichromatic number <= 2) is exactly opg:partitioning_planar_digraphs = partition of V into two acyclic sets (verified match).

rows.statement: $\operatorname{inv}(n) \leq \left\lfloor \dfrac{n-1}{2} \right\rfloor$ for all $n$.
rows.reason: Named conjecture inv(n) <= floor((n-1)/2) for the digraph inversion number; candidates (arxiv:2105.15195/2010.05992 focal families, derived rows) are unrelated; needs the inversion operation as a primitive.

rows.statement: For every simple graph $G$, for any integer $k > \chi'(G)$, for any $k$-edge-colouring $\alpha$, is there a $\chi'(G)$-edge-colouring that can be reached from $\alpha$ through a series of Kempe changes?
rows.reason: Vizing's question that any k-edge-colouring (k > chi') can reach a chi'-edge-colouring via Kempe changes; candidates (opg:a_generalization_of_vizings_theorem is a hypergraph statement, others unrelated) do not match; needs a Kempe-change reachability primitive.

rows.statement: $\mathrm{ch}'(G) \leq \Delta(G) + 1$ for any graph $G$.
rows.reason: Vizing's list edge chromatic conjecture (ch'(G) <= Delta+1) is the list edge colouring conjecture tracked as opg:edge_list_coloring_conjecture (ch' = chi'), the standard named target; matcher missed it here but listed it for the identical cluster 548.

rows.statement: Every simple graph $G$ satisfies $\chi'_\ell(G) \leq \Delta(G) + 1$.
rows.reason: chi'_ell(G) <= Delta+1 is Vizing's list edge colouring conjecture, aliased to opg:edge_list_coloring_conjecture (listed candidate).

rows.statement: Does there exist an absolute constant $c > 0$ such that every connected graph $G$ satisfies $\mathrm{lpt}(G) \leq c$?
rows.reason: Named Walther–Zamfirescu question whether an absolute constant c bounds lpt(G) for all connected graphs — a definite exists-constant proposition; candidates (erdos:833 hypergraph, arxiv:2209.09107 matching AT-number, arxiv:1801.06824 (CC)) do not match; needs a longest-path-transversal primitive.

rows.statement: For all integers $k,c\geq 1$ there exist $m,\ell>0$ with the following property. Let $G$ be a graph and let $S,T\subseteq V(G)$. Then either there are $k$ paths between $S,T$, pairwise at distance at least $c$; or there is a set $X\subseteq V(G)$ with $|X|\leq m$ such that every path between $S,T$ contains a vertex with distance at most $\ell$ from some member of $X$.
rows.reason: General Weak Coarse Menger conjecture (all k,c); candidates are only restricted/special cases (arxiv:2509.07174 surfaces, arxiv:2508.14332#00 c=2 open case, arxiv:2309.07905 bounded-degree), so the general statement is untracked; needs path-distance primitives.

rows.statement: There is an $\varepsilon > 0$ such that every graph $G$ of order $n$ has $c(G) = O(n^{1-\varepsilon})$.
rows.reason: Famous conjecture that the cop number satisfies c(G) = O(n^{1-eps}) for some eps>0; all candidates (Jaeger flow variant, weak pentagon, derived FAS row, directed tree-width) are unrelated; sublinear asymptotic growth claim.

rows.canonical_name: Weak ε-Net Question for Axis-Parallel Rectangles
rows.statement: Do there exist weak $\varepsilon$-nets of size $O\!\left(\frac{1}{\varepsilon}\right)$ for families of axis-parallel rectangles in $\mathbb{R}^2$?
rows.attributed_to: Aronov, Ezra, and Sharir; Pach and Tardos
rows.reason: Named discrete-geometry open question (Aronov–Ezra–Sharir; Pach–Tardos) on linear-size weak ε-nets; candidate arxiv:2003.07061 is the different ε-t-net problem, so no alias; geometry is out of scope for the graph-theory corpus. [verify downgraded: Named discrete-geometry question (weak O(1/ε) ε-nets for axis-parallel rectangles) that is precise but out of scope for the graph-theory formalization; the ε-t-net candidate is a different problem.]

rows.statement: For every finite family $\mathcal{F}$ of axis-parallel rectangles in $\mathbb{R}^2$, $\tau(\mathcal{F}) \leq 2\nu(\mathcal{F})$.
rows.reason: Crisp named conjecture (Wegner) that transversal ≤ twice matching for planar axis-parallel rectangle families, no candidate rows; a purely geometric piercing statement, out of scope. [verify downgraded: Named geometric piercing-vs-matching conjecture τ(F)≤2ν(F) for axis-parallel rectangles; precise but out of scope for the graph-theory corpus (geometric primitives), with no candidates.]

rows.canonical_name: Suboptimality of the 𝔽₂ⁿ Triangle-Removal Exponent C₂
rows.statement: The exponent $C_2 \approx 13.239$ appearing in the lower bound $\delta \leq \epsilon^{C_2 - o(1)}$ for the arithmetic triangle removal lemma over $\mathbb{F}_2^n$ is not optimal; the true exponent may be strictly smaller, or the correct bound may even be superpolynomial in $1/\epsilon$.
rows.reason: Vague/meta, community-unattributed observation (exponent 'may be smaller, or the bound may be superpolynomial'); candidate arxiv:1811.12650 is a different frozen-colouring exponent, so it is a fragmentary studies-backlog item.

rows.statement: Deleting an edge from a graph can change the Ramsey number by at most a constant factor.
rows.reason: Named crisp conjecture that deleting one edge changes the Ramsey number by at most a constant factor; opg edge-colouring/packing candidates are unrelated, and Ramsey numbers sit in extremal-graph-theory.

rows.canonical_name: Wood's Open Problem 18 (Clustered Choosability of Bounded-Degree Graphs on Surfaces)
rows.statement: Consider a surface $\Sigma$ and a positive integer $\Delta$. Is it true that the class of graphs of maximum degree $\Delta$ than can be drawn on $\Sigma$ has clustered choosability at most three?
rows.reason: Named survey open problem (Wood, OP 18) studied by arXiv:2111.07147 on weak-diameter/clustered coloring on surfaces; no candidate matches, and surface embeddings put it under chromatic-theory/needs-planarity.

rows.canonical_name: Wood's Conjecture on the Maximum Number of Cliques in Kₜ-Minor-Free Graphs
rows.statement: The maximum number of cliques a $K_t$-minor free graph on $n$ vertices can have is $2^{t-2}(n-t+3)$ if and only if $t \leq 49$.
rows.reason: Named exact extremal conjecture (max cliques = 2^{t-2}(n-t+3) iff t ≤ 49); distinct from the weaker asymptotic opg 'cliques in minor-closed classes' question, so promote under minor-theory.

rows.canonical_name: Wood's Conjecture on Pathwidth and Binary-Tree Subdivisions
rows.statement: Every graph with treewidth $t$ and not containing a subdivision of a complete binary tree of height $h$ has pathwidth at most $f(t) \cdot h$ for some function $f$.
rows.reason: Named width-parameter conjecture (bounded treewidth + no height-h binary-tree subdivision ⇒ pathwidth ≤ f(t)·h); no candidate matches and treewidth/pathwidth/subdivision are minor-theory primitives.

rows.canonical_name: Wood–Telle Conjecture (Σ deg² Crossing-Number Bound for Minor-Free Graphs)
rows.statement: For every graph $H$, there is a constant $c = c(H)$ such that every $H$-minor-free graph $G$ has crossing number $\mathrm{cr}(G) \leq c \sum_{v \in V(G)} \deg(v)^2$.
rows.reason: Named crisp conjecture bounding cr(G) by c·Σdeg² for H-minor-free graphs; opg 'notions of crossing number' and the others do not match, and crossing number is topological-graph-theory/needs-planarity.

rows.canonical_name: Wood–Telle Question (Linear c·Δ·n Crossing-Number Bound for Minor-Free Graphs)
rows.statement: Is it true that for every graph $H$ there is a constant $c = c(H)$ such that every $H$-minor-free graph $G$ has crossing number $\mathrm{cr}(G) \leq c \cdot \Delta(G) \cdot |G|$?
rows.reason: Distinct named Wood–Telle open question (cr(G) ≤ c·Δ(G)·|G|) from the same paper; erdos:619 is a triangle-free diameter problem and other candidates are unrelated, so promote as a separate crossing-number target.

rows.canonical_name: Woodall's List-Colouring Conjecture for Kₛ,ₜ-Minor-Free Graphs
rows.statement: For every $s, t \in \mathbb{N}$, if $G$ is a graph with $G \not\succeq K_{s,t}$, then $\chi_{\ell}(G) \leq s + t - 1$.
rows.reason: List-Hadwiger-type conjecture (Kₛ,ₜ-minor-free ⇒ χ_ℓ ≤ s+t−1); opg:woodalls_conjecture is the unrelated dijoin conjecture and arxiv:2201.09115#01 is the paper's weaker 2s+t question, so promote under the existing list-hadwiger topic.

rows.canonical_name: Yang's Conjecture (Two-Simultaneous-Cops Cop Number of Planar Graphs)
rows.statement: For any planar graph $G$, $c_2(G) \leq 3$.
rows.reason: Named pursuit-game conjecture proved by arXiv:2406.01068 (c₂ = cop number when ≤2 cops move per round; c₂ ≤ 3 for planar graphs); no candidate matches, filed with the cops-and-robbers convention.

rows.statement: The quantum query complexity of sorting under partial information, given any poset $P$ on $n$ elements, is at least $c \log(|\Delta(P)|)$ for some universal constant $c > 0$.
rows.reason: Named quantum query-complexity conjecture (studied by arXiv:1902.06473) on sorting posets; candidates are unrelated colouring/matching statements and quantum complexity is out of scope. [verify downgraded: Named Yao conjecture on the quantum query complexity of sorting under partial information; a precise but quantum-complexity statement out of scope for the graph-theory formalization.]

rows.canonical_name: Yao–Zhou Conjecture on the Edge Density of 4-Critical Planar Graphs
rows.statement: $L := \limsup_{G\,4\text{-critical planar}} |E(G)|/|V(G)| = 7/3$.
rows.reason: Named conjecture that limsup |E|/|V| = 7/3 over 4-critical planar graphs; opg edge-critical/list candidates and the 12-critical earth-moon derived row are different statements, so promote as a planar colour-critical density target.

rows.canonical_name: You–Liu / Zhai–Shu–Hong Conjecture on Algebraic Connectivity of a Graph and its Complement
rows.statement: For any graph $G$ of order $n \geq 2$, the following holds: $$\lambda_n(G) - \lambda_2(G) \leq n - 1,$$ or equivalently $\lambda_2(G) + \lambda_2(\bar{G}) \geq 1$, with equality if and only if $G$ or $\bar{G}$ is isomorphic to the join of an isolated vertex and a disconnected graph of order $n - 1$.
rows.attributed_to: You and Liu; Zhai, Shu, and Hong
rows.reason: Named Laplacian-eigenvalue conjecture (λ₂(G)+λ₂(Ḡ) ≥ 1, with equality characterization) from arXiv:1806.06770; candidates (digraph colouring, coarse Menger, group flows) are unrelated, so promote under spectral-graph-theory.

rows.statement: If $G$ is a connected graph of order at least three different from the cycle $C_5$, then $\chi'_a(G) \leq \Delta(G) + 2$.
rows.reason: Zhang–Liu–Wang's χ'_a(G) ≤ Δ+2 for connected graphs (C₅ excepted, a redundant caveat since a'(C₅)=3) is exactly the acyclic edge-colouring conjecture already tracked at opg:acyclic_edge_coloring.

rows.canonical_name: Ziegler–Drewnowski–Łuczak–Alon Conjecture on the Chromatic Number of r-Stable Kneser Hypergraphs
rows.statement: The chromatic number of $KG^r(k, n)_{r-\mathrm{stab}}$ is $\left\lceil \dfrac{n - r(k-1)}{r-1} \right\rceil$, the same as the chromatic number of the full Kneser hypergraph $KG^r(k, n)$.
rows.reason: Named conjecture that r-stable Kneser hypergraphs have the same chromatic number ⌈(n−r(k−1))/(r−1)⌉ as the full Kneser hypergraph; candidates (Reed list-analogue, hypergraph minor colouring, matchings) do not match, so promote under hypergraph-theory.
